=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (set+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 5, row 6, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 5, box 6, box 7}.
      Remainder: {r6c3, r9c8} = {r4c9, r8c3}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  SET: {row 5, row 8, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 5, box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r5c8, r8c6} = {r6c6, r7c9}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Trivalue oddagon:
      row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}
        Рєњ via cols {7} [even]
      box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c8}
        Рєњ via cols {7, 8, 9} [odd]
      box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}
        Рєњ via cols {7} [even]
      row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c7}
        Рєњ via cols {3, 6, 7} [even]
      Cycle parity: odd. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r5c5, r5c6, r6c6} (spine r5c5Рђћr5c6).
      Рєњ color(r5c8) = color(r6c6). Identify.

      Рєњ color(r4c9) = color(r6c3). Identify.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (set+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r8c6} = {r7c9}.
      Рєњ color(r8c6) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c3, r6c6, [r7c9=r8c6], r8c3}:
      Diagonals: {r6c3, [r7c9=r8c6]} and {r6c6, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r6c3) = color([r7c9=r8c6]) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c3=X Рєњ r6c6, r6c7РЅаX
        row 8 {[r7c9=r8c6], r8c3, r8c7}: [r7c9=r8c6]=X Рєњ r8c3, r8c7РЅаX
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, [r7c9=r8c6]}: [r7c9=r8c6]=X Рєњ r5c6РЅаX
        box 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r6c6}: r5c6, r6c6РЅаX Рєњ r5c5=X
        box 9 {[r7c9=r8c6], r8c7, r9c8}: [r7c9=r8c6]=X Рєњ r9c8РЅаX
        row 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c5=X Рєњ r5c8РЅаX
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8, r6c7РЅаX Рєњ r4c9=X
        r4c9=X and [r7c9=r8c6]=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c6) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c6=X Рєњ r6c3, r6c7РЅаX
        row 8 {[r7c9=r8c6], r8c3, r8c7}: r8c3=X Рєњ [r7c9=r8c6], r8c7РЅаX
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, [r7c9=r8c6]}: r6c6=X Рєњ r5c6РЅаX
        box 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r6c6}: r6c6=X Рєњ r5c5РЅаX
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r8c3=X Рєњ r9c1, r9c2РЅаX
        box 9 {[r7c9=r8c6], r8c7, r9c8}: [r7c9=r8c6], r8c7РЅаX Рєњ r9c8=X
        row 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c5, r5c6РЅаX Рєњ r5c8=X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8=X Рєњ r4c9РЅаX
        r5c8=X and r9c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (set+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 5, row 6, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 5, box 6, box 7}.
      Remainder: {r6c3, r9c8} = {r4c9, r8c3}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  SET: {row 5, row 8, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 5, box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r5c8, r8c6} = {r6c6, r7c9}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Trivalue oddagon:
      row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}
        Рєњ via cols {7} [even]
      box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c8}
        Рєњ via cols {7, 8, 9} [odd]
      box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}
        Рєњ via cols {7} [even]
      row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c7}
        Рєњ via cols {3, 6, 7} [even]
      Cycle parity: odd. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_set] (diamond+oddwheel+guardian) ===

  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c7=r7c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r4c9=r6c3=r9c4], r5c8, r7c8, r8c7, r8c5} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+set+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 5, row 6}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 5, box 6}.
      Remainder: {r6c3} = {r4c9}.
      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r4c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3], r9c3, r9c9, r9c4} (spine r9c3Рђћr9c9).
      Рєњ color([r4c9=r6c3]) = color(r9c4). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c6, [r4c9=r6c3=r9c4], r7c6, r8c5} (spine [r4c9=r6c3=r9c4]Рђћr7c6).
      Рєњ color(r6c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c8, r6c7, r8c7, r7c8}:
      Diagonals: {r5c8, r8c7} and {r6c7, r7c8} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c8) = color(r8c7) = X
        row 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c8=X Рєњ r5c5, r5c6РЅаX
        box 5 {r5c5, r5c6, [r6c6=r8c5]}: r5c5, r5c6РЅаX Рєњ [r6c6=r8c5]=X
        box 6 {[r4c9=r6c3=r9c4], r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8=X Рєњ [r4c9=r6c3=r9c4], r6c7РЅаX
        box 8 {[r4c9=r6c3=r9c4], [r6c6=r8c5], r7c6}: [r6c6=r8c5]=X Рєњ r7c6РЅаX
        box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r8c7=X Рєњ r7c8, r9c9РЅаX
        [r6c6=r8c5]=X and r8c7=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r7c8) = X
        row 6 {[r4c9=r6c3=r9c4], [r6c6=r8c5], r6c7}: r6c7=X Рєњ [r4c9=r6c3=r9c4], [r6c6=r8c5]РЅаX
        box 6 {[r4c9=r6c3=r9c4], r5c8, r6c7}: r6c7=X Рєњ r5c8РЅаX
        box 8 {[r4c9=r6c3=r9c4], [r6c6=r8c5], r7c6}: [r4c9=r6c3=r9c4], [r6c6=r8c5]РЅаX Рєњ r7c6=X
        box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c8=X Рєњ r8c7, r9c9РЅаX
        r7c6=X and r7c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_diamond] (ladder+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Guardian: r7c8 guards oddagon {r7c8, r5c8, r5c5, r6c6, r7c6} (source r5c6).
      Рєњ color(r7c8) = color(r5c6). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {[r5c6=r7c8]Рђћr7c6, r8c7Рђћr8c5, r9c9Рђћr9c4}:
      Satellites r6c6 (rung 1), r9c3 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Circular ladder {[r5c6=r7c8]Рђћr9c9, r6c6Рђћr9c3, r7c6Рђћr9c4}:
      Satellites r8c7 (rung 1), r6c3 (rung 2), r8c5 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  4.  Guardian: r5c5 guards oddagon {r5c5, r6c6, r6c7, r8c7, r8c5} (source r6c3).
      Рєњ color(r5c5) = color(r6c3). Identify.

      Diagonals: {r4c9, r8c7} and {r6c7, r9c9} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r4c9) = color(r8c7) = X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r4c9=X Рєњ r5c8, r6c7РЅаX
        box 9 {[r5c6=r7c8], r8c7, r9c9}: r8c7=X Рєњ [r5c6=r7c8], r9c9РЅаX
        row 5 {[r5c5=r6c3], [r5c6=r7c8], r5c8}: [r5c6=r7c8], r5c8РЅаX Рєњ [r5c5=r6c3]=X
        row 6 {[r5c5=r6c3], r6c6, r6c7}: [r5c5=r6c3]=X Рєњ r6c6РЅаX
        col 6 {[r5c6=r7c8], r6c6, r7c6}: [r5c6=r7c8], r6c6РЅаX Рєњ r7c6=X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r7c6=X Рєњ r8c5, r9c4РЅаX
        [r5c5=r6c3]=X and r8c7=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r9c9) = X

        row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c9=X Рєњ r9c3, r9c4РЅаX
        box 5 {[r5c5=r6c3], [r5c6=r7c8], r6c6}: [r5c5=r6c3], r6c6РЅаX Рєњ [r5c6=r7c8]=X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r6c7=X Рєњ r4c9, r5c8РЅаX
        box 9 {[r5c6=r7c8], r8c7, r9c9}: [r5c6=r7c8]=X and r9c9=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_diamond] (ladder+xwing+guardian) ===

  1.  Guardian: r7c8 guards oddagon {r7c8, r5c8, r5c5, r6c6, r7c6} (source r5c6).

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (set+ladder+oddwheel+guardian) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 5, row 6}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 5, box 6}.
      Remainder: {r6c3} = {r4c9}.
      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r4c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r7c3Рђћr7c9, r8c2Рђћr8c7, r9c1Рђћr9c8}:
      Satellites [r4c9=r6c3] (rung 1), r9c6 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Guardian: r9c8 guards oddagon {r9c8, r5c8, r6c7, r6c6, r9c6} (source [r4c9=r6c3]).
      Рєњ color(r9c8) = color([r4c9=r6c3]). Identify.

      Bivalue oddagon {r6c6, r6c7, r8c7, r7c9, r7c3, r9c1, r9c6} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+ladder+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r5c5, r5c6, r6c4} (spine r5c5Рђћr5c6).
      Рєњ color(r5c8) = color(r6c4). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r5c8=r6c4], r9c4, r9c8, r9c3} (spine r9c4Рђћr9c8).

  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c3, r7c7, r8c9, r8c3}:
      Diagonals: {r6c3, r8c9} and {r7c7, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r6c3) = color(r8c9) = X
        row 6 {[r5c8=r6c4=r9c3], r6c3, r6c7}: r6c3=X Рєњ [r5c8=r6c4=r9c3], r6c7РЅаX

        box 6 {r4c9, [r5c8=r6c4=r9c3], r6c7}: [r5c8=r6c4=r9c3], r6c7РЅаX Рєњ r4c9=X
        box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c8}: r8c9=X Рєњ r7c7, r9c8РЅаX
        r4c9=X and r8c9=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r7c7) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c9}: r8c3=X Рєњ r8c6, r8c9РЅаX
        col 3 {[r5c8=r6c4=r9c3], r6c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X Рєњ [r5c8=r6c4=r9c3], r6c3РЅаX
        box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c8}: r7c7=X Рєњ r9c8РЅаX
        row 6 {[r5c8=r6c4=r9c3], r6c3, r6c7}: [r5c8=r6c4=r9c3], r6c3РЅаX Рєњ r6c7=X

        r6c7=X and r7c7=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

      Each pair shares a column Рєњ distinct permutations.

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_diamond] (set+xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 5, row 6}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 5, box 6}.
      Remainder: {r6c3} = {r4c9}.
      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r4c9). Identify.
  2.  Branch on [r4c9=r6c3], r9c5:
      Case A: color([r4c9=r6c3]) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        3.  Guardian: r5c6 guards oddagon {r5c6, r5c8, r5c5, r8c5, r7c6} (source [r4c9=r6c3=r9c5]).
            Рєњ color(r5c6) = color([r4c9=r6c3=r9c5]). Identify.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c8, r6c7, r8c7, r7c8}:
            Diagonals: {r5c8, r8c7} and {r6c7, r7c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c8) = color(r8c7) = X
              row 5 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c3=r9c5], r5c5, r5c8}: r5c8=X Рєњ [r4c9=r5c6=r6c3=r9c5], r5c5РЅаX
              col 5 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c3=r9c5], r5c5, r8c5}: [r4c9=r5c6=r6c3=r9c5], r5c5РЅаX Рєњ r8c5=X
              box 5 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c3=r9c5], r5c5, r6c4}: [r4c9=r5c6=r6c3=r9c5], r5c5РЅаX Рєњ r6c4=X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c3=r9c5], r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8=X Рєњ r6c7РЅаX
              box 8 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c3=r9c5], r7c6, r8c5}: r8c5=X Рєњ r7c6РЅаX
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r8c7=X Рєњ r7c8, r9c9РЅаX
              r8c5=X and r8c7=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r7c8) = X
              row 6 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c3=r9c5], r6c4, r6c7}: r6c7=X Рєњ [r4c9=r5c6=r6c3=r9c5], r6c4РЅаX
              box 5 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c3=r9c5], r5c5, r6c4}: [r4c9=r5c6=r6c3=r9c5], r6c4РЅаX Рєњ r5c5=X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c3=r9c5], r5c8, r6c7}: r6c7=X Рєњ r5c8РЅаX
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c8=X Рєњ r8c7, r9c9РЅаX
              row 9 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c3=r9c5], r9c3, r9c9}: [r4c9=r5c6=r6c3=r9c5], r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r9c3=X
              col 5 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c3=r9c5], r5c5, r8c5}: r5c5=X Рєњ r8c5РЅаX
              box 8 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c3=r9c5], r7c6, r8c5}: [r4c9=r5c6=r6c3=r9c5], r8c5РЅаX Рєњ r7c6=X
              r7c6=X and r7c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r4c9=r6c3]) РЅа color(r9c5). Add edge.
        5.  KРѓё on {[r4c9=r6c3], r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond+set+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r7c9} (spine r4c9Рђћr5c9).
      Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 5, row 6, row 8}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{col 3, col 6, col 9}.
      Remainder: {[r6c8=r7c9], r8c5, r8c8} = {r4c9, [r6c8=r7c9], r9c6}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Diamond {r5c9, r4c9, [r6c8=r7c9], r9c6} (spine r4c9Рђћ[r6c8=r7c9]).
      Рєњ color(r5c9) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r5c3, r5c6, [r5c9=r9c6], r6c6} (spine r5c6Рђћ[r5c9=r9c6]).
      Рєњ color(r5c3) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r5c6, [r5c3=r6c6], [r5c9=r9c6], [r6c8=r7c9]} (spine [r5c3=r6c6]Рђћ[r5c9=r9c6]).
      Рєњ color(r5c6) = color([r6c8=r7c9]). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r5c9=r9c6], [r5c3=r6c6], [r5c6=r6c8=r7c9], r6c3} (spine [r5c3=r6c6]Рђћ[r5c6=r6c8=r7c9]).
      Рєњ color([r5c9=r9c6]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  7.  Parity transport:
      row 5 {[r5c3=r6c6], [r5c6=r6c8=r7c9], [r5c9=r6c3=r9c6]}
      row 6 {[r5c9=r6c3=r9c6], [r5c3=r6c6], [r5c6=r6c8=r7c9]}
      row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c8}
      row 9 {r9c2, [r5c9=r6c3=r9c6], r9c7}
      Parallel links: row 5Рєћrow 6 (col, col, box); row 6Рєћrow 9 (box, col, box); row 8Рєћrow 9 (box, box, box)
      Рєњ same permutation parity.
      Each pair shares a column Рєњ distinct permutations.
      4 same-parity permutations from 3 available Рєњ pigeonhole contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

  7.  Parity transport:
      row 5 {[r5c3=r6c6], [r5c6=r6c8=r7c9], [r5c9=r6c3=r9c6]}
      row 6 {[r5c9=r6c3=r9c6], [r5c3=r6c6], [r5c6=r6c8=r7c9]}
      row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c7}
      row 9 {r9c2, [r5c9=r6c3=r9c6], r9c7}
      Parallel links: row 5Рєћrow 6 (col, col, box); row 6Рєћrow 9 (box, col, box); row 8Рєћrow 9 (box, box, col)
      Рєњ same permutation parity.
      Each pair shares a column Рєњ distinct permutations.
      4 same-parity permutations from 3 available Рєњ pigeonhole contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r7c9} (spine r4c9Рђћr5c9).
      Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r5c3Рђћr6c3, r5c6Рђћr6c5, r5c9Рђћ[r6c8=r7c9]}:
      Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Circular ladder {r8c3Рђћr9c2, r8c6Рђћr9c6, r8c8Рђћr9c7}:
      Satellites r5c6 (rung 2), [r6c8=r7c9] (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  4.  Diamond {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9, [r6c8=r7c9]} (spine r5c6Рђћr5c9).
      Рєњ color(r5c3) = color([r6c8=r7c9]). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r5c9, r4c9, [r5c3=r6c8=r7c9], r8c3} (spine r4c9Рђћ[r5c3=r6c8=r7c9]).
      Рєњ color(r5c9) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  6.  Parity transport:
      row 5 {[r5c3=r6c8=r7c9], r5c6, [r5c9=r8c3]}
      row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r5c3=r6c8=r7c9]}
      row 8 {[r5c9=r8c3], r8c6, r8c8}
      row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}
      Parallel links: row 5Рєћrow 6 (col, box, box); row 5Рєћrow 8 (col, col, box); row 8Рєћrow 9 (box, col, box)
      Рєњ same permutation parity.
      Each pair shares a column Рєњ distinct permutations.
      4 same-parity permutations from 3 available Рєњ pigeonhole contradiction.

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r7c9} (spine r4c9Рђћr5c9).
      Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r5c3Рђћr6c3, r5c6Рђћr6c5, r5c9Рђћ[r6c8=r7c9]}:
      Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Circular ladder {r8c3Рђћr9c2, r8c6Рђћr9c6, r8c7Рђћr9c7}:
      Satellites r5c6 (rung 2), [r6c8=r7c9] (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  4.  Diamond {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9, [r6c8=r7c9]} (spine r5c6Рђћr5c9).
      Рєњ color(r5c3) = color([r6c8=r7c9]). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r5c9, r4c9, [r5c3=r6c8=r7c9], r8c3} (spine r4c9Рђћ[r5c3=r6c8=r7c9]).
      Рєњ color(r5c9) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  6.  Parity transport:
      row 5 {[r5c3=r6c8=r7c9], r5c6, [r5c9=r8c3]}
      row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r5c3=r6c8=r7c9]}
      row 8 {[r5c9=r8c3], r8c6, r8c7}
      row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}
      Parallel links: row 5Рєћrow 6 (col, box, box); row 5Рєћrow 8 (col, col, box); row 8Рєћrow 9 (box, col, col)
      Рєњ same permutation parity.
      Each pair shares a column Рєњ distinct permutations.
      4 same-parity permutations from 3 available Рєњ pigeonhole contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r7c9} (spine r4c9Рђћr5c9).

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch+guardian) ===

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r7c9} (spine r4c9Рђћr5c9).
      Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r5c3, r9c7:
      Case A: color(r5c3) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        3.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c3=r9c7] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, r5c9, [r6c8=r7c9], r6c3, r8c3, r9c2, r9c6} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c3) РЅа color(r9c7). Add edge.
        4.  Circular ladder {r5c3Рђћr9c7, r6c3Рђћ[r6c8=r7c9], r8c3Рђћr8c8}:
            Satellites r6c5 (rung 2), r8c4 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        5.  Parity transport:
            row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}
            row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r7c9]}
            row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c8}
            row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}
            Parallel links: row 5Рєћrow 6 (col, box, box); row 6Рєћrow 8 (col, box, col); row 8Рєћrow 9 (box, box, box)
            Рєњ same permutation parity.
            Each pair shares a column Рєњ distinct permutations.
            4 same-parity permutations from 3 available Рєњ pigeonhole contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r7c9} (spine r4c9Рђћr5c9).
      Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r5c3, r9c8:
      Case A: color(r5c3) = color(r9c8). Identify.
        3.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c3=r9c8] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, r5c9, [r6c8=r7c9], r6c3, r8c3, r9c2, r9c6} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c3) РЅа color(r9c8). Add edge.
        4.  Circular ladder {r5c3Рђћr9c8, r6c3Рђћ[r6c8=r7c9], r8c3Рђћr8c7}:
            Satellites r6c5 (rung 2), r8c4 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        5.  Parity transport:
            row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}
            row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r7c9]}
            row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c7}
            row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c8}
            Parallel links: row 5Рєћrow 6 (col, box, box); row 6Рєћrow 8 (col, box, box); row 8Рєћrow 9 (box, box, box)
            Рєњ same permutation parity.
            Each pair shares a column Рєњ distinct permutations.
            4 same-parity permutations from 3 available Рєњ pigeonhole contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r9c9} (spine r4c9Рђћr5c9).
      Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r5c3Рђћr6c3, r5c6Рђћr6c5, r5c9Рђћ[r6c8=r9c9]}:
      Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Branch on r5c6, r8c5:
      Case A: color(r5c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r5c9, r5c3, [r5c6=r8c5], r8c3} (spine r5c3Рђћ[r5c6=r8c5]).
            Рєњ color(r5c9) = color(r8c3). Identify.

            Рєњ color([r5c6=r8c5]) = color(r6c3). Identify.

      Case B: color(r5c6) РЅа color(r8c5). Add edge.
        7.  Diamond {r6c5, r5c6, r8c5, r7c6} (spine r5c6Рђћr8c5).
            Рєњ color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        8.  Parity transport:
            row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}
            row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r7c6], [r6c8=r9c9]}
            row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c7}
            row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, [r6c8=r9c9]}
            Parallel links: row 5Рєћrow 6 (col, box, box); row 6Рєћrow 8 (col, col, box); row 8Рєћrow 9 (box, box, box)
            Рєњ same permutation parity.
            Each pair shares a column Рєњ distinct permutations.
            4 same-parity permutations from 3 available Рєњ pigeonhole contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3Рђћr6c3, r5c6Рђћr6c5, r5c9Рђћr6c8}:
      Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Branch on r6c3, r8c4:
      Case A: color(r6c3) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        3.  Guardian: r5c9 guards oddagon {r5c9, r5c3, r8c3, r8c8, r6c8} (source [r6c3=r8c4]).
            Рєњ color(r5c9) = color([r6c3=r8c4]). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c9=r6c3=r8c4] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r6c8, r6c5, r5c6, r7c6, r9c4, r9c9} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c3) РЅа color(r8c4). Add edge.
        5.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c3, r5c9, r9c9, r9c4, r8c4, r6c3}
            Bridges: r5c6Рђћr7c6 (edges r5c3Рђћr5c9 Рєћ r9c4Рђћr8c4), r4c9Рђћr8c3 (edges r5c9Рђћr9c9 Рєћ r8c4Рђћr6c3), r9c2Рђћr8c3 (edges r9c9Рђћr9c4 Рєћ r6c3Рђћr5c3).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r9c9} (spine r4c9Рђћr5c9).
      Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r5c3Рђћr6c3, r5c6Рђћr6c5, r5c9Рђћ[r6c8=r9c9]}:
      Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Branch on r6c5, r7c6:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        4.  Parity transport:
            row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}
            row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r7c6], [r6c8=r9c9]}
            row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c7}
            row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, [r6c8=r9c9]}
            Parallel links: row 5Рєћrow 6 (col, box, box); row 6Рєћrow 8 (col, box, box); row 8Рєћrow 9 (box, col, box)
            Рєњ same permutation parity.
            Each pair shares a column Рєњ distinct permutations.
            4 same-parity permutations from 3 available Рєњ pigeonhole contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) РЅа color(r7c6). Add edge.
        5.  Circular ladder {r5c3Рђћr6c3, r5c6Рђћr6c5, r5c9Рђћ[r6c8=r9c9]}:
            Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
        6.  Diamond {r4c9, r7c6, r8c3, r8c4} (spine r7c6Рђћr8c3).
            Рєњ color(r4c9) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {r5c9, [r4c9=r8c4], [r6c8=r9c9], r8c7} (spine [r4c9=r8c4]Рђћ[r6c8=r9c9]).
            Рєњ color(r5c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {[r6c8=r9c9], [r4c9=r8c4], [r5c9=r8c7], r8c3} (spine [r4c9=r8c4]Рђћ[r5c9=r8c7]).
            Рєњ color([r6c8=r9c9]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        9.  KРѓё on {[r4c9=r8c4], [r6c8=r8c3=r9c9], r7c6, r9c4}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_hexagon] (diamond+ladder+xwing+oddwheel+branch) ===

      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r7c7). Identify.

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+parity+branch) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9Рђћr5c9).

      Case B: color(r4c9) РЅа color(r8c4). Add edge.

              Рєњ via cols {9} [even]
            box 9 {[r6c8=r8c9], r7c7, r9c7}

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r9c9} (spine r4c9Рђћr5c9).

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (hexagon+xwing+branch) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c3, r9c2:

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+parity+branch) ===

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r9c9} (spine r4c9Рђћr5c9).

        4.  Diamond {r7c3, [r4c9=r8c8], r8c2, r8c6} (spine [r4c9=r8c8]Рђћr8c2).

            Рєњ color(r5c9) = color([r7c3=r8c6]). Identify.

      Case B: color(r4c9) РЅа color(r8c8). Add edge.

            Рєњ color(r5c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {r5c3, r5c6, [r5c9=r8c8], r8c6} (spine r5c6Рђћ[r5c9=r8c8]).

              Рєњ via boxes {5, 6} [even]

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

      Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r5c3Рђћr6c3, r5c6Рђћr6c5, r5c9Рђћ[r6c8=r9c9]}:
      Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Branch on r4c9, r8c8:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r7c3, [r4c9=r8c8], r8c2, r8c6} (spine [r4c9=r8c8]Рђћr8c2).
            Рєњ color(r7c3) = color(r8c6). Identify.
        5.  Diamond {r5c9, r5c3, r5c6, [r7c3=r8c6]} (spine r5c3Рђћr5c6).
            Рєњ color(r5c9) = color([r7c3=r8c6]). Identify.
        6.  KРѓё on {[r5c9=r7c3=r8c6], [r6c8=r9c9], r9c1, r9c4}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) РЅа color(r8c8). Add edge.
        7.  Diamond {r5c9, r4c9, [r6c8=r9c9], r8c8} (spine r4c9Рђћ[r6c8=r9c9]).
            Рєњ color(r5c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {r5c3, r5c6, [r5c9=r8c8], r8c6} (spine r5c6Рђћ[r5c9=r8c8]).
            Рєњ color(r5c3) = color(r8c6). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {r5c6, [r5c3=r8c6], [r5c9=r8c8], r8c2} (spine [r5c3=r8c6]Рђћ[r5c9=r8c8]).
            Рєњ color(r5c6) = color(r8c2). Identify.
        10.  Trivalue oddagon:
            row 5 {[r5c3=r8c6], [r5c6=r8c2], [r5c9=r8c8]}
              Рєњ via cols {9} [odd]
            row 9 {[r6c8=r9c9], r9c1, r9c4}
              Рєњ via boxes {7} [even]
            col 3 {[r5c3=r8c6], r6c3, r7c3}
              Рєњ via rows {5} [even]
            col 9 {r4c9, [r5c9=r8c8], [r6c8=r9c9]}
              Рєњ via rows {9} [odd]
            box 7 {[r5c6=r8c2], r7c3, r9c1}
              Рєњ via cols {3} [odd]
            row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r9c9]}
              Рєњ via boxes {5, 6} [even]
            Cycle parity: odd. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_hexagon] (diamond+xwing+oddwheel+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r9c9} (spine r4c9Рђћr5c9).
      Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r6c5, r7c3:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {[r6c5=r7c3], [r6c8=r9c9], r9c1, r9c4} (spine [r6c8=r9c9]Рђћr9c1).
            Рєњ color([r6c5=r7c3]) = color(r9c4). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c5=r7c3=r9c4] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c3, r5c6, r8c6, r8c2, r9c1, [r6c8=r9c9], r6c3} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) РЅа color(r7c3). Add edge.
        5.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r6c8=r9c9], r8c8, r8c6, r9c4}:
            Diagonals: {[r6c8=r9c9], r8c6} and {r8c8, r9c4} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r6c8=r9c9]) = color(r8c6) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r9c9]}: [r6c8=r9c9]=X Рєњ r6c3, r6c5РЅаX
              row 8 {r8c2, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c6=X Рєњ r8c2, r8c8РЅаX
              row 9 {[r6c8=r9c9], r9c1, r9c4}: [r6c8=r9c9]=X Рєњ r9c1, r9c4РЅаX
              col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, [r6c8=r9c9]}: [r6c8=r9c9]=X Рєњ r4c9, r5c9РЅаX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c1}: r8c2, r9c1РЅаX Рєњ r7c3=X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r7c3}: r7c3=X Рєњ r5c3РЅаX
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3, r5c9РЅаX Рєњ r5c6=X
              r5c6=X and r8c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c8) = color(r9c4) = X
              row 8 {r8c2, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c8=X Рєњ r8c2, r8c6РЅаX
              row 9 {[r6c8=r9c9], r9c1, r9c4}: r9c4=X Рєњ [r6c8=r9c9], r9c1РЅаX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c1}: r8c2, r9c1РЅаX Рєњ r7c3=X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r7c3}: r7c3=X Рєњ r5c3, r6c3РЅаX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r9c9]}: r6c3, [r6c8=r9c9]РЅаX Рєњ r6c5=X
              r6c5=X and r7c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+parity+branch) ===

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (ladder+xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3Рђћr6c3, r5c6Рђћr6c5, r5c9Рђћr6c8}:
      Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Branch on r7c3, r8c7:
      Case A: color(r7c3) = color(r8c7). Identify.
        3.  Guardian: r6c3 guards oddagon {r6c3, r6c8, r5c9, r9c9, [r7c3=r8c7]} (source r4c9).
            Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r4c9). Identify.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c6, r8c6, [r7c3=r8c7]}:
            Diagonals: {r5c3, r8c6} and {r5c6, [r7c3=r8c7]} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r8c6) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3=X Рєњ r5c6, r5c9РЅаX

              col 9 {[r4c9=r6c3], r5c9, r9c9}: [r4c9=r6c3], r5c9РЅаX Рєњ r9c9=X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r6c3], r5c9, r6c8}: [r4c9=r6c3], r5c9РЅаX Рєњ r6c8=X

              r9c2=X and r9c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c6) = color([r7c3=r8c7]) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6=X Рєњ r5c3, r5c9РЅаX
              row 8 {[r7c3=r8c7], r8c2, r8c6}: [r7c3=r8c7]=X Рєњ r8c2, r8c6РЅаX

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_hexagon] (diamond+xwing+oddwheel+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r9c9} (spine r4c9Рђћr5c9).
      Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r6c5, r7c3:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {[r6c5=r7c3], [r6c8=r9c9], r9c2, r9c4} (spine [r6c8=r9c9]Рђћr9c2).
            Рєњ color([r6c5=r7c3]) = color(r9c4). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c5=r7c3=r9c4] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c3, r5c6, r8c6, r8c2, r9c2, [r6c8=r9c9], r6c3} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) РЅа color(r7c3). Add edge.
        5.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r6c8=r9c9], r8c7, r8c6, r9c4}:
            Diagonals: {[r6c8=r9c9], r8c6} and {r8c7, r9c4} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r6c8=r9c9]) = color(r8c6) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r9c9]}: [r6c8=r9c9]=X Рєњ r6c3, r6c5РЅаX
              row 8 {r8c2, r8c6, r8c7}: r8c6=X Рєњ r8c2, r8c7РЅаX
              row 9 {[r6c8=r9c9], r9c2, r9c4}: [r6c8=r9c9]=X Рєњ r9c2, r9c4РЅаX
              col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, [r6c8=r9c9]}: [r6c8=r9c9]=X Рєњ r4c9, r5c9РЅаX

              r5c6=X and r8c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c7) = color(r9c4) = X

              row 9 {[r6c8=r9c9], r9c2, r9c4}: r9c4=X Рєњ [r6c8=r9c9], r9c2РЅаX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r8c2, r9c2РЅаX Рєњ r7c3=X

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r9c9} (spine r4c9Рђћr5c9).
      Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r5c3Рђћr6c3, r5c6Рђћr6c5, r5c9Рђћ[r6c8=r9c9]}:
      Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Branch on r4c9, r8c7:

            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9Рђћr5c9).

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9Рђћr5c9).
      Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r4c9, r9c7:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        3.  Circular ladder {r5c3Рђћr6c3, r5c6Рђћr6c5, r5c9Рђћ[r6c8=r8c9]}:
            Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), [r4c9=r9c7] (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: r5c3 guards oddagon {r5c3, r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9], r7c8, r7c3} (source [r4c9=r9c7]).
            Рєњ color(r5c3) = color([r4c9=r9c7]). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r4c9=r5c3=r9c7] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9], r6c3, r7c3, r9c1, r9c6} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) РЅа color(r9c7). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r5c9, r5c3, r7c3, r9c1, r9c7}
            Bridges: [r6c8=r8c9]Рђћr8c2 (edges r4c9Рђћr5c9 Рєћ r7c3Рђћr9c1), r5c6Рђћr9c6 (edges r5c9Рђћr5c3 Рєћ r9c1Рђћr9c7), r6c3Рђћ[r6c8=r8c9] (edges r5c3Рђћr7c3 Рєћ r9c7Рђћr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+branch) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9Рђћr5c9).

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9Рђћr5c9).
      Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r4c9, r9c7:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        3.  Circular ladder {r5c3Рђћr6c3, r5c6Рђћr6c5, r5c9Рђћ[r6c8=r8c9]}:
            Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), [r4c9=r9c7] (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: r5c3 guards oddagon {r5c3, r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9], r7c7, r7c3} (source [r4c9=r9c7]).
            Рєњ color(r5c3) = color([r4c9=r9c7]). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r4c9=r5c3=r9c7] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9], r6c3, r7c3, r9c2, r9c6} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) РЅа color(r9c7). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r5c9, r5c3, r7c3, r9c2, r9c7}
            Bridges: [r6c8=r8c9]Рђћr8c2 (edges r4c9Рђћr5c9 Рєћ r7c3Рђћr9c2), r5c6Рђћr9c6 (edges r5c9Рђћr5c3 Рєћ r9c2Рђћr9c7), r6c3Рђћ[r6c8=r8c9] (edges r5c3Рђћr7c3 Рєћ r9c7Рђћr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c9, r9c8:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r9c8). Identify.

        3.  Guardian: r5c3 guards oddagon {r5c3, r5c9, r8c9, r7c7, r7c3} (source [r4c9=r9c8]).
            Рєњ color(r5c3) = color([r4c9=r9c8]). Identify.

            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c6=r8c5], r8c3, r9c2, r9c6}:
            Diagonals: {[r5c6=r8c5], r9c2} and {r8c3, r9c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r5c6=r8c5]) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, [r5c6=r8c5], r5c8}: [r5c6=r8c5]=X Рєњ r5c3, r5c8РЅаX
              row 8 {[r5c6=r8c5], r8c3, r8c8}: [r5c6=r8c5]=X Рєњ r8c3, r8c8РЅаX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c2=X Рєњ r9c6, r9c7РЅаX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r5c3, r8c3РЅаX Рєњ r6c3=X
              col 6 {[r5c6=r8c5], r6c6, r9c6}: [r5c6=r8c5]=X Рєњ r6c6РЅаX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r8c8, r9c7РЅаX Рєњ r7c9=X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c3=X Рєњ r6c7РЅаX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8, r6c7РЅаX Рєњ r4c9=X
              r4c9=X and r7c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c3) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 8 {[r5c6=r8c5], r8c3, r8c8}: r8c3=X Рєњ [r5c6=r8c5], r8c8РЅаX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c6=X Рєњ r9c2, r9c7РЅаX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X Рєњ r5c3, r6c3РЅаX
              col 6 {[r5c6=r8c5], r6c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X Рєњ r6c6РЅаX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r8c8, r9c7РЅаX Рєњ r7c9=X
              row 5 {r5c3, [r5c6=r8c5], r5c8}: r5c3, [r5c6=r8c5]РЅаX Рєњ r5c8=X

              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8=X and r6c7=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c6) РЅа color(r8c5). Add edge.
        3.  Parity transport:
            row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}
            row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}
            row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c8}
            row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}
            Parallel links: row 5Рєћrow 6 (col, col, box); row 5Рєћrow 8 (col, box, col); row 8Рєћrow 9 (box, box, box)
            Рєњ same permutation parity.
            Each pair shares a column Рєњ distinct permutations.
            4 same-parity permutations from 3 available Рєњ pigeonhole contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

              r5c3=X and r9c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r5c6=r8c5]) = color(r6c2) = X

      Case B: color(r5c6) РЅа color(r8c5). Add edge.

            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r4c9, r7c9, r9c9, r8c7} (spine r7c9Рђћr9c9).
      Рєњ color(r4c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, r8c5, r9c5, r7c6} (spine r8c5Рђћr9c5).
      Рєњ color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  Branch on r5c3, r9c5:
      Case A: color(r5c3) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r5c8, [r5c3=r9c5], r5c6, [r6c5=r7c6]} (spine [r5c3=r9c5]Рђћr5c6).
            Рєњ color(r5c8) = color([r6c5=r7c6]). Identify.
        5.  Diamond {r6c8, [r4c9=r8c7], [r5c8=r6c5=r7c6], r7c9} (spine [r4c9=r8c7]Рђћ[r5c8=r6c5=r7c6]).
            Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
        6.  Diamond {[r6c8=r7c9], [r4c9=r8c7], [r5c8=r6c5=r7c6], r8c5} (spine [r4c9=r8c7]Рђћ[r5c8=r6c5=r7c6]).
            Рєњ color([r6c8=r7c9]) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        7.  KРѓё on {[r5c3=r9c5], [r5c8=r6c5=r7c6], r6c2, [r6c8=r7c9=r8c5]}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c3) РЅа color(r9c5). Add edge.
        8.  Diamond {r5c3, r9c3, r9c5, r9c9} (spine r9c3Рђћr9c5).
            Рєњ color(r5c3) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r8c7], [r5c3=r9c9], r7c9} (spine [r4c9=r8c7]Рђћ[r5c3=r9c9]).
            Рєњ color(r5c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
        10.  Diamond {[r5c3=r9c9], [r4c9=r8c7], [r5c8=r7c9], r6c8} (spine [r4c9=r8c7]Рђћ[r5c8=r7c9]).
            Рєњ color([r5c3=r9c9]) = color(r6c8). Identify.
        11.  KРѓё on {[r5c3=r6c8=r9c9], r5c6, [r5c8=r7c9], [r6c5=r7c6]}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6, r8c4} (spine r7c6Рђћr9c6).
      Рєњ color(r5c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r6c5, r7c6:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        3.  Circular ladder {r5c3Рђћr6c2, [r5c6=r8c4]Рђћ[r6c5=r7c6], r5c8Рђћr6c8}:
            Satellites r9c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: [r6c5=r7c6] guards oddagon {[r6c5=r7c6], r7c7, r8c9, r4c9, r9c6} (source r9c9).
            Рєњ color([r6c5=r7c6]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c5=r7c6=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r6c8, r6c2, r9c2, r9c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) РЅа color(r7c6). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r6c8, r6c5, r7c6, r9c6, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c8Рђћ[r5c6=r8c4] (edges r4c9Рђћr6c8 Рєћ r7c6Рђћr9c6), r6c2Рђћr9c2 (edges r6c8Рђћr6c5 Рєћ r9c6Рђћr9c9), [r5c6=r8c4]Рђћr8c9 (edges r6c5Рђћr7c6 Рєћ r9c9Рђћr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===

  1.  Branch on r5c6, r8c5:

            Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r5c8, r5c3, r9c3, r9c5} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c6) РЅа color(r8c5). Add edge.

            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6, r8c4} (spine r7c6Рђћr9c6).
      Рєњ color(r5c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r6c5, r7c6:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        3.  Circular ladder {r5c3Рђћr6c2, [r5c6=r8c4]Рђћ[r6c5=r7c6], r5c8Рђћr6c7}:
            Satellites r9c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: [r6c5=r7c6] guards oddagon {[r6c5=r7c6], r7c8, r8c9, r4c9, r9c6} (source r9c9).
            Рєњ color([r6c5=r7c6]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c5=r7c6=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r6c7, r6c2, r9c2, r9c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) РЅа color(r7c6). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r6c7, r6c5, r7c6, r9c6, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c8Рђћ[r5c6=r8c4] (edges r4c9Рђћr6c7 Рєћ r7c6Рђћr9c6), r6c2Рђћr9c2 (edges r6c7Рђћr6c5 Рєћ r9c6Рђћr9c9), [r5c6=r8c4]Рђћr8c9 (edges r6c5Рђћr7c6 Рєћ r9c9Рђћr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3Рђћr8c3).
      Рєњ color(r5c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.

        4.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r8c3], [r5c3=r9c2], r7c3} (spine [r4c9=r8c3]Рђћ[r5c3=r9c2]).

            Рєњ color([r5c3=r9c2]) = color(r6c7). Identify.

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (ladder+xwing+branch+guardian) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c9, r5c6:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r5c6). Identify.

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3Рђћr8c3).
      Рєњ color(r5c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r5c8, r7c3:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        3.  Guardian: r8c5 guards oddagon {r8c5, r7c6, r5c6, [r5c3=r9c2], r8c3} (source [r5c8=r7c3]).
            Рєњ color(r8c5) = color([r5c8=r7c3]). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r7c3=r8c5] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r6c7, r6c5, r5c6, [r5c3=r9c2], r8c3, r8c9} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) РЅа color(r7c3). Add edge.
        5.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c8, r6c7, r6c5, r8c5, r8c3, r7c3}
            Bridges: r4c9Рђћr8c9 (edges r5c8Рђћr6c7 Рєћ r8c5Рђћr8c3), r6c2Рђћ[r5c3=r9c2] (edges r6c7Рђћr6c5 Рєћ r8c3Рђћr7c3), r9c5Рђћ[r5c3=r9c2] (edges r6c5Рђћr8c5 Рєћ r7c3Рђћr5c8).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8, r6c7РЅаX Рєњ r4c9=X
              r4c9=X and r5c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c1} (spine r7c3Рђћr8c3).
      Рєњ color(r5c3) = color(r9c1). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r5c8, r7c3:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        3.  Guardian: r8c5 guards oddagon {r8c5, r7c6, r5c6, [r5c3=r9c1], r8c3} (source [r5c8=r7c3]).
            Рєњ color(r8c5) = color([r5c8=r7c3]). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r7c3=r8c5] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r6c7, r6c5, r5c6, [r5c3=r9c1], r8c3, r8c9} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) РЅа color(r7c3). Add edge.
        5.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c8, r6c7, r6c5, r8c5, r8c3, r7c3}
            Bridges: r4c9Рђћr8c9 (edges r5c8Рђћr6c7 Рєћ r8c5Рђћr8c3), r6c2Рђћ[r5c3=r9c1] (edges r6c7Рђћr6c5 Рєћ r8c3Рђћr7c3), r9c5Рђћ[r5c3=r9c1] (edges r6c5Рђћr8c5 Рєћ r7c3Рђћr5c8).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c5=X Рєњ r6c2, r6c7РЅаX
              col 3 {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r5c3=X Рєњ r7c3, r8c3РЅаX

              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c1}: r7c3, r8c3РЅаX Рєњ r9c1=X

              r4c9=X and r7c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c6) = color(r6c2) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c6=X Рєњ r5c3, r5c8РЅаX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c2=X Рєњ r6c5, r6c7РЅаX

              r4c9=X and r5c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c1} (spine r7c3Рђћr8c3).
      Рєњ color(r5c3) = color(r9c1). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, r7c5, r9c5, r8c4} (spine r7c5Рђћr9c5).
      Рєњ color(r6c5) = color(r8c4). Identify.
  3.  Branch on r4c9, r7c3:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r7c3], [r5c3=r9c1], r8c3} (spine [r4c9=r7c3]Рђћ[r5c3=r9c1]).
            Рєњ color(r5c8) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        5.  Diamond {[r5c3=r9c1], [r4c9=r7c3], [r5c8=r8c3], r6c7} (spine [r4c9=r7c3]Рђћ[r5c8=r8c3]).
            Рєњ color([r5c3=r9c1]) = color(r6c7). Identify.
        6.  KРѓё on {[r5c3=r6c7=r9c1], r5c6, [r5c8=r8c3], [r6c5=r8c4]}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) РЅа color(r7c3). Add edge.
        7.  Diamond {r4c9, r7c3, r7c9, r7c5} (spine r7c3Рђћr7c9).
            Рєњ color(r4c9) = color(r7c5). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {r6c7, [r4c9=r7c5], [r6c5=r8c4], r9c5} (spine [r4c9=r7c5]Рђћ[r6c5=r8c4]).
            Рєњ color(r6c7) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r7c5], [r6c7=r9c5], [r6c5=r8c4]} (spine [r4c9=r7c5]Рђћ[r6c7=r9c5]).
            Рєњ color(r5c8) = color([r6c5=r8c4]). Identify.
        10.  KРѓё on {[r5c3=r9c1], [r5c8=r6c5=r8c4], r6c2, [r6c7=r9c5]}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (ladder+xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c9, r5c6:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r5c6). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r5c3 guards oddagon {r5c3, r5c8, [r4c9=r5c6], r6c5, r6c2} (source r6c7).
            Рєњ color(r5c3) = color(r6c7). Identify.
        3.  Circular ladder {[r4c9=r5c6]Рђћr7c9, [r5c3=r6c7]Рђћr7c3, r6c5Рђћr7c5}:
            Satellites r8c3 (rung 2), r9c5 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c3=r6c7], r6c5, r9c5, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {[r5c3=r6c7], r9c5} and {r6c5, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r5c3=r6c7]) = color(r9c5) = X
              row 5 {[r4c9=r5c6], [r5c3=r6c7], r5c8}: [r5c3=r6c7]=X Рєњ [r4c9=r5c6], r5c8РЅаX
              row 6 {[r5c3=r6c7], r6c2, r6c5}: [r5c3=r6c7]=X Рєњ r6c2, r6c5РЅаX
              col 3 {[r5c3=r6c7], r7c3, r8c3}: [r5c3=r6c7]=X Рєњ r7c3, r8c3РЅаX
              col 5 {r6c5, r7c5, r9c5}: r9c5=X Рєњ r7c5РЅаX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c1}: r7c3, r8c3РЅаX Рєњ r9c1=X
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c4, r9c5}: r9c5=X Рєњ r8c4РЅаX
              r9c1=X and r9c5=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c5) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 6 {[r5c3=r6c7], r6c2, r6c5}: r6c5=X Рєњ [r5c3=r6c7], r6c2РЅаX
              col 3 {[r5c3=r6c7], r7c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X Рєњ r7c3РЅаX
              col 5 {r6c5, r7c5, r9c5}: r6c5=X Рєњ r7c5, r9c5РЅаX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c1}: r8c3=X Рєњ r9c1РЅаX
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c4, r9c5}: r7c5, r9c5РЅаX Рєњ r8c4=X
              r8c3=X and r8c4=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) РЅа color(r5c6). Add edge.
        5.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c6, r6c5, r6c2}:
            Diagonals: {r5c3, r6c5} and {r5c6, r6c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r6c5) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c3=X Рєњ r5c6, r5c8РЅаX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c5=X Рєњ r6c2, r6c7РЅаX
              col 3 {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r5c3=X Рєњ r7c3, r8c3РЅаX

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c1} (spine r7c3Рђћr8c3).
      Рєњ color(r5c3) = color(r9c1). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r5c8, r8c3:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {r5c6, [r5c3=r9c1], [r5c8=r8c3], r7c3} (spine [r5c3=r9c1]Рђћ[r5c8=r8c3]).
            Рєњ color(r5c6) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        4.  Guardian: [r5c8=r8c3] guards oddagon {[r5c8=r8c3], [r5c6=r7c3], r6c5, r9c5, r8c4} (source r7c5).
            Рєњ color([r5c8=r8c3]) = color(r7c5). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r7c5=r8c3] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r6c7, r6c5, [r5c6=r7c3], r7c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) РЅа color(r8c3). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c8, r6c7, r6c5, r7c5, r7c3, r8c3}
            Bridges: r4c9Рђћr7c9 (edges r5c8Рђћr6c7 Рєћ r7c5Рђћr7c3), r6c2Рђћ[r5c3=r9c1] (edges r6c7Рђћr6c5 Рєћ r7c3Рђћr8c3), r9c5Рђћ[r5c3=r9c1] (edges r6c5Рђћr7c5 Рєћ r8c3Рђћr5c8).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

      Рєњ color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9Рђћr5c9).
      Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c3, [r6c5=r7c6], [r6c8=r8c9], r7c8} (spine [r6c5=r7c6]Рђћ[r6c8=r8c9]).
      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r6c5=r7c6], [r6c3=r7c8], [r6c8=r8c9], r9c7} (spine [r6c3=r7c8]Рђћ[r6c8=r8c9]).
      Рєњ color([r6c5=r7c6]) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r6c8=r8c9], [r6c3=r7c8], [r6c5=r7c6=r9c7], r9c3} (spine [r6c3=r7c8]Рђћ[r6c5=r7c6=r9c7]).
      Рєњ color([r6c8=r8c9]) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  6.  KРѓё on {[r6c5=r7c6=r9c7], [r6c8=r8c9=r9c3], r8c4, r9c5}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c5, r4c6, r5c6, r7c6} (spine r4c6Рђћr5c6).
      Рєњ color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9Рђћr5c9).
      Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r6c8=r8c9], [r6c5=r7c6], r8c4, r9c5} (spine [r6c5=r7c6]Рђћr8c4).
      Рєњ color([r6c8=r8c9]) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r8c9=r9c5] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r6c3, [r6c5=r7c6], r7c8, r9c7, r9c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_diamond] (ladder+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c5, r7c6:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        2.  Circular ladder {[r6c5=r7c6]Рђћr7c8, r8c4Рђћr8c9, r9c5Рђћr9c7}:
            Satellites r6c8 (rung 1), r9c3 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        3.  Guardian: r9c7 guards oddagon {r9c7, r7c8, [r6c5=r7c6], r6c3, r9c3} (source r6c8).
            Рєњ color(r9c7) = color(r6c8). Identify.
        4.  KРѓё on {r4c9, r5c9, [r6c8=r9c7], r8c9}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) РЅа color(r7c6). Add edge.
        5.  KРѓё on {r4c6, r5c6, r6c5, r7c6}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_diamond] (set+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c5, r7c6:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        2.  SET: {col 6, col 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 5, box 6}.
            Remainder: {[r6c5=r7c6], r8c9} = {[r6c5=r7c6], r6c8}.
            Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
        3.  Guardian: r9c7 guards oddagon {r9c7, r7c7, r8c9, r8c4, r9c5} (source [r6c5=r7c6]).
            Рєњ color(r9c7) = color([r6c5=r7c6]). Identify.

      Case B: color(r6c5) РЅа color(r7c6). Add edge.

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c8, r7c9:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c6, r4c9, [r5c8=r7c9], r5c5}:

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+ladder+hexagon+xwing+branch) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c6, r9c5:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.

              r5c8=X and r7c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r4c9) = color(r5c5) = X

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r4c6Рђћr6c6).

            Рєњ color([r6c6=r8c5]) = color(r9c9). Identify.

            Bivalue oddagon {[r4c6=r9c4], r4c9, r6c8, r6c3, r9c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond+branch) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r4c6Рђћr6c6).
      Рєњ color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8Рђћr6c8).
      Рєњ color(r4c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  3.  Branch on r6c3, r9c5:
      Case A: color(r6c3) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r6c6, [r5c5=r7c6], [r6c3=r9c5], r8c4} (spine [r5c5=r7c6]Рђћ[r6c3=r9c5]).
            Рєњ color(r6c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        5.  Diamond {[r4c9=r8c8], r4c6, [r6c6=r8c4], [r5c5=r7c6]} (spine r4c6Рђћ[r6c6=r8c4]).
            Рєњ color([r4c9=r8c8]) = color([r5c5=r7c6]). Identify.
        6.  KРѓё on {[r4c9=r5c5=r7c6=r8c8], [r6c3=r9c5], [r6c6=r8c4], r6c8}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c3) РЅа color(r9c5). Add edge.
        7.  Diamond {r6c3, r9c3, r9c5, r9c7} (spine r9c3Рђћr9c5).
            Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {r6c8, [r4c9=r8c8], [r6c3=r9c7], r7c9} (spine [r4c9=r8c8]Рђћ[r6c3=r9c7]).
            Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r8c8], [r6c8=r7c9], [r6c3=r9c7]} (spine [r4c9=r8c8]Рђћ[r6c8=r7c9]).
            Рєњ color(r5c8) = color([r6c3=r9c7]). Identify.
        10.  KРѓё on {[r5c5=r7c6], [r5c8=r6c3=r9c7], r6c6, [r6c8=r7c9]}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (set+hexagon+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c6, r9c5:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        2.  SET: {col 6, col 8}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 8}.
            Remainder: {[r4c6=r9c5], r6c6, r8c8} = {[r4c6=r9c5], r4c9, r8c4}.
            Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
        3.  Guardian: r6c3 guards oddagon {r6c3, r6c6, r8c8, r9c7, r9c3} (source [r4c6=r9c5]).
            Рєњ color(r6c3) = color([r4c6=r9c5]). Identify.
        4.  KРѓё on {[r4c6=r6c3=r9c5], r6c6, r6c8, r8c8}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) РЅа color(r9c5). Add edge.
        5.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c6, r6c6, r6c8, r8c8, r9c7, r9c5}
            Bridges: r7c6Рђћr7c9 (edges r4c6Рђћr6c6 Рєћ r8c8Рђћr9c7), r6c3Рђћr9c3 (edges r6c6Рђћr6c8 Рєћ r9c7Рђћr9c5), r5c8Рђћr5c5 (edges r6c8Рђћr8c8 Рєћ r9c5Рђћr4c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r4c6Рђћr6c6).
      Рєњ color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r6c6, r8c4:
      Case A: color(r6c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {r4c6, [r5c5=r7c6], [r6c6=r8c4], r9c5} (spine [r5c5=r7c6]Рђћ[r6c6=r8c4]).
            Рєњ color(r4c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        4.  Guardian: [r6c6=r8c4] guards oddagon {[r6c6=r8c4], [r4c6=r9c5], r4c9, r7c9, r8c7} (source r9c9).
            Рєњ color([r6c6=r8c4]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c6=r8c4=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r4c6=r9c5], r4c9, r6c8, r6c3, r9c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c6) РЅа color(r8c4). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r6c8, r6c6, r8c4, r9c5, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c8Рђћ[r5c5=r7c6] (edges r4c9Рђћr6c8 Рєћ r8c4Рђћr9c5), r6c3Рђћr9c3 (edges r6c8Рђћr6c6 Рєћ r9c5Рђћr9c9), [r5c5=r7c6]Рђћr7c9 (edges r6c6Рђћr8c4 Рєћ r9c9Рђћr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r4c6Рђћr6c6).
      Рєњ color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r6c6, r8c4:
      Case A: color(r6c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {r4c6, [r5c5=r7c6], [r6c6=r8c4], r9c4} (spine [r5c5=r7c6]Рђћ[r6c6=r8c4]).
            Рєњ color(r4c6) = color(r9c4). Identify.
        4.  Guardian: [r6c6=r8c4] guards oddagon {[r6c6=r8c4], [r4c6=r9c4], r4c9, r7c9, r8c7} (source r9c9).
            Рєњ color([r6c6=r8c4]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c6=r8c4=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r4c6=r9c4], r4c9, r6c8, r6c3, r9c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c6) РЅа color(r8c4). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r6c8, r6c6, r8c4, r9c4, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c8Рђћ[r5c5=r7c6] (edges r4c9Рђћr6c8 Рєћ r8c4Рђћr9c4), r6c3Рђћr9c3 (edges r6c8Рђћr6c6 Рєћ r9c4Рђћr9c9), [r5c5=r7c6]Рђћr7c9 (edges r6c6Рђћr8c4 Рєћ r9c9Рђћr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

  1.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8Рђћr6c8).
      Рєњ color(r4c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r5c8, r8c2:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r8c2). Identify.
        3.  Circular ladder {[r4c9=r8c8]Рђћ[r5c8=r8c2], r7c9Рђћr7c3, r9c9Рђћr9c2}:
            Satellites r6c8 (rung 1), r9c6 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: [r5c8=r8c2] guards oddagon {[r5c8=r8c2], r5c5, r4c6, r9c6, r6c8} (source r6c6).
            Рєњ color([r5c8=r8c2]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r6c6=r8c2] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r6c3, r6c8, r9c6, r9c2, r7c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) РЅа color(r8c2). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c8, r6c8, r6c6, r9c6, r9c2, r8c2}
            Bridges: [r4c9=r8c8]Рђћr9c9 (edges r5c8Рђћr6c8 Рєћ r9c6Рђћr9c2), r6c3Рђћr7c3 (edges r6c8Рђћr6c6 Рєћ r9c2Рђћr8c2), r4c6Рђћ[r4c9=r8c8] (edges r6c6Рђћr9c6 Рєћ r8c2Рђћr5c8).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_hexagon] (diamond+xwing+branch) ===

  1.  Branch on r7c9, r9c6:

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8Рђћr6c8).
      Рєњ color(r4c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r5c8, r8c2:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r8c2). Identify.
        3.  Circular ladder {[r4c9=r8c8]Рђћ[r5c8=r8c2], r7c9Рђћr7c3, r9c7Рђћr9c1}:
            Satellites r6c8 (rung 1), r9c6 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: [r5c8=r8c2] guards oddagon {[r5c8=r8c2], r5c5, r4c6, r9c6, r6c8} (source r6c6).
            Рєњ color([r5c8=r8c2]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r6c6=r8c2] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r6c3, r6c8, r9c6, r9c1, r7c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) РЅа color(r8c2). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c8, r6c8, r6c6, r9c6, r9c1, r8c2}
            Bridges: [r4c9=r8c8]Рђћr9c7 (edges r5c8Рђћr6c8 Рєћ r9c6Рђћr9c1), r6c3Рђћr7c3 (edges r6c8Рђћr6c6 Рєћ r9c1Рђћr8c2), r4c6Рђћ[r4c9=r8c8] (edges r6c6Рђћr9c6 Рєћ r8c2Рђћr5c8).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

  1.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8, r7c8} (spine r5c8Рђћr6c8).
      Рєњ color(r4c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.

            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r4c9, r8c9, r9c9, r7c7} (spine r8c9Рђћr9c9).
      Рєњ color(r4c9) = color(r7c7). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r4c6, r9c9:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        3.  Circular ladder {[r4c6=r9c9]Рђћ[r4c9=r7c7], r5c5Рђћr5c8, r6c6Рђћr6c8}:
            Satellites r8c9 (rung 1), r6c3 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: [r4c6=r9c9] guards oddagon {[r4c6=r9c9], r8c9, r6c3, r7c3, r9c2} (source r8c3).
            Рєњ color([r4c6=r9c9]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r4c6=r8c3=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c5, r6c6, r6c3, r8c9, r8c5} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) РЅа color(r9c9). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c6, r6c6, r6c3, r8c3, r8c9, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c5Рђћr8c5 (edges r4c6Рђћr6c6 Рєћ r8c3Рђћr8c9), r6c8Рђћ[r4c9=r7c7] (edges r6c6Рђћr6c3 Рєћ r8c9Рђћr9c9), r7c3Рђћ[r4c9=r7c7] (edges r6c3Рђћr8c3 Рєћ r9c9Рђћr4c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8Рђћr6c8).
      Рєњ color(r4c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r5c8, r8c2:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r8c2). Identify.
        3.  Circular ladder {[r4c9=r8c8]Рђћ[r5c8=r8c2], r7c7Рђћr7c3, r9c9Рђћr9c2}:
            Satellites r6c8 (rung 1), r9c6 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: [r5c8=r8c2] guards oddagon {[r5c8=r8c2], r5c5, r4c6, r9c6, r6c8} (source r6c6).
            Рєњ color([r5c8=r8c2]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r6c6=r8c2] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r6c3, r6c8, r9c6, r9c2, r7c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) РЅа color(r8c2). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c8, r6c8, r6c6, r9c6, r9c2, r8c2}
            Bridges: [r4c9=r8c8]Рђћr9c9 (edges r5c8Рђћr6c8 Рєћ r9c6Рђћr9c2), r6c3Рђћr7c3 (edges r6c8Рђћr6c6 Рєћ r9c2Рђћr8c2), r4c6Рђћ[r4c9=r8c8] (edges r6c6Рђћr9c6 Рєћ r8c2Рђћr5c8).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8Рђћr6c8).
      Рєњ color(r4c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r5c8, r8c2:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r8c2). Identify.
        3.  Circular ladder {[r4c9=r8c8]Рђћ[r5c8=r8c2], r7c7Рђћr7c3, r9c9Рђћr9c1}:
            Satellites r6c8 (rung 1), r9c6 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: [r5c8=r8c2] guards oddagon {[r5c8=r8c2], r5c5, r4c6, r9c6, r6c8} (source r6c6).
            Рєњ color([r5c8=r8c2]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r6c6=r8c2] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r6c3, r6c8, r9c6, r9c1, r7c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) РЅа color(r8c2). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c8, r6c8, r6c6, r9c6, r9c1, r8c2}
            Bridges: [r4c9=r8c8]Рђћr9c9 (edges r5c8Рђћr6c8 Рєћ r9c6Рђћr9c1), r6c3Рђћr7c3 (edges r6c8Рђћr6c6 Рєћ r9c1Рђћr8c2), r4c6Рђћ[r4c9=r8c8] (edges r6c6Рђћr9c6 Рєћ r8c2Рђћr5c8).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8Рђћr6c8).
      Рєњ color(r4c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r5c8, r8c2:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r8c2). Identify.
        3.  Circular ladder {[r4c9=r8c8]Рђћ[r5c8=r8c2], r7c7Рђћr7c3, r9c7Рђћr9c1}:
            Satellites r6c8 (rung 1), r9c6 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: [r5c8=r8c2] guards oddagon {[r5c8=r8c2], r5c5, r4c6, r9c6, r6c8} (source r6c6).
            Рєњ color([r5c8=r8c2]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r6c6=r8c2] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r6c3, r6c8, r9c6, r9c1, r7c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) РЅа color(r8c2). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c8, r6c8, r6c6, r9c6, r9c1, r8c2}
            Bridges: [r4c9=r8c8]Рђћr9c7 (edges r5c8Рђћr6c8 Рєћ r9c6Рђћr9c1), r6c3Рђћr7c3 (edges r6c8Рђћr6c6 Рєћ r9c1Рђћr8c2), r4c6Рђћ[r4c9=r8c8] (edges r6c6Рђћr9c6 Рєћ r8c2Рђћr5c8).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch+guardian) ===

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r4c6Рђћr6c6).
      Рєњ color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r6c6, r8c4:
      Case A: color(r6c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {r4c6, [r5c5=r7c6], [r6c6=r8c4], r9c5} (spine [r5c5=r7c6]Рђћ[r6c6=r8c4]).
            Рєњ color(r4c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r6c3, [r6c6=r8c4], r6c7, r8c7} (spine [r6c6=r8c4]Рђћr6c7).
            Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r8c7). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub r9c9 forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r4c6=r9c5], r4c9, r7c9, [r6c3=r8c7], r9c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c6) РЅа color(r8c4). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r6c7, r6c6, r8c4, r9c5, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c8Рђћ[r5c5=r7c6] (edges r4c9Рђћr6c7 Рєћ r8c4Рђћr9c5), r6c3Рђћr9c3 (edges r6c7Рђћr6c6 Рєћ r9c5Рђћr9c9), [r5c5=r7c6]Рђћr7c9 (edges r6c6Рђћr8c4 Рєћ r9c9Рђћr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+xwing) ===

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r8c6} (spine r4c6Рђћr6c6).
      Рєњ color(r5c5) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c8, [r5c5=r8c6], r8c8, r8c3} (spine [r5c5=r8c6]Рђћr8c8).
      Рєњ color(r5c8) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c9, r6c7, r9c7, r7c9}:
      Diagonals: {r4c9, r9c7} and {r6c7, r7c9} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r4c9) = color(r9c7) = X
        box 6 {r4c9, [r5c8=r8c3], r6c7}: r4c9=X Рєњ [r5c8=r8c3], r6c7РЅаX
        box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r9c7=X Рєњ r7c9, r8c8РЅаX
        row 8 {[r5c5=r8c6], [r5c8=r8c3], r8c8}: [r5c8=r8c3], r8c8РЅаX Рєњ [r5c5=r8c6]=X
        col 6 {r4c6, [r5c5=r8c6], r6c6}: [r5c5=r8c6]=X Рєњ r4c6, r6c6РЅаX
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c6, r6c7РЅаX Рєњ r6c3=X
        col 3 {[r5c8=r8c3], r6c3, r7c3}: r6c3=X Рєњ r7c3РЅаX
        box 7 {[r5c8=r8c3], r7c3, r9c2}: [r5c8=r8c3], r7c3РЅаX Рєњ r9c2=X
        r9c2=X and r9c7=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r7c9) = X

        box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r7c9=X Рєњ r8c8, r9c7РЅаX

        col 6 {r4c6, [r5c5=r8c6], r6c6}: [r5c5=r8c6]=X Рєњ r4c6РЅаX

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===

  1.  Branch on r4c9, r6c3:

              Contradiction.

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r8c6} (spine r4c6Рђћr6c6).

      Рєњ color(r5c8) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c7, [r5c8=r8c3], r6c3, r7c3} (spine [r5c8=r8c3]Рђћr6c3).
      Рєњ color(r6c7) = color(r7c3). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c7=r7c3] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, [r5c8=r8c3], r9c2, r9c7, r7c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3Рђћr8c3).
      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, [r6c3=r9c2], r6c8, r9c8} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]Рђћr6c8).
      Рєњ color(r6c5) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c8, [r6c3=r9c2], [r6c5=r9c8], r9c6} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]Рђћ[r6c5=r9c8]).
      Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r5c9, r5c6, [r6c8=r9c6], [r6c5=r9c8]} (spine r5c6Рђћ[r6c8=r9c6]).
      Рєњ color(r5c9) = color([r6c5=r9c8]). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c8, [r5c9=r6c5=r9c8], [r6c8=r9c6]} (spine r3c8Рђћ[r5c9=r6c5=r9c8]).
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color([r6c8=r9c6]). Identify.
  6.  KРѓё on {[r3c9=r6c8=r9c6], [r5c9=r6c5=r9c8], r8c5, r8c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (xwing+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r8c3, r9c8:
      Case A: color(r8c3) = color(r9c8). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r3c9 guards oddagon {r3c9, r3c8, r6c8, r6c3, r7c3, r9c2, r9c6, r8c5, r8c9} (source [r8c3=r9c8]).
            Рєњ color(r3c9) = color([r8c3=r9c8]). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c9=r8c3=r9c8], r5c9, r5c6, r9c6}:
            Diagonals: {[r3c9=r8c3=r9c8], r5c6} and {r5c9, r9c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c9=r8c3=r9c8]) = color(r5c6) = X
              row 8 {[r3c9=r8c3=r9c8], r8c5, r8c9}: [r3c9=r8c3=r9c8]=X Рєњ r8c5, r8c9РЅаX
              row 9 {[r3c9=r8c3=r9c8], r9c2, r9c6}: [r3c9=r8c3=r9c8]=X Рєњ r9c2, r9c6РЅаX
              col 3 {[r3c9=r8c3=r9c8], r6c3, r7c3}: [r3c9=r8c3=r9c8]=X Рєњ r6c3, r7c3РЅаX
              col 8 {r3c8, [r3c9=r8c3=r9c8], r6c8}: [r3c9=r8c3=r9c8]=X Рєњ r3c8, r6c8РЅаX
              col 9 {[r3c9=r8c3=r9c8], r5c9, r8c9}: [r3c9=r8c3=r9c8]=X Рєњ r5c9РЅаX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c3, r6c8РЅаX Рєњ r6c5=X
              r5c6=X and r6c5=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 9 {[r3c9=r8c3=r9c8], r9c2, r9c6}: r9c6=X Рєњ [r3c9=r8c3=r9c8], r9c2РЅаX
              col 9 {[r3c9=r8c3=r9c8], r5c9, r8c9}: r5c9=X Рєњ r8c9РЅаX
              box 7 {[r3c9=r8c3=r9c8], r7c3, r9c2}: [r3c9=r8c3=r9c8], r9c2РЅаX Рєњ r7c3=X
              row 8 {[r3c9=r8c3=r9c8], r8c5, r8c9}: [r3c9=r8c3=r9c8], r8c9РЅаX Рєњ r8c5=X
              col 3 {[r3c9=r8c3=r9c8], r6c3, r7c3}: r7c3=X Рєњ r6c3РЅаX
              r8c5=X and r9c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r8c3) РЅа color(r9c8). Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: r6c5 guards oddagon {r6c5, r6c3, r7c3, r9c2, r9c8, r8c9, r8c5} (source r8c3).
            Рєњ color(r6c5) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c5=r8c3] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r6c3, r6c8, r9c8, r9c2, r7c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_ladder] (diamond+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:

      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color([r5c9=r9c8]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c9=r6c3=r9c8] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c8, r3c9, r8c9, r8c3, r9c3, [r5c6=r9c5], r5c8} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9, r6c7} (spine r5c8Рђћr5c9).
      Рєњ color(r5c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r8c3Рђћr9c3, r8c5Рђћr9c5, r8c9Рђћr9c8}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Circular ladder {r6c3Рђћr7c6, r8c3Рђћr8c5, r9c3Рђћr9c5}:
      Satellites [r5c6=r6c7] (rung 1), r8c9 (rung 2), r9c8 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  4.  Diamond {r5c9, [r5c6=r6c7], r5c8, r9c8} (spine [r5c6=r6c7]Рђћr5c8).
      Рєњ color(r5c9) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  5.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c9=r9c8] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c8, r3c9, r8c9, [r5c6=r6c7], r5c8} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

        r6c3=X and r6c6=X are adjacent

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3Рђћr8c3).

      Рєњ color(r7c3) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r6c6, [r6c3=r8c7=r9c2], [r7c3=r8c6], r8c3} (spine [r6c3=r8c7=r9c2]Рђћ[r7c3=r8c6]).
      Рєњ color(r6c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r5c6, [r6c6=r8c3], [r7c3=r8c6], [r6c3=r8c7=r9c2]} (spine [r6c6=r8c3]Рђћ[r7c3=r8c6]).
      Рєњ color(r5c6) = color([r6c3=r8c7=r9c2]). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {r5c9, [r5c6=r6c3=r8c7=r9c2], r5c8, r9c8} (spine [r5c6=r6c3=r8c7=r9c2]Рђћr5c8).

  8.  KРѓё on {[r3c9=r5c8], [r5c6=r6c3=r8c7=r9c2], [r5c9=r9c8], r9c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (set+ladder+guardian) ===

  1.  Circular ladder {r3c8Рђћr3c9, r5c8Рђћr5c9, r9c8Рђћr9c9}:
