  2.  Guardian: r6c3 guards oddagon {r6c3, r6c6, r5c6, r8c7, r8c3} (source r8c6).
      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 8, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {[r6c3=r8c6]} = {r7c3}.
      Рєњ color([r6c3=r8c6]) = color(r7c3), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

      Bivalue oddagon {r3c8, r3c9, r4c9, r6c7, r6c3, r8c3, r8c6, [r5c6=r9c5], r9c8} (length 9) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_diamond] (ladder+xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c8, r4c9:
      Case A: color(r3c8) = color(r4c9). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c6, r8c9, r9c8, r9c5}:
            Diagonals: {r8c6, r9c8} and {r8c9, r9c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r8c6) = color(r9c8) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c9}: r8c6=X Рєњ r8c3, r8c9РЅаX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c8}: r9c8=X Рєњ r9c3, r9c5РЅаX
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3, r9c3РЅаX Рєњ r6c3=X
              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r8c6}: r8c6=X Рєњ r5c6, r7c6РЅаX
              col 8 {[r3c8=r4c9], r5c8, r9c8}: r9c8=X Рєњ [r3c8=r4c9], r5c8РЅаX
              col 9 {[r3c8=r4c9], r3c9, r8c9}: [r3c8=r4c9], r8c9РЅаX Рєњ r3c9=X
              box 6 {[r3c8=r4c9], r5c8, r6c7}: [r3c8=r4c9], r5c8РЅаX Рєњ r6c7=X
              r6c3=X and r6c7=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c9) = color(r9c5) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c9}: r8c9=X Рєњ r8c3, r8c6РЅаX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c8}: r9c5=X Рєњ r9c3, r9c8РЅаX
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3, r9c3РЅаX Рєњ r6c3=X
              col 9 {[r3c8=r4c9], r3c9, r8c9}: r8c9=X Рєњ [r3c8=r4c9], r3c9РЅаX
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c6, r9c5}: r9c5=X Рєњ r7c6РЅаX
              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r8c6}: r7c6, r8c6РЅаX Рєњ r5c6=X
              col 8 {[r3c8=r4c9], r5c8, r9c8}: [r3c8=r4c9], r9c8РЅаX Рєњ r5c8=X
              box 6 {[r3c8=r4c9], r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8=X Рєњ r6c7РЅаX
              r5c6=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c8) РЅа color(r4c9). Add edge.
        3.  Guardian: r8c9 guards oddagon {r8c9, r3c9, r4c9, r5c8, r9c8} (source r3c8).
            Рєњ color(r8c9) = color(r3c8). Identify.
        4.  Circular ladder {[r3c8=r8c9]Рђћr9c8, r8c3Рђћr9c3, r8c6Рђћr9c5}:
            Satellites r5c8 (rung 1), r6c3 (rung 2), r7c6 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
        5.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c8=r8c9], r5c8, r6c3, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {[r3c8=r8c9], r6c3} and {r5c8, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c8=r8c9]) = color(r6c3) = X
              row 8 {[r3c8=r8c9], r8c3, r8c6}: [r3c8=r8c9]=X Рєњ r8c3, r8c6РЅаX
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r6c3=X Рєњ r9c3РЅаX
              col 8 {[r3c8=r8c9], r5c8, r9c8}: [r3c8=r8c9]=X Рєњ r5c8, r9c8РЅаX
              col 9 {[r3c8=r8c9], r3c9, r4c9}: [r3c8=r8c9]=X Рєњ r3c9, r4c9РЅаX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r4c9, r5c8РЅаX Рєњ r6c7=X
              r6c3=X and r6c7=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c8) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 8 {[r3c8=r8c9], r8c3, r8c6}: r8c3=X Рєњ [r3c8=r8c9], r8c6РЅаX
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X Рєњ r6c3, r9c3РЅаX
              col 8 {[r3c8=r8c9], r5c8, r9c8}: r5c8=X Рєњ r9c8РЅаX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8=X Рєњ r4c9, r6c7РЅаX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c8}: r9c3, r9c8РЅаX Рєњ r9c5=X

      Case B: color(r5c9) РЅа color(r9c8). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c9, r6c8, r6c6, r7c6, r8c5, r8c9}
            Bridges: r9c8Рђћr9c4 (edges r5c9Рђћr6c8 Рєћ r7c6Рђћr8c5), r6c3Рђћr8c3 (edges r6c8Рђћr6c6 Рєћ r8c5Рђћr8c9), r3c6Рђћr3c9 (edges r6c6Рђћr7c6 Рєћ r8c9Рђћr5c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3Рђћr8c3).
      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c8, [r6c3=r9c2], r9c8, r9c4} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]Рђћr9c8).
      Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r9c4). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c6, r6c6, r7c6, [r6c8=r9c4]} (spine r6c6Рђћr7c6).
      Рєњ color(r3c6) = color([r6c8=r9c4]). Identify.
  4.  Guardian: r8c3 guards oddagon {r8c3, [r6c3=r9c2], r6c6, r7c6, r8c5} (source [r3c6=r6c8=r9c4]).
      Рєњ color(r8c3) = color([r3c6=r6c8=r9c4]). Identify.
  5.  KРѓё on {[r3c6=r6c8=r8c3=r9c4], r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (set+hexagon+guardian) ===

      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  2.  Guardian: r9c8 guards oddagon {r9c8, [r3c9=r8c7], r3c6, r7c6, r9c5} (source r8c6).
      Рєњ color(r9c8) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  3.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r6c3, r6c9, r7c9, r7c6, r9c5, r9c3}

      Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
      but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

      Case A: color(r6c7) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c8, [r6c7=r7c6], r8c5, r8c8}:
            Diagonals: {r5c8, r8c5} and {[r6c7=r7c6], r8c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c8) = color(r8c5) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c8}: r8c5=X Рєњ r8c3, r8c8РЅаX
              box 6 {r5c8, [r6c7=r7c6], r6c9}: r5c8=X Рєњ [r6c7=r7c6], r6c9РЅаX
              box 8 {[r6c7=r7c6], r8c5, r9c6}: r8c5=X Рєњ r9c6РЅаX
              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c7=r7c6], r6c9}: [r6c7=r7c6], r6c9РЅаX Рєњ r6c3=X
              col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r6c3=X Рєњ r7c3РЅаX
              col 6 {r3c6, [r6c7=r7c6], r9c6}: [r6c7=r7c6], r9c6РЅаX Рєњ r3c6=X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3, r8c3РЅаX Рєњ r9c2=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c2=X Рєњ r9c9РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r6c9, r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r3c9=X
              r3c6=X and r3c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r6c7=r7c6]) = color(r8c8) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c7=r7c6], r6c9}: [r6c7=r7c6]=X Рєњ r6c3, r6c9РЅаX
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c8}: r8c8=X Рєњ r8c3, r8c5РЅаX
              col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r6c3, r8c3РЅаX Рєњ r7c3=X
              col 6 {r3c6, [r6c7=r7c6], r9c6}: [r6c7=r7c6]=X Рєњ r3c6, r9c6РЅаX
              box 6 {r5c8, [r6c7=r7c6], r6c9}: [r6c7=r7c6]=X Рєњ r5c8РЅаX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3=X Рєњ r9c2РЅаX
              [r6c7=r7c6]=X and r7c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c7) РЅа color(r7c6). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c3, r8c8, r9c9, r9c2}:
            Diagonals: {r8c3, r9c9} and {r8c8, r9c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r8c3) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c8}: r8c3=X Рєњ r8c5, r8c8РЅаX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c9=X Рєњ r9c2, r9c6РЅаX
              col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X Рєњ r6c3, r7c3РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X Рєњ r3c9, r6c9РЅаX
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c6}: r8c5, r9c6РЅаX Рєњ r7c6=X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c7, r6c9}: r6c3, r6c9РЅаX Рєњ r6c7=X
              col 6 {r3c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r7c6=X Рєњ r3c6РЅаX
              box 6 {r5c8, r6c7, r6c9}: r6c7=X Рєњ r5c8РЅаX
              r6c7=X and r7c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c8) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c8}: r8c8=X Рєњ r8c3, r8c5РЅаX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c2=X Рєњ r9c6, r9c9РЅаX

              col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3, r8c3РЅаX Рєњ r6c3=X
              col 6 {r3c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r7c6=X Рєњ r3c6РЅаX

              r5c8=X and r8c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_set] (diamond+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:

      Рєњ color(r3c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  3.  Guardian: r9c9 guards oddagon {r9c9, r8c8, [r5c8=r6c3], r7c3, r9c2} (source r8c3).
      Рєњ color(r9c9) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r8c3=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r3c6=r8c5], r3c9, r6c9, [r5c8=r6c3], r8c8} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 8, box 9}.

      Рєњ color(r3c6) = color(r8c7). Identify.

      Remainder: {r3c9} = {[r3c6=r8c7]}.
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color([r3c6=r8c7]), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+set+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3, r9c2} (spine r8c3Рђћr9c3).
      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c6, [r6c3=r9c2], r6c8, r9c8} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]Рђћr6c8).
      Рєњ color(r6c6) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 6, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 7}.
      Remainder: {[r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r9c8]} = {r5c8, r5c9, r8c3}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c9, r6c8, [r6c3=r9c2], r8c3}:
      Diagonals: {r5c9, [r6c3=r9c2]} and {r6c8, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c9) = color([r6c3=r9c2]) = X
        row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r9c8], r6c8}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X Рєњ [r6c6=r9c8], r6c8РЅаX
        row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r9c8], r9c3}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X Рєњ r9c3РЅаX
        col 3 {[r6c3=r9c2], r8c3, r9c3}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X Рєњ r8c3РЅаX
        col 8 {r5c8, [r6c6=r9c8], r6c8}: [r6c6=r9c8], r6c8РЅаX Рєњ r5c8=X
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r5c9=X Рєњ r3c9, r8c9РЅаX
        box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c8}: r5c8=X and r5c9=X clash
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c8) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r9c8], r6c8}: r6c8=X Рєњ [r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r9c8]РЅаX
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c3=X Рєњ r8c5, r8c9РЅаX
        row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r9c8], r9c3}: [r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r9c8]РЅаX Рєњ r9c3=X
        col 3 {[r6c3=r9c2], r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X and r9c3=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+set+xwing+guardian) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c6, r8c6, r9c6, r8c5} (spine r8c6Рђћr9c6).
      Рєњ color(r3c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r3c6=r8c5], r3c9, r8c9, r5c9} (spine r3c9Рђћr8c9).
      Рєњ color([r3c6=r8c5]) = color(r5c9). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 8, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 8, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c3} = {r7c8}.
      Рєњ color(r9c3) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub r6c8 forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r3c6=r5c9=r8c5], r5c8, [r7c8=r9c3], r6c3, r6c5} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

  3.  SET: {col 6, col 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 8, box 9}.
      Remainder: {[r3c6=r5c9=r8c5], r3c9} = {[r3c6=r5c9=r8c5], r7c8, r9c7}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  4.  KРѓё on {[r3c6=r5c9=r8c5], r5c8, r6c8, r7c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_diamond] (set+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Guardian: r3c9 guards oddagon {r3c9, r3c6, r9c6, r8c5, r8c9} (source r8c6).
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 8, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 8, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c3} = {r7c8}.
      Рєњ color(r9c3) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  3.  Branch on r5c9, r8c5:
      Case A: color(r5c9) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub r6c8 forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c8, [r5c9=r8c5], r6c5, r6c3, [r7c8=r9c3]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c9) РЅа color(r8c5). Add edge.
        5.  KРѓё on {[r3c9=r8c6], r5c9, r8c5, r8c9}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c6, r7c6, r9c6, r8c5} (spine r7c6Рђћr9c6).
      Рєњ color(r3c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 7, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 8, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c3} = {[r3c6=r8c5]}.
      Рєњ color(r9c3) = color([r3c6=r8c5]). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r3c6=r8c5=r9c3], r6c3, r6c5, r6c7} (spine r6c3Рђћr6c5).
      Рєњ color([r3c6=r8c5=r9c3]) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c6=r6c7=r8c5=r9c3], r3c9, r7c9, r7c6}:
      Diagonals: {[r3c6=r6c7=r8c5=r9c3], r7c9} and {r3c9, r7c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r3c6=r6c7=r8c5=r9c3]) = color(r7c9) = X
        row 6 {[r3c6=r6c7=r8c5=r9c3], r6c3, r6c5}: [r3c6=r6c7=r8c5=r9c3]=X Рєњ r6c3, r6c5РЅаX
        row 7 {r7c6, r7c8, r7c9}: r7c9=X Рєњ r7c6, r7c8РЅаX
        row 9 {[r3c6=r6c7=r8c5=r9c3], r9c6, r9c8}: [r3c6=r6c7=r8c5=r9c3]=X Рєњ r9c6, r9c8РЅаX
        col 8 {r5c8, r7c8, r9c8}: r7c8, r9c8РЅаX Рєњ r5c8=X
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r7c9=X Рєњ r3c9, r5c9РЅаX
        box 6 {[r3c6=r6c7=r8c5=r9c3], r5c8, r5c9}: [r3c6=r6c7=r8c5=r9c3]=X and r5c8=X clash
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r7c6) = X
        row 7 {r7c6, r7c8, r7c9}: r7c6=X Рєњ r7c8, r7c9РЅаX
        col 6 {[r3c6=r6c7=r8c5=r9c3], r7c6, r9c6}: r7c6=X Рєњ [r3c6=r6c7=r8c5=r9c3], r9c6РЅаX
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r3c9=X Рєњ r5c9РЅаX
        box 6 {[r3c6=r6c7=r8c5=r9c3], r5c8, r5c9}: [r3c6=r6c7=r8c5=r9c3], r5c9РЅаX Рєњ r5c8=X
        box 9 {r7c8, r7c9, r9c8}: r7c8, r7c9РЅаX Рєњ r9c8=X
        r5c8=X and r9c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+set+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 6, row 8}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 8}.
      Remainder: {r6c3, r8c8} = {r5c8, r9c6}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Diamond {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6, r5c8} (spine r5c6Рђћr9c6).
      Рєњ color(r3c6) = color(r5c8). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c6=r5c8], r3c9, r7c9, r8c8}:
      Diagonals: {[r3c6=r5c8], r7c9} and {r3c9, r8c8} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r3c6=r5c8]) = color(r7c9) = X
        col 6 {[r3c6=r5c8], r5c6, r9c6}: [r3c6=r5c8]=X Рєњ r5c6, r9c6РЅаX
        col 8 {[r3c6=r5c8], r6c8, r8c8}: [r3c6=r5c8]=X Рєњ r6c8, r8c8РЅаX
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: r7c9=X Рєњ r3c9, r6c9РЅаX
        box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r7c9=X Рєњ r9c7РЅаX
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c8, r6c9РЅаX Рєњ r6c3=X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c6, r9c7РЅаX Рєњ r9c3=X
        r6c3=X and r9c3=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r8c8) = X
        row 8 {r8c4, r8c5, r8c8}: r8c8=X Рєњ r8c4, r8c5РЅаX
        col 8 {[r3c6=r5c8], r6c8, r8c8}: r8c8=X Рєњ [r3c6=r5c8], r6c8РЅаX
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: r3c9=X Рєњ r6c9, r7c9РЅаX
        box 8 {r8c4, r8c5, r9c6}: r8c4, r8c5РЅаX Рєњ r9c6=X
        box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r8c8=X Рєњ r9c7РЅаX
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c8, r6c9РЅаX Рєњ r6c3=X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c6=X Рєњ r9c3РЅаX
        col 6 {[r3c6=r5c8], r5c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X Рєњ r5c6РЅаX
        r6c3=X and r8c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  3.  SET: {row 8, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 7, box 9}.

      Рєњ color(r9c6) = color([r6c8=r7c9=r8c3]). Identify.

      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r6c9, r5c8, r5c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r9c9, r8c7, r8c3, r6c3, r6c8, r5c8, r5c6} (length 9) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c9, r5c8, r6c8, r7c8} (spine r5c8Рђћr6c8).
      Рєњ color(r6c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c8, r6c8, [r6c9=r7c8], r6c3} (spine r6c8Рђћ[r6c9=r7c8]).

  3.  Diamond {r3c9, [r6c9=r7c8], r9c9, r8c7} (spine [r6c9=r7c8]Рђћr9c9).
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r3c9=r8c7], r3c6, r8c6, r5c6} (spine r3c6Рђћr8c6).

      Рєњ color([r6c9=r7c8]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  6.  KРѓё on {[r6c9=r7c8=r8c3], r9c2, r9c3, r9c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (set+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 6, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{col 3, col 8}.
      Remainder: {r6c9, r9c2, r9c9} = {r5c8, r7c8, r8c3}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Guardian: r5c6 guards oddagon {r5c6, r5c8, r7c8, r8c7, r8c6} (source r8c3).
      Рєњ color(r5c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c6=r8c3], r5c8, r6c9, r6c3}:
      Diagonals: {[r5c6=r8c3], r6c9} and {r5c8, r6c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r5c6=r8c3]) = color(r6c9) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X Рєњ r6c3, r6c8РЅаX
        row 8 {[r5c6=r8c3], r8c6, r8c7}: [r5c6=r8c3]=X Рєњ r8c6, r8c7РЅаX
        col 3 {[r5c6=r8c3], r6c3, r9c3}: [r5c6=r8c3]=X Рєњ r9c3РЅаX
        col 6 {r3c6, [r5c6=r8c3], r8c6}: [r5c6=r8c3]=X Рєњ r3c6РЅаX
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r6c9=X Рєњ r3c9, r9c9РЅаX
        box 6 {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X Рєњ r5c8РЅаX
        box 7 {[r5c6=r8c3], r9c2, r9c3}: [r5c6=r8c3]=X Рєњ r9c2РЅаX
        box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r8c7, r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r7c8=X
        [r5c6=r8c3]=X and r7c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c8) = color(r6c3) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c3=X Рєњ r6c8, r6c9РЅаX
        col 3 {[r5c6=r8c3], r6c3, r9c3}: r6c3=X Рєњ [r5c6=r8c3], r9c3РЅаX
        col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r7c8}: r5c8=X Рєњ r7c8РЅаX
        box 7 {[r5c6=r8c3], r9c2, r9c3}: [r5c6=r8c3], r9c3РЅаX Рєњ r9c2=X
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c9}: r9c2=X Рєњ r9c9РЅаX
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r6c9, r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r3c9=X
        box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c8, r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r8c7=X
        row 8 {[r5c6=r8c3], r8c6, r8c7}: r8c7=X Рєњ r8c6РЅаX
        col 6 {r3c6, [r5c6=r8c3], r8c6}: [r5c6=r8c3], r8c6РЅаX Рєњ r3c6=X
        r3c6=X and r3c9=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.

      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color([r3c9=r8c7]). Identify.

      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  4.  KРѓё on {[r3c9=r6c3=r8c7], r5c8, r6c8, r6c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8Рђћr6c8).
      Рєњ color(r6c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c3, [r6c9=r8c8], r8c3, r8c5} (spine [r6c9=r8c8]Рђћr8c3).
      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 6, row 8}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 7}.
      Remainder: {[r6c3=r8c5], [r6c9=r8c8]} = {r5c8, r9c2, r9c3}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c8, r6c8, [r6c3=r8c5], r9c3}:
      Diagonals: {r5c8, [r6c3=r8c5]} and {r6c8, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c8) = color([r6c3=r8c5]) = X
        row 6 {[r6c3=r8c5], r6c8, [r6c9=r8c8]}: [r6c3=r8c5]=X Рєњ r6c8, [r6c9=r8c8]РЅаX
        row 8 {[r6c3=r8c5], [r6c9=r8c8], r8c3}: [r6c3=r8c5]=X Рєњ r8c3РЅаX
        col 3 {[r6c3=r8c5], r8c3, r9c3}: [r6c3=r8c5]=X Рєњ r9c3РЅаX
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}: r8c3, r9c3РЅаX Рєњ r9c2=X
        r5c8=X and r9c2=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c8) = color(r9c3) = X
        row 6 {[r6c3=r8c5], r6c8, [r6c9=r8c8]}: r6c8=X Рєњ [r6c3=r8c5], [r6c9=r8c8]РЅаX
        row 8 {[r6c3=r8c5], [r6c9=r8c8], r8c3}: [r6c3=r8c5], [r6c9=r8c8]РЅаX Рєњ r8c3=X
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c9}: r9c3=X Рєњ r9c2, r9c9РЅаX
        col 3 {[r6c3=r8c5], r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X and r9c3=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r7c7). Identify.

      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.

      Diagonals: {[r3c9=r5c6=r7c7], r6c3} and {r6c9, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r3c9=r5c6=r7c7]) = color(r6c3) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c3=X Рєњ r6c8, r6c9РЅаX

        box 9 {[r3c9=r5c6=r7c7], r8c8, r9c9}: [r3c9=r5c6=r7c7]=X Рєњ r8c8РЅаX
        [r3c9=r5c6=r7c7]=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c9) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X Рєњ r6c3, r6c8РЅаX
        col 9 {[r3c9=r5c6=r7c7], r6c9, r9c9}: r6c9=X Рєњ [r3c9=r5c6=r7c7], r9c9РЅаX
        box 6 {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X Рєњ r5c8РЅаX

        box 9 {[r3c9=r5c6=r7c7], r8c8, r9c9}: [r3c9=r5c6=r7c7], r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r8c8=X
        r8c3=X and r8c8=X are adjacent

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+set+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3Рђћr8c3).
      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 6, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 9}.
      Remainder: {[r6c3=r9c2]} = {r5c8, r8c7}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c8, r8c7, r8c6}:
      Diagonals: {r5c6, r8c7} and {r5c8, r8c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r8c7) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c7}: r8c7=X Рєњ r8c3, r8c6РЅаX
        col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r8c6}: r5c6=X Рєњ r3c6РЅаX
        box 9 {r8c7, r9c8, r9c9}: r8c7=X Рєњ r9c8, r9c9РЅаX
        row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], r9c8, r9c9}: r9c8, r9c9РЅаX Рєњ [r6c3=r9c2]=X
        col 3 {[r6c3=r9c2], r7c3, r8c3}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X Рєњ r7c3РЅаX
        row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], r6c8, r6c9}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X Рєњ r6c8, r6c9РЅаX
        col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r9c8}: r6c8, r9c8РЅаX Рєњ r5c8=X
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r6c9, r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r3c9=X
        r5c6=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c8) = color(r8c6) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c7}: r8c6=X Рєњ r8c3, r8c7РЅаX
        col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r8c6}: r8c6=X Рєњ r3c6, r5c6РЅаX
        col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r9c8}: r5c8=X Рєњ r6c8, r9c8РЅаX
        box 6 {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9}: r5c8=X Рєњ r6c9РЅаX
        box 9 {r8c7, r9c8, r9c9}: r8c7, r9c8РЅаX Рєњ r9c9=X
        row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], r6c8, r6c9}: r6c8, r6c9РЅаX Рєњ [r6c3=r9c2]=X
        row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], r9c8, r9c9}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X and r9c9=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (oddwheel+guardian) ===

      Рєњ color(r8c3) = color(r6c8). Identify.

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+set+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8Рђћr6c8).
      Рєњ color(r6c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c3, [r6c9=r8c8], r8c3, r8c5} (spine [r6c9=r8c8]Рђћr8c3).
      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 6, row 8}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 7}.
      Remainder: {[r6c3=r8c5], [r6c9=r8c8]} = {r5c8, r7c3, r9c2}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c8, r6c8, [r6c3=r8c5], r7c3}:
      Diagonals: {r5c8, [r6c3=r8c5]} and {r6c8, r7c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c8) = color([r6c3=r8c5]) = X
        row 6 {[r6c3=r8c5], r6c8, [r6c9=r8c8]}: [r6c3=r8c5]=X Рєњ r6c8, [r6c9=r8c8]РЅаX
        row 8 {[r6c3=r8c5], [r6c9=r8c8], r8c3}: [r6c3=r8c5]=X Рєњ r8c3РЅаX
        col 3 {[r6c3=r8c5], r7c3, r8c3}: [r6c3=r8c5]=X Рєњ r7c3РЅаX
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3, r8c3РЅаX Рєњ r9c2=X
        r5c8=X and r9c2=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c8) = color(r7c3) = X
        row 6 {[r6c3=r8c5], r6c8, [r6c9=r8c8]}: r6c8=X Рєњ [r6c3=r8c5], [r6c9=r8c8]РЅаX
        row 8 {[r6c3=r8c5], [r6c9=r8c8], r8c3}: [r6c3=r8c5], [r6c9=r8c8]РЅаX Рєњ r8c3=X
        col 3 {[r6c3=r8c5], r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3=X and r8c3=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c9, r9c3:
      Case A: color(r6c9) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        2.  SET: {row 6, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 8}.
            Remainder: {r6c3, [r6c9=r9c3]} = {r5c8, r8c6}.
            Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
        3.  Guardian: r8c6 guards oddagon {r8c6, r5c8, r6c7, r6c3, r8c3} (source [r6c9=r9c3]).
            Рєњ color(r8c6) = color([r6c9=r9c3]). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c9=r8c6=r9c3] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r7c9, r8c7, r6c7, r5c8, r5c6} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c9) РЅа color(r9c3). Add edge.
        5.  Guardian: r8c7 guards oddagon {r8c7, r6c7, r6c9, r9c3, r8c3} (source r6c3).
            Рєњ color(r8c7) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        6.  SET: {row 6, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 8}.
            Remainder: {[r6c3=r8c7], r9c3} = {r5c8, r8c6}.
            Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
        7.  Guardian: r5c8 guards oddagon {r5c8, r6c9, r9c3, r8c3, r8c6} (source [r6c3=r8c7]).
            Рєњ color(r5c8) = color([r6c3=r8c7]). Identify.
        8.  SET: {row 6, row 8}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 8}.
            Remainder: {[r5c8=r6c3=r8c7], r8c3} = {[r5c8=r6c3=r8c7], r9c4, r9c5}.
            Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
        9.  KРѓё on {[r5c8=r6c3=r8c7], r8c6, r9c4, r9c5}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

      Bridges: r6c9Рђћ[r6c3=r8c7] (edges r5c8Рђћr6c7 Рєћ r8c9Рђћr8c3), [r6c3=r8c7]Рђћr9c3 (edges r6c7Рђћr7c7 Рєћ r8c3Рђћr9c2), [r6c3=r8c7]Рђћr9c3 (edges r7c7Рђћr8c9 Рєћ r9c2Рђћr5c8).
      Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
      but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r6c7, r6c9, r6c3} (spine r6c7Рђћr6c9).

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_hexagon] (set+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Guardian: r6c3 guards oddagon {r6c3, r6c7, r7c7, r8c9, r8c3} (source r8c7).
      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r8c7). Identify.

      Remainder: {[r6c3=r8c7], r8c9} = {r5c8, r9c2, r9c3}.

        box 9 {[r6c3=r8c7], r7c7, r8c9}: [r6c3=r8c7]=X Рєњ r8c9РЅаX

        row 8 {[r6c3=r8c7], r8c3, r8c9}: [r6c3=r8c7]=X and r8c3=X clash

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (set+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {col 7, col 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r3c9} = {r5c8}.
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r5c8). Identify.
  2.  Branch on [r3c9=r5c8], r9c2:
      Case A: color([r3c9=r5c8]) = color(r9c2). Identify.
        3.  KРѓё on {r3c6, [r3c9=r5c8=r9c2], r5c6, r9c6}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r3c9=r5c8]) РЅа color(r9c2). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, [r3c9=r5c8], r9c2, r9c6}:
            Diagonals: {r3c6, r9c2} and {[r3c9=r5c8], r9c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c6}: r9c2=X Рєњ r9c3, r9c6РЅаX
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r3c6=X Рєњ r5c6РЅаX
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}: r9c2=X Рєњ r8c3РЅаX
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3, r9c3РЅаX Рєњ r6c3=X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c7, r6c9}: r6c3=X Рєњ r6c7, r6c9РЅаX
              box 6 {[r3c9=r5c8], r6c7, r6c9}: r6c7, r6c9РЅаX Рєњ [r3c9=r5c8]=X
              r3c6=X and [r3c9=r5c8]=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r3c9=r5c8]) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c6}: r9c6=X Рєњ r9c2, r9c3РЅаX
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X Рєњ r3c6, r5c6РЅаX
              col 9 {[r3c9=r5c8], r6c9, r7c9}: [r3c9=r5c8]=X Рєњ r6c9, r7c9РЅаX

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c8, r9c5:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r6c3 guards oddagon {r6c3, r6c7, r6c9, r9c9, r9c3} (source [r5c8=r9c5]).
            Рєњ color(r6c3) = color([r5c8=r9c5]). Identify.
        3.  SET: {row 6, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 9}.
            Remainder: {[r5c8=r6c3=r9c5], r9c3} = {[r5c8=r6c3=r9c5], r8c7, r8c8}.
            Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
        4.  KРѓё on {[r5c8=r6c3=r9c5], r8c3, r8c7, r8c8}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) РЅа color(r9c5). Add edge.
        5.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c8, r8c8, r9c9, r9c5}:
            Diagonals: {r5c8, r9c9} and {r8c8, r9c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c8) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c9=X Рєњ r9c3, r9c5РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X Рєњ r3c9, r6c9РЅаX
              box 6 {r5c8, r6c7, r6c9}: r5c8=X Рєњ r6c7РЅаX
              box 9 {r8c7, r8c8, r9c9}: r9c9=X Рєњ r8c7, r8c8РЅаX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c7, r6c9}: r6c7, r6c9РЅаX Рєњ r6c3=X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c7, r8c8}: r8c7, r8c8РЅаX Рєњ r8c3=X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r6c3=X and r8c3=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c8) = color(r9c5) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c7, r8c8}: r8c8=X Рєњ r8c3, r8c7РЅаX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c5=X Рєњ r9c3, r9c9РЅаX
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3, r9c3РЅаX Рєњ r6c3=X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c7, r6c9}: r6c3=X Рєњ r6c7, r6c9РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r6c9, r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r3c9=X
              box 6 {r5c8, r6c7, r6c9}: r6c7, r6c9РЅаX Рєњ r5c8=X
              r5c8=X and r8c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

  1.  SET: {col 7, col 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r3c9} = {r5c8}.

      Remainder: {r6c3, r6c7, r6c9} = {r7c7, r8c3, r8c9}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.

      Diagonals: {r6c3, r7c7} and {r6c7, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r6c3) = color(r7c7) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c7, r6c9}: r6c3=X Рєњ r6c7, r6c9РЅаX
        col 7 {r6c7, r7c7, r9c7}: r7c7=X Рєњ r9c7РЅаX

        [r3c9=r5c8=r7c6]=X and r7c7=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c7, r6c9}: r6c7=X Рєњ r6c3, r6c9РЅаX
        col 7 {r6c7, r7c7, r9c7}: r6c7=X Рєњ r7c7, r9c7РЅаX

        box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c7}: r7c7, r9c7РЅаX Рєњ r8c9=X
        r8c3=X and r8c9=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:

  3.  Diamond {r6c7, [r5c8=r6c3], r6c9, [r8c3=r9c9]} (spine [r5c8=r6c3]Рђћr6c9).

  5.  Diamond {r3c9, [r6c7=r8c3=r9c9], [r6c9=r8c8], [r5c8=r6c3]} (spine [r6c7=r8c3=r9c9]Рђћ[r6c9=r8c8]).

  7.  KРѓё on {[r3c9=r5c8=r6c3=r7c6], [r6c7=r8c3=r9c9], [r6c9=r8c8], r7c7}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (set+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 6, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 7}.
      Remainder: {r6c3, r9c9} = {r5c8, r8c3}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Branch on r3c9, r5c8:
      Case A: color(r3c9) = color(r5c8). Identify.
        3.  Guardian: r7c7 guards oddagon {r7c7, r6c7, r6c9, r9c9, r8c8} (source [r3c9=r5c8]).
            Рєњ color(r7c7) = color([r3c9=r5c8]). Identify.
        4.  KРѓё on {r3c6, [r3c9=r5c8=r7c7], r5c6, r7c6}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c9) РЅа color(r5c8). Add edge.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub r6c9 forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r3c9, r5c8, r6c7, r6c3, r9c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

  6.  KРѓё on {[r3c9=r6c7], [r6c9=r9c7], r8c7, r8c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_ladder] (diamond+set+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {col 7, col 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r3c9} = {r5c8}.
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r5c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r3c9=r5c8], r3c6, r5c6, r9c6} (spine r3c6Рђћr5c6).
      Рєњ color([r3c9=r5c8]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c3, r6c7, r8c7, r8c3}:
      Diagonals: {r6c3, r8c7} and {r6c7, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r6c3) = color(r8c7) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c7, r6c9}: r6c3=X Рєњ r6c7, r6c9РЅаX
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c7, r8c9}: r8c7=X Рєњ r8c3, r8c9РЅаX
        col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r6c3=X Рєњ r7c3РЅаX
        col 7 {r6c7, r8c7, r9c7}: r8c7=X Рєњ r9c7РЅаX
        col 9 {[r3c9=r5c8=r9c6], r6c9, r8c9}: r6c9, r8c9РЅаX Рєњ [r3c9=r5c8=r9c6]=X
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3, r8c3РЅаX Рєњ r9c2=X
        [r3c9=r5c8=r9c6]=X and r9c2=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c7, r6c9}: r6c7=X Рєњ r6c3, r6c9РЅаX
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c7, r8c9}: r8c3=X Рєњ r8c7, r8c9РЅаX
        col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X Рєњ r7c3РЅаX
        col 7 {r6c7, r8c7, r9c7}: r6c7=X Рєњ r9c7РЅаX
        col 9 {[r3c9=r5c8=r9c6], r6c9, r8c9}: r6c9, r8c9РЅаX Рєњ [r3c9=r5c8=r9c6]=X
        box 6 {[r3c9=r5c8=r9c6], r6c7, r6c9}: [r3c9=r5c8=r9c6]=X and r6c7=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (set+hexagon+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r8c6} = {r7c3}.
      Рєњ color(r8c6) = color(r7c3). Identify.

      Remainder: {r6c3, [r7c3=r8c6]} = {r5c8, [r7c3=r8c6]}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Branch on r5c6, r6c7:
      Case A: color(r5c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r7c3=r8c6], r8c7, r9c9, r9c2}:
            Diagonals: {[r7c3=r8c6], r9c9} and {r8c7, r9c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r7c3=r8c6]) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 8 {[r7c3=r8c6], r8c3, r8c7}: [r7c3=r8c6]=X Рєњ r8c3, r8c7РЅаX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c8, r9c9}: r9c9=X Рєњ r9c2, r9c8РЅаX
              col 3 {r6c3, [r7c3=r8c6], r8c3}: [r7c3=r8c6]=X Рєњ r6c3РЅаX
              col 6 {r3c6, [r5c6=r6c7], [r7c3=r8c6]}: [r7c3=r8c6]=X Рєњ r3c6, [r5c6=r6c7]РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X Рєњ r3c9, r6c9РЅаX
              box 6 {[r5c6=r6c7], r5c8, r6c9}: [r5c6=r6c7], r6c9РЅаX Рєњ r5c8=X
              r5c8=X and [r7c3=r8c6]=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c7) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 8 {[r7c3=r8c6], r8c3, r8c7}: r8c7=X Рєњ [r7c3=r8c6], r8c3РЅаX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c8, r9c9}: r9c2=X Рєњ r9c8, r9c9РЅаX
              col 3 {r6c3, [r7c3=r8c6], r8c3}: [r7c3=r8c6], r8c3РЅаX Рєњ r6c3=X
              row 6 {[r5c6=r6c7], r6c3, r6c9}: r6c3=X Рєњ [r5c6=r6c7], r6c9РЅаX
              col 6 {r3c6, [r5c6=r6c7], [r7c3=r8c6]}: [r5c6=r6c7], [r7c3=r8c6]РЅаX Рєњ r3c6=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r6c9, r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r3c9=X
              box 6 {[r5c6=r6c7], r5c8, r6c9}: [r5c6=r6c7], r6c9РЅаX Рєњ r5c8=X
              r3c6=X and r3c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c6) РЅа color(r6c7). Add edge.
        5.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c6, r6c7, r6c9, r9c9, r9c2, [r7c3=r8c6]}
            Bridges: r5c8Рђћr9c8 (edges r5c6Рђћr6c7 Рєћ r9c9Рђћr9c2), r6c3Рђћr8c3 (edges r6c7Рђћr6c9 Рєћ r9c2Рђћ[r7c3=r8c6]), r3c9Рђћr3c6 (edges r6c9Рђћr9c9 Рєћ [r7c3=r8c6]Рђћr5c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

      Рєњ color(r6c7) = color(r7c9). Identify.

      Рєњ color(r6c9) = color([r7c3=r8c8]). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r6c7=r7c9], [r5c8=r6c3=r8c7], [r6c9=r7c3=r8c8], r8c3} (spine [r5c8=r6c3=r8c7]Рђћ[r6c9=r7c3=r8c8]).
      Рєњ color([r6c7=r7c9]) = color(r8c3). Identify.

      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color([r5c8=r6c3=r8c7]). Identify.

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (set+xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c3, r8c7:
      Case A: color(r6c3) = color(r8c7). Identify.
        2.  SET: {row 6, row 8}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 7}.
            Remainder: {[r6c3=r8c7], r8c8} = {r5c8, r7c3, r9c2}.
            Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c8, r9c2, r9c6}:
            Diagonals: {r5c6, r9c2} and {r5c8, r9c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r9c2) = X
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r5c6=X Рєњ r3c6, r9c6РЅаX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X Рєњ r7c3, r8c3РЅаX
              col 3 {[r6c3=r8c7], r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3, r8c3РЅаX Рєњ [r6c3=r8c7]=X
              box 9 {[r6c3=r8c7], r7c9, r8c8}: [r6c3=r8c7]=X Рєњ r7c9, r8c8РЅаX
              row 6 {[r6c3=r8c7], r6c7, r6c9}: [r6c3=r8c7]=X Рєњ r6c7, r6c9РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: r6c9, r7c9РЅаX Рєњ r3c9=X
              box 6 {r5c8, r6c7, r6c9}: r6c7, r6c9РЅаX Рєњ r5c8=X
              r5c6=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c8) = color(r9c6) = X
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X Рєњ r3c6, r5c6РЅаX
              box 6 {r5c8, r6c7, r6c9}: r5c8=X Рєњ r6c7, r6c9РЅаX
              row 6 {[r6c3=r8c7], r6c7, r6c9}: r6c7, r6c9РЅаX Рєњ [r6c3=r8c7]=X
              row 8 {[r6c3=r8c7], r8c3, r8c8}: [r6c3=r8c7]=X Рєњ r8c3, r8c8РЅаX
              col 3 {[r6c3=r8c7], r7c3, r8c3}: [r6c3=r8c7]=X Рєњ r7c3РЅаX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3, r8c3РЅаX Рєњ r9c2=X
              box 9 {[r6c3=r8c7], r7c9, r8c8}: [r6c3=r8c7]=X Рєњ r7c9РЅаX
              r5c8=X and r9c2=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c3) РЅа color(r8c7). Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: r8c8 guards oddagon {r8c8, r5c8, r6c9, r6c3, r8c7} (source r6c7).
            Рєњ color(r8c8) = color(r6c7). Identify.
        5.  KРѓё on {r6c3, [r6c7=r8c8], r8c3, r8c7}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

        Contradiction.

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_set] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r6c7, r6c9, r6c3} (spine r6c7Рђћr6c9).

  3.  Diamond {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6, [r5c8=r6c3=r9c2]} (spine r5c6Рђћr9c6).

      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c2]) = color(r8c9) = X
        row 6 {[r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c2], r6c7, r6c9}: [r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c2]=X Рєњ r6c7, r6c9РЅаX

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+ladder+xwing) ===

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_diamond] (oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c7, r5c8, r6c9} (spine r5c7Рђћr5c8).
      Рєњ color(r5c6) = color(r6c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r5c6=r6c9], r9c6} (spine r3c6Рђћ[r5c6=r6c9]).
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3Рђћr8c3).
      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c9=r9c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r5c6=r6c9], [r6c3=r9c2], r9c4, r8c5, r8c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_diamond] (set+xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c6, r6c9:
      Case A: color(r5c6) = color(r6c9). Identify.
        2.  SET: {row 5, row 8}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 9}.
            Remainder: {[r5c6=r6c9], r8c3} = {[r5c6=r6c9], r7c9}.
            Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
        3.  Guardian: r9c6 guards oddagon {r9c6, [r5c6=r6c9], r6c3, r7c3, r9c2} (source r8c3).
            Рєњ color(r9c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c7, r5c8, r8c8, r8c7}:
            Diagonals: {r5c7, r8c8} and {r5c8, r8c7} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c7) = color(r8c8) = X
              row 5 {[r5c6=r6c9], r5c7, r5c8}: r5c7=X Рєњ [r5c6=r6c9], r5c8РЅаX
              row 8 {[r8c3=r9c6], r8c7, r8c8}: r8c8=X Рєњ [r8c3=r9c6], r8c7РЅаX
              col 6 {r3c6, [r5c6=r6c9], [r8c3=r9c6]}: [r5c6=r6c9], [r8c3=r9c6]РЅаX Рєњ r3c6=X
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r8c8}: r8c8=X Рєњ r7c9РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, [r5c6=r6c9], r7c9}: [r5c6=r6c9], r7c9РЅаX Рєњ r3c9=X
              r3c6=X and r3c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c8) = color(r8c7) = X
              row 5 {[r5c6=r6c9], r5c7, r5c8}: r5c8=X Рєњ [r5c6=r6c9], r5c7РЅаX
              row 8 {[r8c3=r9c6], r8c7, r8c8}: r8c7=X Рєњ [r8c3=r9c6], r8c8РЅаX
              col 6 {r3c6, [r5c6=r6c9], [r8c3=r9c6]}: [r5c6=r6c9], [r8c3=r9c6]РЅаX Рєњ r3c6=X
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r8c8}: r8c7=X Рєњ r7c9РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, [r5c6=r6c9], r7c9}: [r5c6=r6c9], r7c9РЅаX Рєњ r3c9=X
              r3c6=X and r3c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c6) РЅа color(r6c9). Add edge.
        5.  KРѓё on {r5c6, r5c7, r5c8, r6c9}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c9, r7c9, r9c9, r8c8} (spine r7c9Рђћr9c9).
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r5c8, r9c9:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {r6c8, [r3c9=r8c8], [r5c8=r9c9], r7c9} (spine [r3c9=r8c8]Рђћ[r5c8=r9c9]).
            Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r6c3, r6c6, [r6c8=r7c9], r7c6} (spine r6c6Рђћ[r6c8=r7c9]).
            Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub r9c5 forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c5, [r5c8=r9c9], r9c3, [r6c3=r7c6], r8c5} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) РЅа color(r9c9). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c8, r6c8, r6c6, r7c6, r9c5, r9c9}
            Bridges: [r3c9=r8c8]Рђћr8c5 (edges r5c8Рђћr6c8 Рєћ r7c6Рђћr9c5), r6c3Рђћr9c3 (edges r6c8Рђћr6c6 Рєћ r9c5Рђћr9c9), r3c6Рђћ[r3c9=r8c8] (edges r6c6Рђћr7c6 Рєћ r9c9Рђћr5c8).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

              [r3c9=r6c6]=X and r5c5=X are adjacent

              r6c3=X and [r7c9=r9c3]=X are adjacent

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c6, r9c4:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r9c4). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r6c6, [r3c6=r9c4], r7c6, r8c5} (spine [r3c6=r9c4]Рђћr7c6).
            Рєњ color(r6c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {r6c3, [r6c6=r8c5], r6c8, r8c8} (spine [r6c6=r8c5]Рђћr6c8).
            Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r8c8). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub r9c9 forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r3c6=r9c4], r3c9, r7c9, [r6c3=r8c8], r9c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) РЅа color(r9c4). Add edge.
        5.  Guardian: r5c5 guards oddagon {r5c5, r6c6, r3c6, r9c4, r8c5} (source r7c6).
            Рєњ color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r3c6, r6c6, r6c8, r8c8, r9c9, r9c4}
            Bridges: [r5c5=r7c6]Рђћr7c9 (edges r3c6Рђћr6c6 Рєћ r8c8Рђћr9c9), r6c3Рђћr9c3 (edges r6c6Рђћr6c8 Рєћ r9c9Рђћr9c4), r5c8Рђћ[r5c5=r7c6] (edges r6c8Рђћr8c8 Рєћ r9c4Рђћr3c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

        3.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c9, r6c7, r6c6, r9c6, r8c5, r8c9}

            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_set] (diamond+xwing) ===

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+ladder+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c2, r7c2, r8c2, r9c1} (spine r7c2Рђћr8c2).
      Рєњ color(r6c2) = color(r9c1). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c6, [r6c2=r9c1], r6c8, r9c8} (spine [r6c2=r9c1]Рђћr6c8).
      Рєњ color(r6c6) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  3.  Circular ladder {r3c6Рђћr3c9, r5c6Рђћr5c9, [r6c6=r9c8]Рђћr8c9}:
      Satellites r5c3 (rung 2), r9c7 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c9, r8c9, r9c7}:
      Diagonals: {r5c3, r8c9} and {r5c9, r9c7} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r8c9) = X
        row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3=X Рєњ r5c6, r5c9РЅаX
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r8c9=X Рєњ r3c9РЅаX
        box 9 {[r6c6=r9c8], r8c9, r9c7}: r8c9=X Рєњ [r6c6=r9c8], r9c7РЅаX
        row 9 {[r6c2=r9c1], [r6c6=r9c8], r9c7}: [r6c6=r9c8], r9c7РЅаX Рєњ [r6c2=r9c1]=X
        col 2 {[r6c2=r9c1], r7c2, r8c2}: [r6c2=r9c1]=X Рєњ r7c2, r8c2РЅаX
        col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, [r6c6=r9c8]}: r5c6, [r6c6=r9c8]РЅаX Рєњ r3c6=X
        r5c3=X and [r6c2=r9c1]=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r9c7) = X
        row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X Рєњ r5c3, r5c6РЅаX
        row 9 {[r6c2=r9c1], [r6c6=r9c8], r9c7}: r9c7=X Рєњ [r6c2=r9c1], [r6c6=r9c8]РЅаX
        col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, [r6c6=r9c8]}: r5c6, [r6c6=r9c8]РЅаX Рєњ r3c6=X
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r5c9=X Рєњ r3c9, r8c9РЅаX
        row 6 {[r6c2=r9c1], [r6c6=r9c8], r6c8}: [r6c2=r9c1], [r6c6=r9c8]РЅаX Рєњ r6c8=X
        r5c9=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 4/4 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+ladder+xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r6c3Рђћr9c3, r6c5Рђћr9c5, r6c9Рђћr9c9}:
      Satellites r5c3 (rung 1), r3c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Branch on r3c9, r5c8:
      Case A: color(r3c9) = color(r5c8). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {[r3c9=r5c8], r3c6, r5c6, r8c6} (spine r3c6Рђћr5c6).
            Рєњ color([r3c9=r5c8]) = color(r8c6). Identify.
        4.  KРѓё on {[r3c9=r5c8=r8c6], r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c9) РЅа color(r5c8). Add edge.
        5.  Guardian: r9c3 guards oddagon {r9c3, r5c3, r5c8, r6c9, r9c9} (source r3c9).
            Рєњ color(r9c3) = color(r3c9). Identify.
        6.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c8, r6c9, r6c5}:
            Diagonals: {r5c6, r6c9} and {r5c8, r6c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r6c9) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c6=X Рєњ r5c3, r5c8РЅаX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c9}: r6c9=X Рєњ r6c3, r6c5РЅаX
              col 3 {[r3c9=r9c3], r5c3, r6c3}: r5c3, r6c3РЅаX Рєњ [r3c9=r9c3]=X
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r8c6}: r5c6=X Рєњ r3c6, r8c6РЅаX
              col 9 {[r3c9=r9c3], r6c9, r9c9}: [r3c9=r9c3]=X and r6c9=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c8) = color(r6c5) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c8=X Рєњ r5c3, r5c6РЅаX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c9}: r6c5=X Рєњ r6c3, r6c9РЅаX
              col 3 {[r3c9=r9c3], r5c3, r6c3}: r5c3, r6c3РЅаX Рєњ [r3c9=r9c3]=X
              col 9 {[r3c9=r9c3], r6c9, r9c9}: [r3c9=r9c3]=X Рєњ r9c9РЅаX
              [r3c9=r9c3]=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

        col 3 {[r3c6=r6c3=r7c5], r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3, r9c3РЅаX Рєњ [r3c6=r6c3=r7c5]=X
        col 6 {[r3c6=r6c3=r7c5], r8c6, r9c6}: [r3c6=r6c3=r7c5]=X and r9c6=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c9, r4c9, r5c9, r6c8} (spine r4c9Рђћr5c9).
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r6c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c6, r8c6, r9c6, r7c5} (spine r8c6Рђћr9c6).
      Рєњ color(r3c6) = color(r7c5). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r3c6=r7c5], [r3c9=r6c8], r6c5, r6c3} (spine [r3c9=r6c8]Рђћr6c5).
      Рєњ color([r3c6=r7c5]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r8c6, [r3c6=r6c3=r7c5], r8c3, r9c3} (spine [r3c6=r6c3=r7c5]Рђћr8c3).
      Рєњ color(r8c6) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r8c3, [r3c6=r6c3=r7c5], [r8c6=r9c3], r9c6} (spine [r3c6=r6c3=r7c5]Рђћ[r8c6=r9c3]).
      Рєњ color(r8c3) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  6.  KРѓё on {[r8c3=r9c6], [r8c6=r9c3], r8c7, r9c7}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder) ===

      Рєњ color(r3c6) = color(r7c5). Identify.
  3.  Circular ladder {r8c3Рђћr9c3, r8c6Рђћr9c6, r8c7Рђћr9c7}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), [r3c6=r7c5] (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  4.  KРѓё on {[r3c6=r7c5], [r3c9=r6c8], r6c3, r6c5}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_diamond] (set+ladder+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3Рђћr9c3, r8c6Рђћr9c6, r8c7Рђћr9c7}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), r3c6 (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Branch on r3c6, r7c5:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r7c5). Identify.
        3.  SET: {col 6, col 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 8}.
            Remainder: {[r3c6=r7c5], r3c9} = {[r3c6=r7c5], r6c8}.
            Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
        4.  KРѓё on {[r3c6=r7c5], r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) РЅа color(r7c5). Add edge.
        5.  KРѓё on {r3c6, r7c5, r8c6, r9c6}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r4c9) = X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r3c6=X Рєњ r6c6, r8c6РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r9c9}: r4c9=X Рєњ r3c9, r9c9РЅаX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r4c9=X Рєњ r5c8, r6c8РЅаX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6, r6c8РЅаX Рєњ r6c3=X
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r7c8}: r5c8, r6c8РЅаX Рєњ r7c8=X
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c8=X Рєњ r8c7РЅаX
              row 8 {r8c5, r8c6, r8c7}: r8c6, r8c7РЅаX Рєњ r8c5=X
              box 8 {r8c5, r8c6, r9c4}: r8c5=X Рєњ r9c4РЅаX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c4, r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r9c3=X
              r6c3=X and r9c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c8=X Рєњ r6c3, r6c6РЅаX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r7c8}: r6c8=X Рєњ r5c8, r7c8РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r9c9}: r3c9=X Рєњ r4c9, r9c9РЅаX
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c8, r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r8c7=X
              row 8 {r8c5, r8c6, r8c7}: r8c7=X Рєњ r8c5, r8c6РЅаX
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r6c6, r8c6РЅаX Рєњ r3c6=X
              box 8 {r8c5, r8c6, r9c4}: r8c5, r8c6РЅаX Рєњ r9c4=X
              r3c6=X and r3c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 8, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c3} = {r7c8}.
      Рєњ color(r9c3) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c6, r6c3, r6c8, [r7c8=r9c3]} (spine r6c3Рђћr6c8).
      Рєњ color(r6c6) = color([r7c8=r9c3]). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c6, [r6c6=r7c8=r9c3], r8c6, r8c7} (spine [r6c6=r7c8=r9c3]Рђћr8c6).
      Рєњ color(r3c6) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  4.  SET: {col 8, col 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r3c9} = {[r3c6=r8c7]}.
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color([r3c6=r8c7]), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+set+xwing) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3, r9c2} (spine r8c3Рђћr9c3).
      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c6, [r6c3=r9c2], r6c8, r9c8} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]Рђћr6c8).

      Remainder: {[r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r9c8]} = {r4c9, r5c8, r8c3}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c9, r6c8, [r6c3=r9c2], r8c3}:
      Diagonals: {r4c9, [r6c3=r9c2]} and {r6c8, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r4c9) = color([r6c3=r9c2]) = X
        row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r9c8], r6c8}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X Рєњ [r6c6=r9c8], r6c8РЅаX

        Contradiction.

  1.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3, r9c8} (spine r9c2Рђћr9c3).
      Рєњ color(r8c3) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r7c9, [r8c3=r9c8], r8c7, r8c6} (spine [r8c3=r9c8]Рђћr8c7).
      Рєњ color(r7c9) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  3.  Guardian: r6c7 guards oddagon {r6c7, r4c9, r3c9, r3c6, r6c6} (source [r7c9=r8c6]).
      Рєњ color(r6c7) = color([r7c9=r8c6]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c7=r7c9=r8c6] forces rim to 2 colors.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+set+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r8c6} = {r7c9}.
      Рєњ color(r8c6) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r7c9=r8c6], r6c6} (spine r3c6Рђћ[r7c9=r8c6]).
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r4c9, [r3c9=r6c6], r6c7, r6c3} (spine [r3c9=r6c6]Рђћr6c7).
      Рєњ color(r4c9) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r7c9=r8c6], r8c3, r9c2, r9c8}:
      Diagonals: {[r7c9=r8c6], r9c2} and {r8c3, r9c8} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r7c9=r8c6]) = color(r9c2) = X
        row 8 {[r7c9=r8c6], r8c3, r8c7}: [r7c9=r8c6]=X Рєњ r8c3, r8c7РЅаX
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c8}: r9c2=X Рєњ r9c3, r9c8РЅаX
        col 3 {[r4c9=r6c3], r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3, r9c3РЅаX Рєњ [r4c9=r6c3]=X
        col 6 {r3c6, [r3c9=r6c6], [r7c9=r8c6]}: [r7c9=r8c6]=X Рєњ r3c6, [r3c9=r6c6]РЅаX
        col 9 {[r3c9=r6c6], [r4c9=r6c3], [r7c9=r8c6]}: [r4c9=r6c3]=X and [r7c9=r8c6]=X clash
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c3) = color(r9c8) = X
        row 8 {[r7c9=r8c6], r8c3, r8c7}: r8c3=X Рєњ [r7c9=r8c6], r8c7РЅаX
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c8}: r9c8=X Рєњ r9c2, r9c3РЅаX
        col 3 {[r4c9=r6c3], r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X Рєњ [r4c9=r6c3]РЅаX
        col 9 {[r3c9=r6c6], [r4c9=r6c3], [r7c9=r8c6]}: [r4c9=r6c3], [r7c9=r8c6]РЅаX Рєњ [r3c9=r6c6]=X
        row 6 {[r3c9=r6c6], [r4c9=r6c3], r6c7}: [r3c9=r6c6]=X Рєњ r6c7РЅаX
        col 6 {r3c6, [r3c9=r6c6], [r7c9=r8c6]}: [r3c9=r6c6]=X Рєњ r3c6РЅаX
        box 6 {[r4c9=r6c3], r5c8, r6c7}: [r4c9=r6c3], r6c7РЅаX Рєњ r5c8=X

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (set+hexagon+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 8, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c3} = {r7c8}.
      Рєњ color(r9c3) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  2.  Branch on [r7c8=r9c3], r8c4:
      Case A: color([r7c8=r9c3]) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        3.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r6c7, r6c6, r9c6, r8c5, r8c9}
            Bridges: r5c8Рђћ[r7c8=r8c4=r9c3] (edges r4c9Рђћr6c7 Рєћ r9c6Рђћr8c5), r6c3Рђћ[r7c8=r8c4=r9c3] (edges r6c7Рђћr6c6 Рєћ r8c5Рђћr8c9), r3c6Рђћr3c9 (edges r6c6Рђћr9c6 Рєћ r8c9Рђћr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r7c8=r9c3]) РЅа color(r8c4). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r6c7, r6c6, r9c6, r8c4, r8c9}
            Bridges: r5c8Рђћ[r7c8=r9c3] (edges r4c9Рђћr6c7 Рєћ r9c6Рђћr8c4), r6c3Рђћ[r7c8=r9c3] (edges r6c7Рђћr6c6 Рєћ r8c4Рђћr8c9), r3c6Рђћr3c9 (edges r6c6Рђћr9c6 Рєћ r8c9Рђћr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (set+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {col 8, col 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r3c9} = {r8c7}.
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  2.  Branch on [r3c9=r8c7], r5c6:
      Case A: color([r3c9=r8c7]) = color(r5c6). Identify.
        3.  SET: {col 3, col 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 7}.
            Remainder: {[r3c9=r5c6=r8c7], r6c3, r9c9} = {r5c8, r6c8, r9c2}.
            Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
        4.  KРѓё on {r5c8, r6c8, r9c2, r9c8}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r3c9=r8c7]) РЅа color(r5c6). Add edge.
        5.  KРѓё on {r3c6, [r3c9=r8c7], r5c6, r8c6}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {col 7, col 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r3c9} = {r5c8}.
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r5c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r3c9=r5c8], r3c6, r5c6, r9c6} (spine r3c6Рђћr5c6).
      Рєњ color([r3c9=r5c8]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c9=r5c8=r9c6], r8c9, r8c3, r9c2}:
      Diagonals: {[r3c9=r5c8=r9c6], r8c3} and {r8c9, r9c2} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r3c9=r5c8=r9c6]) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c7, r8c9}: r8c3=X Рєњ r8c7, r8c9РЅаX
        row 9 {[r3c9=r5c8=r9c6], r9c2, r9c7}: [r3c9=r5c8=r9c6]=X Рєњ r9c2, r9c7РЅаX

        col 7 {r6c7, r8c7, r9c7}: r8c7, r9c7РЅаX Рєњ r6c7=X

        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c9) = color(r9c2) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c7, r8c9}: r8c9=X Рєњ r8c3, r8c7РЅаX

        r6c3=X and r6c7=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

        2.  Guardian: r5c6 guards oddagon {r5c6, r6c5, r6c7, r9c7, r9c6} (source [r6c3=r9c2]).
            Рєњ color(r5c6) = color([r6c3=r9c2]). Identify.

            Рєњ color(r8c9) = color(r6c7). Identify.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c9, [r6c7=r8c9], [r5c6=r6c3=r9c2]}:
            Diagonals: {r3c6, [r6c7=r8c9]} and {r3c9, [r5c6=r6c3=r9c2]} (non-adjacent).

              [r6c7=r8c9]=X and r9c7=X are adjacent

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (set+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Guardian: r6c9 guards oddagon {r6c9, r6c3, r8c3, r9c2, r9c9} (source r9c3).
      Рєњ color(r6c9) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 6, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{col 3, col 9}.
      Remainder: {r6c6, r9c2} = {r3c9, r8c3}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c9, [r6c9=r9c3], r6c6}:
      Diagonals: {r3c6, [r6c9=r9c3]} and {r3c9, r6c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c6) = color([r6c9=r9c3]) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, [r6c9=r9c3]}: [r6c9=r9c3]=X Рєњ r6c3, r6c6РЅаX
        row 9 {[r6c9=r9c3], r9c2, r9c9}: [r6c9=r9c3]=X Рєњ r9c2, r9c9РЅаX
        col 3 {r6c3, [r6c9=r9c3], r8c3}: [r6c9=r9c3]=X Рєњ r8c3РЅаX
        col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r3c6=X Рєњ r8c6РЅаX
        col 9 {r3c9, [r6c9=r9c3], r9c9}: [r6c9=r9c3]=X Рєњ r3c9РЅаX
        box 6 {r4c8, r5c8, [r6c9=r9c3]}: [r6c9=r9c3]=X Рєњ r4c8, r5c8РЅаX
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c7}: r8c3, r8c6РЅаX Рєњ r8c7=X
        col 8 {r4c8, r5c8, r7c8}: r4c8, r5c8РЅаX Рєњ r7c8=X
        box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c8=X and r8c7=X clash
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r6c6) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, [r6c9=r9c3]}: r6c6=X Рєњ r6c3, [r6c9=r9c3]РЅаX
        col 3 {r6c3, [r6c9=r9c3], r8c3}: r6c3, [r6c9=r9c3]РЅаX Рєњ r8c3=X
        col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r6c6=X Рєњ r3c6, r8c6РЅаX
        col 9 {r3c9, [r6c9=r9c3], r9c9}: r3c9=X Рєњ r9c9РЅаX
        box 7 {[r6c9=r9c3], r8c3, r9c2}: r8c3=X Рєњ r9c2РЅаX
        r3c9=X and r8c3=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_diamond] (set+branch+guardian) ===

        3.  Guardian: r8c3 guards oddagon {r8c3, r6c3, r6c9, r9c9, r8c8} (source [r3c9=r6c6=r7c7]).

        5.  KРѓё on {r3c6, [r3c9=r7c7], r6c6, r7c6}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c9, r4c8, r5c8, r7c8} (spine r4c8Рђћr5c8).
      Рєњ color(r6c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c6, r6c3, [r6c9=r7c8], r7c3} (spine r6c3Рђћ[r6c9=r7c8]).
      Рєњ color(r6c6) = color(r7c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c6, [r6c6=r7c3], r9c6, r9c2} (spine [r6c6=r7c3]Рђћr9c6).
      Рєњ color(r3c6) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c6=r9c2], r8c2, r8c7, r9c9}:
      Diagonals: {[r3c6=r9c2], r8c7} and {r8c2, r9c9} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r3c6=r9c2]) = color(r8c7) = X
        row 9 {[r3c6=r9c2], r9c6, r9c9}: [r3c6=r9c2]=X Рєњ r9c6, r9c9РЅаX
        col 6 {[r3c6=r9c2], [r6c6=r7c3], r9c6}: [r3c6=r9c2]=X Рєњ [r6c6=r7c3]РЅаX
        box 7 {[r3c6=r9c2], [r6c6=r7c3], r8c2}: [r3c6=r9c2]=X Рєњ r8c2РЅаX
        box 9 {[r6c9=r7c8], r8c7, r9c9}: r8c7=X Рєњ [r6c9=r7c8]РЅаX
        row 6 {r6c3, [r6c6=r7c3], [r6c9=r7c8]}: [r6c6=r7c3], [r6c9=r7c8]РЅаX Рєњ r6c3=X
        col 9 {r3c9, [r6c9=r7c8], r9c9}: [r6c9=r7c8], r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r3c9=X
        [r3c6=r9c2]=X and r3c9=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c2) = color(r9c9) = X
        row 9 {[r3c6=r9c2], r9c6, r9c9}: r9c9=X Рєњ [r3c6=r9c2], r9c6РЅаX

        box 7 {[r3c6=r9c2], [r6c6=r7c3], r8c2}: [r6c6=r7c3]=X and r8c2=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_oddwheel] (set+guardian) ===

      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r8c6). Identify.

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+set+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {col 8, col 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r3c9} = {r5c7}.
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r5c7). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r3c9=r5c7], r3c6, r5c6, r8c6} (spine r3c6Рђћr5c6).
      Рєњ color([r3c9=r5c7]) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r3c9=r5c7=r8c6], r4c8, r8c8, r7c8} (spine r4c8Рђћr8c8).
      Рєњ color([r3c9=r5c7=r8c6]) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c9=r5c7=r7c8=r8c6], r6c9, r6c3, r8c3}:
      Diagonals: {[r3c9=r5c7=r7c8=r8c6], r6c3} and {r6c9, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r3c9=r5c7=r7c8=r8c6]) = color(r6c3) = X
        row 8 {[r3c9=r5c7=r7c8=r8c6], r8c3, r8c8}: [r3c9=r5c7=r7c8=r8c6]=X Рєњ r8c3, r8c8РЅаX
        col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r6c3=X Рєњ r9c3РЅаX
        col 6 {r3c6, [r3c9=r5c7=r7c8=r8c6], r5c6}: [r3c9=r5c7=r7c8=r8c6]=X Рєњ r3c6, r5c6РЅаX
        col 8 {[r3c9=r5c7=r7c8=r8c6], r4c8, r8c8}: [r3c9=r5c7=r7c8=r8c6]=X Рєњ r4c8РЅаX
        col 9 {[r3c9=r5c7=r7c8=r8c6], r6c9, r9c9}: [r3c9=r5c7=r7c8=r8c6]=X Рєњ r6c9, r9c9РЅаX
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c3, r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r9c5=X
        [r3c9=r5c7=r7c8=r8c6]=X and r9c5=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c9) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 8 {[r3c9=r5c7=r7c8=r8c6], r8c3, r8c8}: r8c3=X Рєњ [r3c9=r5c7=r7c8=r8c6], r8c8РЅаX
        col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X Рєњ r6c3, r9c3РЅаX
        col 8 {[r3c9=r5c7=r7c8=r8c6], r4c8, r8c8}: [r3c9=r5c7=r7c8=r8c6], r8c8РЅаX Рєњ r4c8=X
        col 9 {[r3c9=r5c7=r7c8=r8c6], r6c9, r9c9}: r6c9=X Рєњ r9c9РЅаX
        box 6 {[r3c9=r5c7=r7c8=r8c6], r4c8, r6c9}: r4c8=X and r6c9=X clash

  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r6c9=r8c8], r8c3, r9c2, r9c8}:
      Diagonals: {[r6c9=r8c8], r9c2} and {r8c3, r9c8} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r6c9=r8c8]) = color(r9c2) = X
        row 8 {[r3c9=r5c7=r8c6], [r6c9=r8c8], r8c3}: [r6c9=r8c8]=X Рєњ [r3c9=r5c7=r8c6], r8c3РЅаX
        col 8 {r4c8, [r6c9=r8c8], r9c8}: [r6c9=r8c8]=X Рєњ r4c8, r9c8РЅаX
        col 9 {[r3c9=r5c7=r8c6], [r6c9=r8c8], r7c9}: [r6c9=r8c8]=X Рєњ r7c9РЅаX
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X Рєњ r7c3РЅаX
        col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3, r8c3РЅаX Рєњ r6c3=X
        r6c3=X and [r6c9=r8c8]=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c3) = color(r9c8) = X
        row 8 {[r3c9=r5c7=r8c6], [r6c9=r8c8], r8c3}: r8c3=X Рєњ [r3c9=r5c7=r8c6], [r6c9=r8c8]РЅаX
        col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X Рєњ r6c3, r7c3РЅаX
        col 8 {r4c8, [r6c9=r8c8], r9c8}: r9c8=X Рєњ r4c8РЅаX
        col 9 {[r3c9=r5c7=r8c6], [r6c9=r8c8], r7c9}: [r3c9=r5c7=r8c6], [r6c9=r8c8]РЅаX Рєњ r7c9=X
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r8c3=X Рєњ r9c2РЅаX
        box 9 {[r6c9=r8c8], r7c9, r9c8}: r7c9=X and r9c8=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3Рђћr8c3).
      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c8, r8c8, r9c8, r7c9} (spine r8c8Рђћr9c8).
      Рєњ color(r4c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.

  4.  Diamond {[r3c9=r5c7], r3c6, r5c6, r8c6} (spine r3c6Рђћr5c6).
      Рєњ color([r3c9=r5c7]) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r6c9, [r3c9=r5c7=r8c6], [r4c8=r7c9], r8c8} (spine [r3c9=r5c7=r8c6]Рђћ[r4c8=r7c9]).
      Рєњ color(r6c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.

      Рєњ color([r4c8=r7c9]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  7.  KРѓё on {[r4c8=r7c9=r8c3], [r6c3=r9c2], [r6c9=r8c8], r9c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

      Рєњ color(r4c8) = color(r8c3). Identify.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_diamond] (set+oddwheel+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {col 8, col 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r3c9} = {r5c7}.
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r5c7). Identify.
  2.  Branch on [r3c9=r5c7], r8c6:
      Case A: color([r3c9=r5c7]) = color(r8c6). Identify.
        3.  SET: {col 3, col 8}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 7}.
            Remainder: {r6c3, r8c8, r9c8} = {[r3c9=r5c7=r8c6], r6c9, r9c2}.
            Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c9=r5c7=r8c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r4c8, r6c9, r9c2, r8c3, r8c8} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r3c9=r5c7]) РЅа color(r8c6). Add edge.
        5.  KРѓё on {r3c6, [r3c9=r5c7], r5c6, r8c6}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

        r3c6=X and r7c6=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 4/4 [no_guardian] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3Рђћr9c3, r8c5Рђћr9c4, r8c8Рђћr9c7}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2), r7c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Diamond {r6c9, r6c3, r7c9, r7c6} (spine r6c3Рђћr7c9).
      Рєњ color(r6c9) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c8, r3c6, r3c9, [r6c9=r7c6]} (spine r3c6Рђћr3c9).
      Рєњ color(r3c8) = color([r6c9=r7c6]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c8=r6c9=r7c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r6c3, r6c5, r8c5, r8c8, r7c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (set+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 7, box 8}.
      Remainder: {r8c8} = {r7c6}.
      Рєњ color(r8c8) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  SET: {col 5, col 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 8}.
      Remainder: {r3c9, r6c5} = {r6c7, [r7c6=r8c8]}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Branch on r3c9, r6c7:
      Case A: color(r3c9) = color(r6c7). Identify.
        4.  KРѓё on {r3c6, r3c8, [r3c9=r6c7], [r7c6=r8c8]}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c9) РЅа color(r6c7). Add edge.
        5.  KРѓё on {r3c9, r5c9, r6c7, r6c9}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

              r3c6=X and r8c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c9) РЅа color(r6c8). Add edge.
        3.  Guardian: r9c9 guards oddagon {r9c9, r5c9, r3c9, r3c8, r7c8} (source r6c8).
            Рєњ color(r9c9) = color(r6c8). Identify.

              r3c6=X and r8c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_set] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:

  1.  Circular ladder {r3c8Рђћr3c9, r6c8Рђћr5c9, r7c8Рђћr9c9}:

  2.  Diamond {r6c7, r3c6, r8c7, r8c6} (spine r3c6Рђћr8c7).
      Рєњ color(r6c7) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  3.  Guardian: r9c9 guards oddagon {r9c9, r5c9, r6c8, r6c5, r9c5} (source [r6c7=r8c6]).
      Рєњ color(r9c9) = color([r6c7=r8c6]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c7=r8c6=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r6c5, r6c8, r7c8, r8c7, r8c3, r9c2, r9c5} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

              r3c6=X and r7c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c8) = color(r8c5) = X
              row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c8=X Рєњ r3c6, r3c9РЅаX
              row 8 {[r8c3=r9c8], r8c5, r8c9}: r8c5=X Рєњ [r8c3=r9c8], r8c9РЅаX
              col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, [r8c3=r9c8]}: r3c8=X Рєњ r6c8РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r3c9, r8c9РЅаX Рєњ r5c9=X
              box 6 {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8}: r5c9=X Рєњ r6c7РЅаX
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r8c5=X Рєњ r7c6, r9c4РЅаX
              row 6 {r6c5, r6c7, r6c8}: r6c7, r6c8РЅаX Рєњ r6c5=X
              row 9 {[r8c3=r9c8], r9c2, r9c4}: [r8c3=r9c8], r9c4РЅаX Рєњ r9c2=X
              r6c5=X and r8c5=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3, r9c5} (spine r9c2Рђћr9c3).
      Рєњ color(r8c3) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, [r8c3=r9c5], r8c5, r8c9} (spine [r8c3=r9c5]Рђћr8c5).
      Рєњ color(r6c5) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c9, [r6c5=r8c9], r6c8, r7c8} (spine [r6c5=r8c9]Рђћr6c8).
      Рєњ color(r5c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c9, [r6c5=r8c9], r6c6}:
      Diagonals: {r3c6, [r6c5=r8c9]} and {r3c9, r6c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c6) = color([r6c5=r8c9]) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c6=X Рєњ r3c8, r3c9РЅаX
        row 6 {[r6c5=r8c9], r6c6, r6c8}: [r6c5=r8c9]=X Рєњ r6c6, r6c8РЅаX
        row 8 {[r6c5=r8c9], [r8c3=r9c5], r8c5}: [r6c5=r8c9]=X Рєњ [r8c3=r9c5], r8c5РЅаX
        col 8 {r3c8, [r5c9=r7c8], r6c8}: r3c8, r6c8РЅаX Рєњ [r5c9=r7c8]=X
        col 9 {r3c9, [r5c9=r7c8], [r6c5=r8c9]}: [r5c9=r7c8]=X and [r6c5=r8c9]=X clash
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r6c6) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c9=X Рєњ r3c6, r3c8РЅаX
        row 6 {[r6c5=r8c9], r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6=X Рєњ [r6c5=r8c9], r6c8РЅаX
        col 8 {r3c8, [r5c9=r7c8], r6c8}: r3c8, r6c8РЅаX Рєњ [r5c9=r7c8]=X
        col 9 {r3c9, [r5c9=r7c8], [r6c5=r8c9]}: r3c9=X and [r5c9=r7c8]=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

            Case 1: color(r8c3) = color(r9c4) = X
              row 8 {[r3c8=r8c9], r8c3, r8c5}: r8c3=X Рєњ [r3c8=r8c9], r8c5РЅаX

              r5c9=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c5) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 8 {[r3c8=r8c9], r8c3, r8c5}: r8c5=X Рєњ [r3c8=r8c9], r8c3РЅаX

            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r7c6, r8c3:
      Case A: color(r7c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r6c8, r6c3, r6c6, [r7c6=r8c3]} (spine r6c3Рђћr6c6).
            Рєњ color(r6c8) = color([r7c6=r8c3]). Identify.
        3.  KРѓё on {[r6c8=r7c6=r8c3], r9c2, r9c4, r9c8}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r7c6) РЅа color(r8c3). Add edge.
        4.  Diamond {r8c3, r7c6, r8c5, r9c4} (spine r7c6Рђћr8c5).
            Рєњ color(r8c3) = color(r9c4). Identify.
        5.  Diamond {r7c6, [r8c3=r9c4], r8c5, r8c9} (spine [r8c3=r9c4]Рђћr8c5).
            Рєњ color(r7c6) = color(r8c9). Identify.
        6.  Diamond {r3c8, r3c6, r3c9, [r7c6=r8c9]} (spine r3c6Рђћr3c9).
            Рєњ color(r3c8) = color([r7c6=r8c9]). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r3c8=r7c6=r8c9], r6c6} (spine r3c6Рђћ[r3c8=r7c6=r8c9]).
            Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        8.  KРѓё on {[r3c8=r7c6=r8c9], [r3c9=r6c6], r5c9, r6c8}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c8, r5c9:
      Case A: color(r3c8) = color(r5c9). Identify.
        2.  Bridged hexagon: ring {[r3c8=r5c9], r6c8, r6c6, r7c6, r8c5, r8c9}
            Bridges: r9c8Рђћr9c4 (edges [r3c8=r5c9]Рђћr6c8 Рєћ r7c6Рђћr8c5), r6c3Рђћr8c3 (edges r6c8Рђћr6c6 Рєћ r8c5Рђћr8c9), r3c6Рђћr3c9 (edges r6c6Рђћr7c6 Рєћ r8c9Рђћ[r3c8=r5c9]).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c8) РЅа color(r5c9). Add edge.
        3.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c8, r5c9, r6c8} (spine r3c8Рђћr5c9).
            Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r6c8). Identify.
        4.  Circular ladder {r8c3Рђћr9c2, r8c5Рђћr9c4, r8c9Рђћr9c8}:
            Satellites r7c6 (rung 2), [r3c9=r6c8] (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        5.  KРѓё on {r3c6, [r3c9=r6c8], r6c6, r7c6}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c8, r9c3:
      Case A: color(r6c8) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9, [r6c8=r9c3]} (spine r5c9Рђћr9c9).

            Рєњ color([r3c9=r6c8=r9c3]) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c9=r6c8=r7c6=r9c3] forces rim to 2 colors.

      Case B: color(r6c8) РЅа color(r9c3). Add edge.
        5.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r3c6, r3c9, r9c9, r9c3, r6c3, r6c6}

            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (xwing+guardian) ===

  1.  Guardian: r6c8 guards oddagon {r6c8, r6c3, r8c3, r9c2, r9c8} (source r9c3).

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_diamond] (ladder+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r3c8Рђћr3c9, r6c8Рђћr5c9, r8c8Рђћr7c9}:
      Satellites r3c6 (rung 1), r9c7 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.

  3.  Guardian: r3c9 guards oddagon {r3c9, r3c6, r6c6, r6c8, r5c9} (source r9c6).
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r9c6). Identify.

      Рєњ color(r8c2) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  5.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c9=r9c6], r6c8, r8c8, [r8c2=r9c7]}:
      Diagonals: {[r3c9=r9c6], r8c8} and {r6c8, [r8c2=r9c7]} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r3c9=r9c6]) = color(r8c8) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, [r3c9=r9c6]}: [r3c9=r9c6]=X Рєњ r3c6, r3c8РЅаX
        row 9 {[r3c9=r9c6], [r8c2=r9c7], r9c1}: [r3c9=r9c6]=X Рєњ [r8c2=r9c7], r9c1РЅаX

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon) ===

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

              r6c5=X and r9c5=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c6) = color(r9c3) = X

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.
