=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+ladder+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3Рђћr9c3, r8c6Рђћr9c5, r8c9Рђћr9c7}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), r7c8 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, r6c3, r6c8, r7c8} (spine r6c3Рђћr6c8).
      Рєњ color(r6c5) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c8, [r6c5=r7c8], r6c8, r6c3} (spine [r6c5=r7c8]Рђћr6c8).
      Рєњ color(r3c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.

      Рєњ color(r8c3) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  5.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, [r3c8=r6c3], [r8c3=r9c7], r8c6}:
      Diagonals: {r3c6, [r8c3=r9c7]} and {[r3c8=r6c3], r8c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c6) = color([r8c3=r9c7]) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, [r3c8=r6c3], r3c9}: r3c6=X Рєњ [r3c8=r6c3], r3c9РЅаX

        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r3c9, r8c9РЅаX Рєњ r5c9=X
        box 9 {[r6c5=r7c8], [r8c3=r9c7], r8c9}: [r8c3=r9c7]=X Рєњ [r6c5=r7c8]РЅаX
        row 6 {[r3c8=r6c3], [r6c5=r7c8], r6c8}: [r3c8=r6c3], [r6c5=r7c8]РЅаX Рєњ r6c8=X
        r5c9=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color([r3c8=r6c3]) = color(r8c6) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, [r3c8=r6c3], r3c9}: [r3c8=r6c3]=X Рєњ r3c6, r3c9РЅаX
        row 6 {[r3c8=r6c3], [r6c5=r7c8], r6c8}: [r3c8=r6c3]=X Рєњ [r6c5=r7c8], r6c8РЅаX
        row 8 {[r8c3=r9c7], r8c6, r8c9}: r8c6=X Рєњ [r8c3=r9c7], r8c9РЅаX
        col 3 {[r3c8=r6c3], [r8c3=r9c7], r9c3}: [r3c8=r6c3]=X Рєњ r9c3РЅаX
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r3c9, r8c9РЅаX Рєњ r5c9=X

      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r8c9). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c6=r8c9], r8c3, r9c2, r9c8}:
            Diagonals: {[r3c6=r8c9], r9c2} and {r8c3, r9c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.

              [r3c6=r8c9]=X and r7c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.

              r6c5=X and r6c7=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) РЅа color(r8c9). Add edge.
        3.  Guardian: r5c8 guards oddagon {r5c8, r3c8, r3c6, r8c9, r5c9} (source r3c9).
            Рєњ color(r5c8) = color(r3c9). Identify.

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_ladder] (diamond+xwing+branch) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c8, r5c9:

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c5, r8c5, r9c5, r7c6} (spine r8c5Рђћr9c5).
      Рєњ color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r3c8Рђћr3c9, r5c8Рђћr5c9, r9c8Рђћr8c9}:
      Satellites r3c6 (rung 1), r6c7 (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Circular ladder {r3c6Рђћr6c7, r3c8Рђћr5c8, r3c9Рђћr5c9}:
      Satellites [r6c5=r7c6] (rung 1), r9c8 (rung 2), r8c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  4.  Diamond {r8c9, [r6c5=r7c6], r8c5, r9c5} (spine [r6c5=r7c6]Рђћr8c5).
      Рєњ color(r8c9) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  5.  Odd wheel: hub [r8c9=r9c5] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r6c5=r7c6], r8c5, r8c3, r9c2, r9c8} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

        col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3, r8c3РЅаX Рєњ r6c3=X
        r6c3=X and r6c6=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3Рђћr8c3).
      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r6c3=r9c2], r9c8, r9c9, r8c7} (spine r9c8Рђћr9c9).
      Рєњ color([r6c3=r9c2]) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r7c3, [r6c3=r8c7=r9c2], r8c3, r8c6} (spine [r6c3=r8c7=r9c2]Рђћr8c3).
      Рєњ color(r7c3) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r6c6, [r6c3=r8c7=r9c2], [r7c3=r8c6], r8c3} (spine [r6c3=r8c7=r9c2]Рђћ[r7c3=r8c6]).
      Рєњ color(r6c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r3c6, [r6c6=r8c3], [r7c3=r8c6], [r6c3=r8c7=r9c2]} (spine [r6c6=r8c3]Рђћ[r7c3=r8c6]).
      Рєњ color(r3c6) = color([r6c3=r8c7=r9c2]). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {r3c9, [r3c6=r6c3=r8c7=r9c2], r3c8, r9c8} (spine [r3c6=r6c3=r8c7=r9c2]Рђћr3c8).
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {r3c8, [r3c6=r6c3=r8c7=r9c2], [r3c9=r9c8], r9c9} (spine [r3c6=r6c3=r8c7=r9c2]Рђћ[r3c9=r9c8]).
      Рєњ color(r3c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  8.  KРѓё on {[r3c8=r9c9], [r3c9=r9c8], r5c8, r5c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (set+ladder+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r3c8Рђћr3c9, r5c8Рђћr5c9, r9c8Рђћr9c9}:
      Satellites r3c6 (rung 1), r8c7 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Guardian: r6c3 guards oddagon {r6c3, r6c6, r3c6, r8c7, r8c3} (source r8c6).
      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 8, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {[r6c3=r8c6]} = {r7c3}.
      Рєњ color([r6c3=r8c6]) = color(r7c3), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  3.  Circular ladder {r3c6Рђћr6c7, r3c8Рђћr5c8, r3c9Рђћr5c9}:
      Satellites r6c6 (rung 1), [r8c3=r9c8] (rung 2), r7c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub r6c6 forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r6c7, r6c3, [r8c3=r9c8], r8c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_hexagon] (diamond+xwing+oddwheel+branch) ===

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (hexagon+xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c6, r7c9:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r7c9). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r9c2 guards oddagon {r9c2, r8c3, r8c6, [r3c6=r7c9], r9c8} (source r8c7).
            Рєњ color(r9c2) = color(r8c7). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c6=r7c9], r6c9, r6c3, r7c3}:
            Diagonals: {[r3c6=r7c9], r6c3} and {r6c9, r7c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.

              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c9) = color(r7c3) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c9}: r6c9=X Рєњ r6c3, r6c5РЅаX
              col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3=X Рєњ r8c3РЅаX

        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: r7c9=X Рєњ r6c9РЅаX
        box 6 {[r5c7=r9c6], r6c8, r6c9}: r6c8, r6c9РЅаX Рєњ [r5c7=r9c6]=X

        row 9 {[r5c7=r9c6], r9c1, r9c2}: [r5c7=r9c6]=X Рєњ r9c1, r9c2РЅаX
        col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, [r5c7=r9c6]}: [r5c7=r9c6]=X Рєњ r5c6РЅаX
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r9c1, r9c2РЅаX Рєњ r8c3=X
        r6c3=X and r8c3=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r7c8) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c9=X Рєњ r3c6, r3c8РЅаX
        col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r7c8}: r7c8=X Рєњ r6c8РЅаX
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: r3c9=X Рєњ r6c9, r7c9РЅаX

        row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c8, r6c9РЅаX Рєњ r6c3=X

        r6c3=X and r8c3=X are adjacent

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c7, r6c8, r6c9, r6c3} (spine r6c8Рђћr6c9).
      Рєњ color(r5c7) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2, r9c6} (spine r9c1Рђћr9c2).
      Рєњ color(r8c3) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c6, r5c6, [r8c3=r9c6], [r5c7=r6c3]} (spine r5c6Рђћ[r8c3=r9c6]).
      Рєњ color(r3c6) = color([r5c7=r6c3]). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c9, [r3c6=r5c7=r6c3], r3c8, r6c8} (spine [r3c6=r5c7=r6c3]Рђћr3c8).
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r6c8). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r3c8, [r3c6=r5c7=r6c3], [r3c9=r6c8], r6c9} (spine [r3c6=r5c7=r6c3]Рђћ[r3c9=r6c8]).
      Рєњ color(r3c8) = color(r6c9). Identify.
  6.  KРѓё on {[r3c8=r6c9], [r3c9=r6c8], r7c8, r7c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (ladder+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r3c8Рђћr3c9, r6c8Рђћr6c9, r7c8Рђћr7c9}:
      Satellites r3c6 (rung 1), r5c7 (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Branch on r5c7, r9c6:
      Case A: color(r5c7) = color(r9c6). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c8, r3c9, r7c9, r7c8}:
            Diagonals: {r3c8, r7c9} and {r3c9, r7c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c8) = color(r7c9) = X
              row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c8=X Рєњ r3c6, r3c9РЅаX
              col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r7c8}: r3c8=X Рєњ r6c8, r7c8РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: r7c9=X Рєњ r6c9РЅаX
              box 6 {[r5c7=r9c6], r6c8, r6c9}: r6c8, r6c9РЅаX Рєњ [r5c7=r9c6]=X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c8, r6c9РЅаX Рєњ r6c3=X
              row 9 {[r5c7=r9c6], r9c1, r9c2}: [r5c7=r9c6]=X Рєњ r9c1, r9c2РЅаX
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, [r5c7=r9c6]}: [r5c7=r9c6]=X Рєњ r5c6РЅаX
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r9c1, r9c2РЅаX Рєњ r8c3=X
              r6c3=X and r8c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r7c8) = X
              row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c9=X Рєњ r3c6, r3c8РЅаX
              col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r7c8}: r7c8=X Рєњ r6c8РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: r3c9=X Рєњ r6c9, r7c9РЅаX
              box 6 {[r5c7=r9c6], r6c8, r6c9}: r6c8, r6c9РЅаX Рєњ [r5c7=r9c6]=X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c8, r6c9РЅаX Рєњ r6c3=X
              row 9 {[r5c7=r9c6], r9c1, r9c2}: [r5c7=r9c6]=X Рєњ r9c1, r9c2РЅаX
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, [r5c7=r9c6]}: [r5c7=r9c6]=X Рєњ r5c6РЅаX
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r9c1, r9c2РЅаX Рєњ r8c3=X
              r6c3=X and r8c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c7) РЅа color(r9c6). Add edge.
        4.  KРѓё on {r3c6, r5c6, r5c7, r9c6}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder) ===

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_ladder] (diamond+oddwheel) ===

      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r6c8). Identify.

      Bivalue oddagon {[r3c6=r5c7=r6c3], r3c8, r7c8, r7c9, r6c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_ladder] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:

  1.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2, r9c8} (spine r9c1Рђћr9c2).

      Bivalue oddagon {r3c8, r3c9, r7c9, r7c6, r5c6, r5c7, r6c8} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

  3.  Diamond {[r5c7=r6c3], r7c3, r9c2, r8c2} (spine r7c3Рђћr9c2).
      Рєњ color([r5c7=r6c3]) = color(r8c2). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r6c8, [r5c7=r6c3=r8c2], r6c9, r8c9} (spine [r5c7=r6c3=r8c2]Рђћr6c9).
      Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9, [r6c8=r8c9]} (spine r3c8Рђћr3c9).
      Рєњ color(r3c6) = color([r6c8=r8c9]). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {r3c9, [r3c6=r6c8=r8c9], r3c8, r9c8} (spine [r3c6=r6c8=r8c9]Рђћr3c8).
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {r3c8, [r3c6=r6c8=r8c9], [r3c9=r9c8], r6c9} (spine [r3c6=r6c8=r8c9]Рђћ[r3c9=r9c8]).
      Рєњ color(r3c8) = color(r6c9). Identify.
  8.  Diamond {[r3c9=r9c8], [r3c6=r6c8=r8c9], [r3c8=r6c9], [r5c7=r6c3=r8c2]} (spine [r3c6=r6c8=r8c9]Рђћ[r3c8=r6c9]).
      Рєњ color([r3c9=r9c8]) = color([r5c7=r6c3=r8c2]). Identify.
  9.  KРѓё on {[r3c6=r6c8=r8c9], [r3c9=r5c7=r6c3=r8c2=r9c8], r5c6, r9c6}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (ladder+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r3c8Рђћr3c9, r6c8Рђћr6c9, r9c8Рђћr8c9}:
      Satellites r3c6 (rung 1), r5c7 (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Circular ladder {r3c6Рђћr5c7, r3c8Рђћr6c8, r3c9Рђћr6c9}:
      Satellites r5c6 (rung 1), r9c8 (rung 2), r8c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  3.  Guardian: r6c8 guards oddagon {r6c8, r5c7, r3c6, r9c6, r9c8} (source r5c6).
      Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r5c6). Identify.
  4.  Guardian: r8c2 guards oddagon {r8c2, r8c9, [r5c6=r6c8], r9c6, r9c2} (source r9c8).
      Рєњ color(r8c2) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  5.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c8, r3c9, r8c9, [r8c2=r9c8]}:
      Diagonals: {r3c8, r8c9} and {r3c9, [r8c2=r9c8]} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c8) = color(r8c9) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c8=X Рєњ r3c6, r3c9РЅаX
        col 8 {r3c8, [r5c6=r6c8], [r8c2=r9c8]}: r3c8=X Рєњ [r5c6=r6c8], [r8c2=r9c8]РЅаX
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r8c9}: r8c9=X Рєњ r6c9РЅаX
        box 6 {[r5c6=r6c8], r5c7, r6c9}: [r5c6=r6c8], r6c9РЅаX Рєњ r5c7=X
        row 6 {[r5c6=r6c8], r6c3, r6c9}: [r5c6=r6c8], r6c9РЅаX Рєњ r6c3=X
        col 6 {r3c6, [r5c6=r6c8], r9c6}: r3c6, [r5c6=r6c8]РЅаX Рєњ r9c6=X
        row 9 {[r8c2=r9c8], r9c2, r9c6}: r9c6=X Рєњ r9c2РЅаX
        box 7 {r7c3, [r8c2=r9c8], r9c2}: [r8c2=r9c8], r9c2РЅаX Рєњ r7c3=X
        r6c3=X and r7c3=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color([r8c2=r9c8]) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c9=X Рєњ r3c6, r3c8РЅаX
        row 9 {[r8c2=r9c8], r9c2, r9c6}: [r8c2=r9c8]=X Рєњ r9c2, r9c6РЅаX
        col 6 {r3c6, [r5c6=r6c8], r9c6}: r3c6, r9c6РЅаX Рєњ [r5c6=r6c8]=X
        col 8 {r3c8, [r5c6=r6c8], [r8c2=r9c8]}: [r5c6=r6c8]=X and [r8c2=r9c8]=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_set] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 7, box 8}.

            Diagonals: {r3c6, r6c8} and {r3c8, r6c4} (non-adjacent).

  3.  Diamond {r3c8, r3c6, r3c9, [r5c5=r6c9=r7c6]} (spine r3c6Рђћr3c9).
      Рєњ color(r3c8) = color([r5c5=r6c9=r7c6]). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r3c8=r5c5=r6c9=r7c6], r6c6} (spine r3c6Рђћ[r3c8=r5c5=r6c9=r7c6]).
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r3c6, [r3c8=r5c5=r6c9=r7c6], [r3c9=r6c6], r6c4} (spine [r3c8=r5c5=r6c9=r7c6]Рђћ[r3c9=r6c6]).
      Рєњ color(r3c6) = color(r6c4). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {r8c8, [r3c8=r5c5=r6c9=r7c6], r8c5, r9c5} (spine [r3c8=r5c5=r6c9=r7c6]Рђћr8c5).
      Рєњ color(r8c8) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  7.  KРѓё on {r8c3, [r8c8=r9c5], r9c2, r9c3}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_guardian] (set+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 7, box 8}.
      Remainder: {r8c8} = {r7c6}.
      Рєњ color(r8c8) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  SET: {col 5, col 6}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 5, box 8}.
      Remainder: {r3c6} = {r6c4}.
      Рєњ color(r3c6) = color(r6c4). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c6=r6c4] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c8, r3c9, r6c9, r6c6, [r7c6=r8c8]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3Рђћr9c3, r8c5Рђћr9c5, r8c9Рђћr9c8}:

      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_ladder] (diamond+set+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r8c5, r9c5, r7c6} (spine r8c5Рђћr9c5).
      Рєњ color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 3, row 8, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{col 3, col 5, col 8}.
      Remainder: {r3c6, r3c9} = {[r5c5=r7c6], r6c3}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Diamond {[r5c5=r7c6], r6c3, r6c4, r6c9} (spine r6c3Рђћr6c4).
      Рєњ color([r5c5=r7c6]) = color(r6c9). Identify.

      Рєњ color(r3c8) = color([r5c5=r6c9=r7c6]). Identify.
  5.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c3, r8c8, r9c8, r9c3}:
      Diagonals: {r8c3, r9c8} and {r8c8, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r8c3) = color(r9c8) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c8}: r8c3=X Рєњ r8c5, r8c8РЅаX
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c8}: r9c8=X Рєњ r9c3, r9c5РЅаX

        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c8) = color(r9c3) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c8}: r8c8=X Рєњ r8c3, r8c5РЅаX
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c8}: r9c3=X Рєњ r9c5, r9c8РЅаX
        col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X Рєњ r6c3РЅаX

        col 8 {[r3c8=r5c5=r6c9=r7c6], r8c8, r9c8}: [r3c8=r5c5=r6c9=r7c6]=X and r8c8=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

            Рєњ color([r3c6=r6c8=r9c9]) = color(r8c5). Identify.

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

      Рєњ color(r3c8) = color(r5c9). Identify.

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_ladder] (diamond+xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c8, r7c7:
      Case A: color(r3c8) = color(r7c7). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {[r3c8=r7c7], r3c6, r7c6, r6c6} (spine r3c6Рђћr7c6).
            Рєњ color([r3c8=r7c7]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        3.  Guardian: r9c2 guards oddagon {r9c2, r6c2, r6c8, r8c8, r9c9} (source [r3c8=r6c6=r7c7]).
            Рєњ color(r9c2) = color([r3c8=r6c6=r7c7]). Identify.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c8=r6c6=r7c7=r9c2], r6c2, r5c3, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {[r3c8=r6c6=r7c7=r9c2], r5c3} and {r6c2, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c8=r6c6=r7c7=r9c2]) = color(r5c3) = X
              row 3 {r3c6, [r3c8=r6c6=r7c7=r9c2], r3c9}: [r3c8=r6c6=r7c7=r9c2]=X Рєњ r3c6, r3c9РЅаX
              row 6 {[r3c8=r6c6=r7c7=r9c2], r6c2, r6c8}: [r3c8=r6c6=r7c7=r9c2]=X Рєњ r6c2, r6c8РЅаX
              col 6 {r3c6, [r3c8=r6c6=r7c7=r9c2], r7c6}: [r3c8=r6c6=r7c7=r9c2]=X Рєњ r7c6РЅаX
              col 8 {[r3c8=r6c6=r7c7=r9c2], r6c8, r8c8}: [r3c8=r6c6=r7c7=r9c2]=X Рєњ r8c8РЅаX
              box 9 {[r3c8=r6c6=r7c7=r9c2], r8c8, r9c9}: [r3c8=r6c6=r7c7=r9c2]=X Рєњ r9c9РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r3c9, r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r5c9=X
              r5c3=X and r5c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c2) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 6 {[r3c8=r6c6=r7c7=r9c2], r6c2, r6c8}: r6c2=X Рєњ [r3c8=r6c6=r7c7=r9c2], r6c8РЅаX
              col 8 {[r3c8=r6c6=r7c7=r9c2], r6c8, r8c8}: [r3c8=r6c6=r7c7=r9c2], r6c8РЅаX Рєњ r8c8=X
              box 9 {[r3c8=r6c6=r7c7=r9c2], r8c8, r9c9}: r8c8=X Рєњ r9c9РЅаX
              r8c3=X and r8c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c8) РЅа color(r7c7). Add edge.
        5.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c8, r3c9, r5c9, r6c8}:
            Diagonals: {r3c8, r5c9} and {r3c9, r6c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c8) = color(r5c9) = X
              row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c8=X Рєњ r3c6, r3c9РЅаX
              col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r3c8=X Рєњ r6c8, r8c8РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r5c9=X Рєњ r9c9РЅаX

              r3c8=X and r7c7=X are adjacent

        r8c3=X and r9c2=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond+set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r7c7, r9c8, r9c9, r9c2} (spine r9c8Рђћr9c9).
      Рєњ color(r7c7) = color(r9c2). Identify.

      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Diamond {r6c6, r3c6, r7c6, [r7c7=r9c2]} (spine r3c6Рђћr7c6).

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_ladder] (diamond+xwing+branch) ===

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c5, r8c5, r9c5, r7c6} (spine r8c5Рђћr9c5).
      Рєњ color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r3c8Рђћr3c9, r5c8Рђћr4c9, r9c8Рђћr8c9}:
      Satellites r3c6 (rung 1), r6c7 (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Circular ladder {r3c6Рђћr6c7, r3c8Рђћr5c8, r3c9Рђћr4c9}:
      Satellites [r6c5=r7c6] (rung 1), r9c8 (rung 2), r8c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  4.  Diamond {r8c9, [r6c5=r7c6], r8c5, r9c5} (spine [r6c5=r7c6]Рђћr8c5).
      Рєњ color(r8c9) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  5.  Odd wheel: hub [r8c9=r9c5] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r6c5=r7c6], r8c5, r8c3, r9c2, r9c8} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

        r6c3=X and r6c6=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3Рђћr8c3).
      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r6c3=r9c2], r9c8, r9c9, r8c7} (spine r9c8Рђћr9c9).
      Рєњ color([r6c3=r9c2]) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r7c3, [r6c3=r8c7=r9c2], r8c3, r8c6} (spine [r6c3=r8c7=r9c2]Рђћr8c3).
      Рєњ color(r7c3) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r6c6, [r6c3=r8c7=r9c2], [r7c3=r8c6], r8c3} (spine [r6c3=r8c7=r9c2]Рђћ[r7c3=r8c6]).
      Рєњ color(r6c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r3c6, [r6c6=r8c3], [r7c3=r8c6], [r6c3=r8c7=r9c2]} (spine [r6c6=r8c3]Рђћ[r7c3=r8c6]).
      Рєњ color(r3c6) = color([r6c3=r8c7=r9c2]). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {r3c9, [r3c6=r6c3=r8c7=r9c2], r3c8, r9c8} (spine [r3c6=r6c3=r8c7=r9c2]Рђћr3c8).
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r9c8). Identify.

      Рєњ color(r3c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  8.  KРѓё on {[r3c8=r9c9], [r3c9=r9c8], r4c9, r5c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (set+ladder+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r3c8Рђћr3c9, r5c8Рђћr4c9, r9c8Рђћr9c9}:

  2.  Guardian: r6c3 guards oddagon {r6c3, r6c6, r3c6, r8c7, r8c3} (source r8c6).
      Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 8, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {[r6c3=r8c6]} = {r7c3}.
      Рєњ color([r6c3=r8c6]) = color(r7c3), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

            Diagonals: {r4c9, r9c8} and {r5c8, r7c9} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c9) = color(r9c8) = X
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c8}: r9c8=X Рєњ r9c1, r9c2РЅаX
              col 8 {r3c8, r5c8, r9c8}: r9c8=X Рєњ r3c8, r5c8РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r7c9}: r4c9=X Рєњ r3c9, r7c9РЅаX

              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r9c1, r9c2РЅаX Рєњ r8c3=X

              r3c6=X and r6c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.

              col 8 {r3c8, r5c8, r9c8}: r5c8=X Рєњ r3c8, r9c8РЅаX

              r3c6=X and r6c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2, r9c8} (spine r9c1Рђћr9c2).
      Рєњ color(r8c3) = color(r9c8). Identify.

  3.  Circular ladder {r3c6Рђћr6c7, r3c8Рђћr5c8, r3c9Рђћr4c9}:
      Satellites r6c6 (rung 1), [r8c3=r9c8] (rung 2), r7c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub r6c6 forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r6c7, r6c3, [r8c3=r9c8], r8c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

  1.  Circular ladder {r3c8Рђћr3c9, r6c8Рђћr4c9, r8c8Рђћr7c9}:
      Satellites r3c6 (rung 1), r9c7 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Diamond {r6c6, r3c6, r9c6, r9c7} (spine r3c6Рђћr9c6).
      Рєњ color(r6c6) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c8, r6c8, r8c8, [r6c6=r9c7]} (spine r6c8Рђћr8c8).
      Рєњ color(r3c8) = color([r6c6=r9c7]). Identify.
  4.  Guardian: r5c3 guards oddagon {r5c3, r6c2, r6c8, r8c8, r7c9, r3c9, r3c6, r9c6, r9c3} (source [r3c8=r6c6=r9c7]).
      Рєњ color(r5c3) = color([r3c8=r6c6=r9c7]). Identify.
  5.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c8=r5c3=r6c6=r9c7] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r7c9, r8c8, r8c3, r9c3, r9c6} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r3c8Рђћr3c9, r6c8Рђћr4c9, r8c8Рђћr7c9}:

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_ladder] (set+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c8, r9c7:
      Case A: color(r3c8) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        2.  SET: {row 3, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{col 6, col 9}.
            Remainder: {[r3c8=r9c7], r9c3} = {r4c9, r6c6, r7c9}.
            Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c8=r9c7], r6c8, r6c6, r9c6}:
            Diagonals: {[r3c8=r9c7], r6c6} and {r6c8, r9c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c8=r9c7]) = color(r6c6) = X
              row 3 {r3c6, [r3c8=r9c7], r3c9}: [r3c8=r9c7]=X Рєњ r3c6, r3c9РЅаX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6=X Рєњ r6c2, r6c8РЅаX
              row 9 {[r3c8=r9c7], r9c3, r9c6}: [r3c8=r9c7]=X Рєњ r9c3, r9c6РЅаX
              col 8 {[r3c8=r9c7], r6c8, r8c8}: [r3c8=r9c7]=X Рєњ r8c8РЅаX
              box 9 {[r3c8=r9c7], r7c9, r8c8}: [r3c8=r9c7]=X Рєњ r7c9РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r7c9}: r3c9, r7c9РЅаX Рєњ r4c9=X
              r4c9=X and r6c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c8) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c8=X Рєњ r6c2, r6c6РЅаX
              row 9 {[r3c8=r9c7], r9c3, r9c6}: r9c6=X Рєњ [r3c8=r9c7], r9c3РЅаX
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X Рєњ r3c6РЅаX
              col 8 {[r3c8=r9c7], r6c8, r8c8}: r6c8=X Рєњ r8c8РЅаX
              box 9 {[r3c8=r9c7], r7c9, r8c8}: [r3c8=r9c7], r8c8РЅаX Рєњ r7c9=X
              row 3 {r3c6, [r3c8=r9c7], r3c9}: r3c6, [r3c8=r9c7]РЅаX Рєњ r3c9=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r7c9}: r3c9=X and r7c9=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c8) РЅа color(r9c7). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c8, r3c9, r4c9, r6c8}:
            Diagonals: {r3c8, r4c9} and {r3c9, r6c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c8) = color(r4c9) = X
              row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c8=X Рєњ r3c6, r3c9РЅаX
              col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r3c8=X Рєњ r6c8, r8c8РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r7c9}: r4c9=X Рєњ r7c9РЅаX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r7c9, r8c8РЅаX Рєњ r9c7=X
              r3c8=X and r9c7=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c9=X Рєњ r3c6, r3c8РЅаX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c8=X Рєњ r6c2, r6c6РЅаX

              col 9 {r3c9, [r5c9=r6c6=r8c8], r9c9}: [r5c9=r6c6=r8c8]=X Рєњ r3c9РЅаX

              Contradiction.

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 3, row 6, row 8}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{col 3, col 6, col 9}.

      Diagonals: {r5c9, r7c8} and {r6c8, [r8c9=r9c5]} (non-adjacent).

        r5c9=X and r6c8=X are adjacent

        row 9 {[r8c9=r9c5], r9c3, r9c7}: [r8c9=r9c5]=X Рєњ r9c3, r9c7РЅаX

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===

      Рєњ color(r3c3) = color(r6c6). Identify.

  7.  KРѓё on {[r5c9=r9c5], [r6c8=r8c3], [r8c5=r9c9], r9c2}. Contradiction.

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_set] (hexagon+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c9, r8c8:
      Case A: color(r5c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c9=r8c8], r8c3, r9c2, r9c9}:
            Diagonals: {[r5c9=r8c8], r9c2} and {r8c3, r9c9} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r5c9=r8c8]) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 8 {[r5c9=r8c8], r8c3, r8c5}: [r5c9=r8c8]=X Рєњ r8c3, r8c5РЅаX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c2=X Рєњ r9c5, r9c9РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, [r5c9=r8c8], r9c9}: [r5c9=r8c8]=X Рєњ r3c9РЅаX
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c5}: r8c5, r9c5РЅаX Рєњ r7c6=X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r7c6=X Рєњ r3c6, r6c6РЅаX
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c6, r3c9РЅаX Рєњ r3c3=X
              col 3 {r3c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r3c3=X Рєњ r6c3РЅаX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c3, r6c6РЅаX Рєњ r6c8=X
              [r5c9=r8c8]=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c3) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 8 {[r5c9=r8c8], r8c3, r8c5}: r8c3=X Рєњ [r5c9=r8c8], r8c5РЅаX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c9=X Рєњ r9c2, r9c5РЅаX
              col 3 {r3c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X Рєњ r3c3, r6c3РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, [r5c9=r8c8], r9c9}: r9c9=X Рєњ r3c9РЅаX
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c5}: r8c5, r9c5РЅаX Рєњ r7c6=X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3, r3c9РЅаX Рєњ r3c6=X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r3c6=X and r7c6=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c9) РЅа color(r8c8). Add edge.
        3.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r3c6, r3c9, r5c9, r8c8, r8c5, r7c6}
            Bridges: r3c3Рђћr8c3 (edges r3c6Рђћr3c9 Рєћ r8c8Рђћr8c5), r9c9Рђћr9c5 (edges r3c9Рђћr5c9 Рєћ r8c5Рђћr7c6), r6c8Рђћr6c6 (edges r5c9Рђћr8c8 Рєћ r7c6Рђћr3c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_set] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r7c7, r9c3:
      Case A: color(r7c7) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c3, r3c6, r7c6, [r7c7=r9c3]}:
            Diagonals: {r3c3, r7c6} and {r3c6, [r7c7=r9c3]} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c3) = color(r7c6) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3=X Рєњ r3c6, r3c9РЅаX
              col 3 {r3c3, r6c3, [r7c7=r9c3]}: r3c3=X Рєњ r6c3, [r7c7=r9c3]РЅаX
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r7c6=X Рєњ r6c6РЅаX
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c5}: r7c6=X Рєњ r8c5, r9c5РЅаX

              box 9 {[r7c7=r9c3], r8c9, r9c7}: r9c7=X Рєњ r8c9РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r3c9, r8c9РЅаX Рєњ r5c9=X
              r5c9=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c6) = color([r7c7=r9c3]) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c6=X Рєњ r3c3, r3c9РЅаX
              row 9 {[r7c7=r9c3], r9c5, r9c7}: [r7c7=r9c3]=X Рєњ r9c5, r9c7РЅаX
              col 3 {r3c3, r6c3, [r7c7=r9c3]}: [r7c7=r9c3]=X Рєњ r6c3РЅаX
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r3c6=X Рєњ r6c6, r7c6РЅаX
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c5}: r7c6, r9c5РЅаX Рєњ r8c5=X
              box 9 {[r7c7=r9c3], r8c9, r9c7}: [r7c7=r9c3]=X Рєњ r8c9РЅаX

              r5c9=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r7c7) РЅа color(r9c3). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c6, r7c7, r8c9, r8c5}:
            Diagonals: {r7c6, r8c9} and {r7c7, r8c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c6) = color(r8c9) = X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r7c6=X Рєњ r3c6, r6c6РЅаX

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

        r5c9=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_ladder] (diamond+set+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 8, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c3} = {r7c8}.
      Рєњ color(r9c3) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, r6c3, r6c8, [r7c8=r9c3]} (spine r6c3Рђћr6c8).
      Рєњ color(r6c5) = color([r7c8=r9c3]). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c3, r3c6, r9c6, [r6c5=r7c8=r9c3]}:
      Diagonals: {r3c3, r9c6} and {r3c6, [r6c5=r7c8=r9c3]} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c3) = color(r9c6) = X
        row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3=X Рєњ r3c6, r3c9РЅаX
        row 9 {[r6c5=r7c8=r9c3], r9c6, r9c9}: r9c6=X Рєњ [r6c5=r7c8=r9c3], r9c9РЅаX
        col 3 {r3c3, r6c3, [r6c5=r7c8=r9c3]}: r3c3=X Рєњ r6c3РЅаX
        col 6 {r3c6, r8c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X Рєњ r8c6РЅаX
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r3c9, r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r5c9=X
        box 8 {r8c5, r8c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X Рєњ r8c5РЅаX
        box 9 {[r6c5=r7c8=r9c3], r8c7, r9c9}: [r6c5=r7c8=r9c3], r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r8c7=X
        row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r7c8=r9c3], r6c8}: r6c3, [r6c5=r7c8=r9c3]РЅаX Рєњ r6c8=X
        r5c9=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c6) = color([r6c5=r7c8=r9c3]) = X
        row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c6=X Рєњ r3c3, r3c9РЅаX
        row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r7c8=r9c3], r6c8}: [r6c5=r7c8=r9c3]=X Рєњ r6c3, r6c8РЅаX

        col 6 {r3c6, r8c6, r9c6}: r3c6=X Рєњ r8c6РЅаX
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r3c9, r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r5c9=X
        box 8 {r8c5, r8c6, r9c6}: r8c6, r9c6РЅаX Рєњ r8c5=X

        [r6c5=r7c8=r9c3]=X and r8c5=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r3c3Рђћr9c3, r3c6Рђћr9c6, r3c9Рђћr9c9}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), r8c6 (rung 2), r5c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Diamond {r6c8, r5c9, r6c3, r8c6} (spine r5c9Рђћr6c3).
      Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r7c8, [r6c8=r8c6], r8c7, r8c5} (spine [r6c8=r8c6]Рђћr8c7).
      Рєњ color(r7c8) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r3c3, r3c9, r9c9, [r7c8=r8c5], r6c5, r6c3}
      Bridges: r3c6Рђћ[r6c8=r8c6] (edges r3c3Рђћr3c9 Рєћ [r7c8=r8c5]Рђћr6c5), r5c9Рђћ[r6c8=r8c6] (edges r3c9Рђћr9c9 Рєћ r6c5Рђћr6c3), r9c6Рђћr9c3 (edges r9c9Рђћ[r7c8=r8c5] Рєћ r6c3Рђћr3c3).
      Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
      but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_ladder] (diamond+xwing+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===

  1.  Diamond {r3c6, r7c6, r8c6, r9c5} (spine r7c6Рђћr8c6).

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (hexagon+xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c6, r7c7:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r7c7). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r6c8 guards oddagon {r6c8, r5c9, r3c9, [r3c6=r7c7], r9c8} (source r8c9).
            Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
        3.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r3c3, r3c9, [r6c8=r8c9], r8c5, r9c6, r9c3}
            Bridges: [r3c6=r7c7]Рђћr7c6 (edges r3c3Рђћr3c9 Рєћ r8c5Рђћr9c6), [r3c6=r7c7]Рђћr9c8 (edges r3c9Рђћ[r6c8=r8c9] Рєћ r9c6Рђћr9c3), r6c5Рђћr6c3 (edges [r6c8=r8c9]Рђћr8c5 Рєћ r9c3Рђћr3c3).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) РЅа color(r7c7). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c6, r7c7, r8c9, r8c5}:
            Diagonals: {r7c6, r8c9} and {r7c7, r8c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c6) = color(r8c9) = X
              col 6 {r3c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r7c6=X Рєњ r3c6, r9c6РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r8c9=X Рєњ r3c9, r5c9РЅаX
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c6}: r7c6=X Рєњ r8c5РЅаX
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c8}: r8c9=X Рєњ r7c7, r9c8РЅаX

              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c6, r9c8РЅаX Рєњ r9c3=X
              col 3 {r3c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r3c3=X and r9c3=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c7) = color(r8c5) = X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c6}: r8c5=X Рєњ r7c6, r9c6РЅаX
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c8}: r7c7=X Рєњ r8c9, r9c8РЅаX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c6, r9c8РЅаX Рєњ r9c3=X
              col 3 {r3c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X Рєњ r3c3, r6c3РЅаX
              col 6 {r3c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r7c6, r9c6РЅаX Рєњ r3c6=X
              r3c6=X and r7c7=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

        [r5c9=r6c5]=X and r8c5=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond+set+ladder) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c6, r7c6, r9c6, r8c5} (spine r7c6Рђћr9c6).
      Рєњ color(r3c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 3, row 6, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{col 3, col 6, col 9}.
      Remainder: {r6c5, r6c8} = {r5c9, r7c6}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Circular ladder {r3c3Рђћr9c3, [r3c6=r8c5]Рђћr9c6, r3c9Рђћr9c9}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2), r5c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  4.  Diamond {r6c8, r5c9, r6c3, r7c6} (spine r5c9Рђћr6c3).
      Рєњ color(r6c8) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r6c5, [r3c6=r8c5], [r6c8=r7c6], r8c8} (spine [r3c6=r8c5]Рђћ[r6c8=r7c6]).
      Рєњ color(r6c5) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r6c5=r8c8], [r3c6=r8c5], [r6c8=r7c6], r9c6} (spine [r3c6=r8c5]Рђћ[r6c8=r7c6]).
      Рєњ color([r6c5=r8c8]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {[r6c8=r7c6], r6c3, [r6c5=r8c8=r9c6], r9c3} (spine r6c3Рђћ[r6c5=r8c8=r9c6]).
      Рєњ color([r6c8=r7c6]) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  8.  KРѓё on {[r6c5=r8c8=r9c6], [r6c8=r7c6=r9c3], r7c7, r9c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (ladder+guardian) ===

      Рєњ color(r8c5) = color(r6c3). Identify.

  4.  KРѓё on {[r6c3=r7c7=r8c5], r9c3, r9c6, r9c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_ladder] (diamond+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c3, r6c5:
      Case A: color(r3c3) = color(r6c5). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c6, r7c7, r8c8, r8c5}:
            Diagonals: {r7c6, r8c8} and {r7c7, r8c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c6) = color(r8c8) = X
              col 6 {r3c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r7c6=X Рєњ r3c6, r9c6РЅаX
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c6}: r7c6=X Рєњ r8c5РЅаX
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c8, r9c9}: r8c8=X Рєњ r7c7, r9c9РЅаX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c6, r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r9c3=X
              col 3 {[r3c3=r6c5], r6c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X Рєњ [r3c3=r6c5], r6c3РЅаX
              row 3 {[r3c3=r6c5], r3c6, r3c9}: [r3c3=r6c5], r3c6РЅаX Рєњ r3c9=X
              row 6 {[r3c3=r6c5], r6c3, r6c8}: [r3c3=r6c5], r6c3РЅаX Рєњ r6c8=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r3c9=X Рєњ r5c9РЅаX
              r6c8=X and r8c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c7) = color(r8c5) = X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c6}: r8c5=X Рєњ r7c6, r9c6РЅаX
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c8, r9c9}: r7c7=X Рєњ r8c8, r9c9РЅаX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c6, r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r9c3=X
              col 3 {[r3c3=r6c5], r6c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X Рєњ [r3c3=r6c5], r6c3РЅаX
              col 6 {r3c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r7c6, r9c6РЅаX Рєњ r3c6=X
              row 3 {[r3c3=r6c5], r3c6, r3c9}: r3c6=X Рєњ r3c9РЅаX
              row 6 {[r3c3=r6c5], r6c3, r6c8}: [r3c3=r6c5], r6c3РЅаX Рєњ r6c8=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r3c9, r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r5c9=X
              r5c9=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c3) РЅа color(r6c5). Add edge.
        3.  Diamond {r6c5, r3c3, r6c3, r9c3} (spine r3c3Рђћr6c3).
            Рєњ color(r6c5) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c3, r3c6, r9c6, [r6c5=r9c3]}:
            Diagonals: {r3c3, r9c6} and {r3c6, [r6c5=r9c3]} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c3) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3=X Рєњ r3c6, r3c9РЅаX
              row 9 {[r6c5=r9c3], r9c6, r9c9}: r9c6=X Рєњ [r6c5=r9c3], r9c9РЅаX
              col 3 {r3c3, r6c3, [r6c5=r9c3]}: r3c3=X Рєњ r6c3РЅаX
              col 6 {r3c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X Рєњ r7c6РЅаX
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r3c9, r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r5c9=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c6}: r9c6=X Рєњ r8c5РЅаX
              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r9c3], r6c8}: r6c3, [r6c5=r9c3]РЅаX Рєњ r6c8=X
              r5c9=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c6) = color([r6c5=r9c3]) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c6=X Рєњ r3c3, r3c9РЅаX
              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r9c3], r6c8}: [r6c5=r9c3]=X Рєњ r6c3, r6c8РЅаX

              [r6c5=r9c3]=X and r8c5=X are adjacent

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon) ===

      but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

              r5c8=X and r8c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_diamond] (ladder+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r3c6Рђћr3c9, r6c6Рђћr6c9, r8c6Рђћr8c9}:
      Satellites r3c3 (rung 1), r6c8 (rung 2), r8c2 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Guardian: r9c8 guards oddagon {r9c8, r6c8, r3c3, r7c3, r9c1} (source r8c2).
      Рєњ color(r9c8) = color(r8c2). Identify.
  3.  Guardian: r3c9 guards oddagon {r3c9, r3c3, [r8c2=r9c8], r5c8, r6c9} (source r6c8).
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r6c8). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r8c2=r9c8] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c3, [r3c9=r6c8], r8c9, r8c6, r9c5, r9c1, r7c3} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

        row 6 {[r6c6=r9c1], r6c8, r6c9}: [r6c6=r9c1]=X Рєњ r6c8, r6c9РЅаX
        row 9 {[r6c6=r9c1], r9c6, r9c9}: [r6c6=r9c1]=X Рєњ r9c6, r9c9РЅаX
        col 6 {[r3c6=r7c3], [r6c6=r9c1], r9c6}: [r6c6=r9c1]=X Рєњ [r3c6=r7c3]РЅаX
        col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r8c8=X Рєњ r5c8РЅаX
        col 9 {[r3c9=r7c7], r6c9, r9c9}: r6c9, r9c9РЅаX Рєњ [r3c9=r7c7]=X
        box 7 {[r3c6=r7c3], [r6c6=r9c1], r8c2}: [r6c6=r9c1]=X Рєњ r8c2РЅаX
        box 9 {[r3c9=r7c7], r8c8, r9c9}: [r3c9=r7c7]=X and r8c8=X clash
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c2) = color(r9c9) = X
        row 9 {[r6c6=r9c1], r9c6, r9c9}: r9c9=X Рєњ [r6c6=r9c1], r9c6РЅаX
        col 6 {[r3c6=r7c3], [r6c6=r9c1], r9c6}: [r6c6=r9c1], r9c6РЅаX Рєњ [r3c6=r7c3]=X
        col 9 {[r3c9=r7c7], r6c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X Рєњ [r3c9=r7c7], r6c9РЅаX
        box 7 {[r3c6=r7c3], [r6c6=r9c1], r8c2}: [r3c6=r7c3]=X and r8c2=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8Рђћr6c8).
      Рєњ color(r6c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r3c6Рђћr3c9, r6c6Рђћ[r6c9=r8c8], r9c6Рђћr9c9}:
      Satellites r3c3 (rung 1), r6c8 (rung 2), r9c1 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  3.  Guardian: [r6c9=r8c8] guards oddagon {[r6c9=r8c8], r6c8, r3c3, r7c3, r8c2} (source r9c1).
      Рєњ color([r6c9=r8c8]) = color(r9c1). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c9=r8c8=r9c1] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c3, r3c9, r9c9, r7c7, r7c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

  2.  Diamond {r3c6, [r5c8=r6c6], r9c6, r9c8} (spine [r5c8=r6c6]Рђћr9c6).

  3.  Diamond {r3c9, [r3c6=r9c8], r8c9, r7c7} (spine [r3c6=r9c8]Рђћr8c9).

  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c9=r7c7], r7c3, r8c2, r8c9}:
      Diagonals: {[r3c9=r7c7], r8c2} and {r7c3, r8c9} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r3c9=r7c7]) = color(r8c2) = X

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c6, r6c9:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r6c9). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c3, r7c7, r8c9, r8c2}:
            Diagonals: {r7c3, r8c9} and {r7c7, r8c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c3) = color(r8c9) = X
              col 9 {[r3c6=r6c9], r3c9, r8c9}: r8c9=X Рєњ [r3c6=r6c9], r3c9РЅаX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r7c3=X Рєњ r8c2, r9c2РЅаX
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c8}: r8c9=X Рєњ r7c7, r9c8РЅаX
              row 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r6c9], r3c9}: [r3c6=r6c9], r3c9РЅаX Рєњ r3c3=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c2, r9c8РЅаX Рєњ r9c6=X
              col 6 {[r3c6=r6c9], r6c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X Рєњ r6c6РЅаX
              r3c3=X and r7c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c7) = color(r8c2) = X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r8c2=X Рєњ r7c3, r9c2РЅаX
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c8}: r7c7=X Рєњ r8c9, r9c8РЅаX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c2, r9c8РЅаX Рєњ r9c6=X
              col 6 {[r3c6=r6c9], r6c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X Рєњ [r3c6=r6c9], r6c6РЅаX
              col 9 {[r3c6=r6c9], r3c9, r8c9}: [r3c6=r6c9], r8c9РЅаX Рєњ r3c9=X

              r6c7=X and r7c7=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.

            Рєњ color(r9c8) = color(r6c9). Identify.

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+set+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8Рђћr6c8).
      Рєњ color(r6c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, [r6c9=r8c8], r8c5, r8c3} (spine [r6c9=r8c8]Рђћr8c5).
      Рєњ color(r6c5) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 6, row 8}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 8}.
      Remainder: {[r6c5=r8c3], [r6c9=r8c8]} = {r5c8, r7c6, r9c5}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c8, r6c8, [r6c5=r8c3], r9c5}:
      Diagonals: {r5c8, [r6c5=r8c3]} and {r6c8, r9c5} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c8) = color([r6c5=r8c3]) = X
        row 6 {[r6c5=r8c3], r6c8, [r6c9=r8c8]}: [r6c5=r8c3]=X Рєњ r6c8, [r6c9=r8c8]РЅаX

        col 5 {[r6c5=r8c3], r8c5, r9c5}: [r6c5=r8c3]=X Рєњ r9c5РЅаX
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c5}: r8c5, r9c5РЅаX Рєњ r7c6=X
        r5c8=X and r7c6=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c8) = color(r9c5) = X
        row 6 {[r6c5=r8c3], r6c8, [r6c9=r8c8]}: r6c8=X Рєњ [r6c5=r8c3], [r6c9=r8c8]РЅаX

        col 5 {[r6c5=r8c3], r8c5, r9c5}: r8c5=X and r9c5=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

        row 6 {[r6c5=r8c3], [r6c7=r8c8], r6c8}: [r6c5=r8c3]=X Рєњ [r6c7=r8c8], r6c8РЅаX

        r5c8=X and r7c6=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c8) = color(r9c5) = X

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c7, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8Рђћr6c8).
      Рєњ color(r6c7) = color(r8c8). Identify.

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c6, r7c6, r8c6, r9c5} (spine r7c6Рђћr8c6).
      Рєњ color(r3c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {[r3c6=r9c5]Рђћr8c6, r9c2Рђћr8c3, r9c8Рђћr8c8}:
      Satellites r7c6 (rung 1), r5c8 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Branch on r3c9, r5c8:
      Case A: color(r3c9) = color(r5c8). Identify.
        4.  Parity transport:
            row 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r9c5], [r3c9=r5c8]}
            row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c9}
            row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}
            row 9 {r9c2, [r3c6=r9c5], r9c8}
            Parallel links: row 3Рєћrow 6 (col, box, col); row 3Рєћrow 8 (col, col, box); row 8Рєћrow 9 (box, box, col)
            Рєњ same permutation parity.
            Each pair shares a column Рєњ distinct permutations.
            4 same-parity permutations from 3 available Рєњ pigeonhole contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c9) РЅа color(r5c8). Add edge.
        5.  Circular ladder {r3c3Рђћr6c3, [r3c6=r9c5]Рђћr6c5, r3c9Рђћr6c9}:
            Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r5c8 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        6.  Diamond {r8c3, r5c8, r8c8, r9c8} (spine r5c8Рђћr8c8).
            Рєњ color(r8c3) = color(r9c8). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c3, [r3c6=r9c5], [r8c3=r9c8]} (spine r3c3Рђћ[r3c6=r9c5]).
            Рєњ color(r3c9) = color([r8c3=r9c8]). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {[r3c6=r9c5], r3c3, [r3c9=r8c3=r9c8], r6c3} (spine r3c3Рђћ[r3c9=r8c3=r9c8]).
            Рєњ color([r3c6=r9c5]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {r3c3, [r3c6=r6c3=r9c5], [r3c9=r8c3=r9c8], r6c9} (spine [r3c6=r6c3=r9c5]Рђћ[r3c9=r8c3=r9c8]).
            Рєњ color(r3c3) = color(r6c9). Identify.
        10.  Diamond {[r3c9=r8c3=r9c8], [r3c3=r6c9], [r3c6=r6c3=r9c5], r6c5} (spine [r3c3=r6c9]Рђћ[r3c6=r6c3=r9c5]).
            Рєњ color([r3c9=r8c3=r9c8]) = color(r6c5). Identify.
        11.  Diamond {[r3c6=r6c3=r9c5], [r3c3=r6c9], [r3c9=r6c5=r8c3=r9c8], r5c8} (spine [r3c3=r6c9]Рђћ[r3c9=r6c5=r8c3=r9c8]).
            Рєњ color([r3c6=r6c3=r9c5]) = color(r5c8). Identify.
        12.  KРѓё on {[r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c5], [r3c9=r6c5=r8c3=r9c8], r8c6, r8c8}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c6, r7c6, r8c6, r9c5} (spine r7c6Рђћr8c6).

      Case B: color(r3c9) РЅа color(r5c8). Add edge.

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

            Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {[r3c9=r8c8], [r3c3=r9c8], [r3c6=r8c5], r9c6} (spine [r3c3=r9c8]Рђћ[r3c6=r8c5]).
            Рєњ color([r3c9=r8c8]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
        8.  KРѓё on {[r3c3=r9c8], [r3c9=r8c8=r9c6], [r5c8=r6c3=r7c7], r9c3}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) РЅа color(r7c6). Add edge.
        9.  Diamond {r6c5, [r3c6=r8c5], r7c6, r9c6} (spine [r3c6=r8c5]Рђћr7c6).
            Рєњ color(r6c5) = color(r9c6). Identify.
        10.  Diamond {r6c9, r6c3, [r6c5=r9c6], r9c3} (spine r6c3Рђћ[r6c5=r9c6]).
            Рєњ color(r6c9) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        11.  Diamond {[r3c6=r8c5], r3c3, r3c9, [r6c9=r9c3]} (spine r3c3Рђћr3c9).
            Рєњ color([r3c6=r8c5]) = color([r6c9=r9c3]). Identify.
        12.  KРѓё on {[r3c6=r6c9=r8c5=r9c3], [r5c8=r7c7], r8c8, r9c8}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c9, r8c8:
      Case A: color(r6c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
        2.  Circular ladder {[r6c9=r8c8]Рђћr8c5, r7c7Рђћr7c6, r9c8Рђћr9c6}:
            Satellites r6c5 (rung 1), r9c3 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        3.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r3c3, r3c6, r7c6, r8c5, r6c5, r6c3}
            Bridges: r3c9Рђћ[r6c9=r8c8] (edges r3c3Рђћr3c6 Рєћ r8c5Рђћr6c5), r9c6Рђћr9c3 (edges r3c6Рђћr7c6 Рєћ r6c5Рђћr6c3), r9c6Рђћr9c3 (edges r7c6Рђћr8c5 Рєћ r6c3Рђћr3c3).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c9) РЅа color(r8c8). Add edge.
        4.  Diamond {r6c9, r5c8, r8c8, r9c8} (spine r5c8Рђћr8c8).
            Рєњ color(r6c9) = color(r9c8). Identify.
        5.  Diamond {r6c3, [r6c9=r9c8], r9c3, r9c6} (spine [r6c9=r9c8]Рђћr9c3).
            Рєњ color(r6c3) = color(r9c6). Identify.
        6.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c3=r9c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r3c3, r3c6, r7c6, r8c5, r6c5, [r6c9=r9c8], r9c3} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_diamond] (ladder+xwing+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c5, r8c8:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r8c8). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r9c3 guards oddagon {r9c3, r6c3, r6c9, r5c8, r9c8} (source [r6c5=r8c8]).

        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c3, r3c6, r9c6, [r6c5=r8c8=r9c3]}:
            Diagonals: {r3c3, r9c6} and {r3c6, [r6c5=r8c8=r9c3]} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c3) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3=X Рєњ r3c6, r3c9РЅаX

              box 9 {[r6c5=r8c8=r9c3], r7c7, r9c8}: [r6c5=r8c8=r9c3], r9c8РЅаX Рєњ r7c7=X

              r5c8=X and r6c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.

              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c6=X Рєњ r3c3, r3c9РЅаX
              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r8c8=r9c3], r6c9}: [r6c5=r8c8=r9c3]=X Рєњ r6c3, r6c9РЅаX

            Diagonals: {r3c6, r6c7} and {r3c9, r8c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r6c7) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c6=X Рєњ r3c3, r3c9РЅаX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c7=X Рєњ r6c3, r6c5РЅаX

              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c8}: r9c3=X Рєњ r9c5, r9c8РЅаX

              r5c8=X and r6c7=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.

=== Greedy proof (diamond+set+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c9, r5c9, r6c9, r5c8} (spine r5c9Рђћr6c9).
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r5c8). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 3, row 6, row 8}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{col 3, col 6, col 8}.
      Remainder: {[r3c9=r5c8], r6c9, r8c5} = {[r3c9=r5c8], r9c6, r9c8}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Diamond {r3c3, r3c6, [r3c9=r5c8], r9c6} (spine r3c6Рђћ[r3c9=r5c8]).
      Рєњ color(r3c3) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  4.  Parity transport:
      row 3 {[r3c3=r9c6], r3c6, [r3c9=r5c8]}
      row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c9}
      row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c8}
      row 9 {r9c2, [r3c3=r9c6], r9c8}
      Parallel links: row 3Рєћrow 6 (col, col, col); row 3Рєћrow 9 (box, col, box); row 8Рєћrow 9 (box, box, col)
      Рєњ same permutation parity.
      Each pair shares a column Рєњ distinct permutations.
      4 same-parity permutations from 3 available Рєњ pigeonhole contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond+set+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c9, r8c8:
      Case A: color(r3c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r3c6, r3c3, [r3c9=r8c8], r8c3} (spine r3c3Рђћ[r3c9=r8c8]).
            Рєњ color(r3c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {[r3c9=r8c8], r3c3, [r3c6=r8c3], r6c3} (spine r3c3Рђћ[r3c6=r8c3]).
            Рєњ color([r3c9=r8c8]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        4.  KРѓё on {[r3c9=r6c3=r8c8], r5c8, r5c9, r6c7}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c9) РЅа color(r8c8). Add edge.
        5.  SET: {row 3, row 6, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{col 3, col 6, col 8}.
            Remainder: {r3c9, r6c7, r9c2} = {r5c8, r8c3, r8c8}.
            Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
        6.  Diamond {r5c9, r3c9, r6c7, r9c2} (spine r3c9Рђћr6c7).
            Рєњ color(r5c9) = color(r9c2). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {r6c7, r5c8, [r5c9=r9c2], r8c3} (spine r5c8Рђћ[r5c9=r9c2]).
            Рєњ color(r6c7) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {r3c6, r3c3, r3c9, [r6c7=r8c3]} (spine r3c3Рђћr3c9).
            Рєњ color(r3c6) = color([r6c7=r8c3]). Identify.
        9.  Parity transport:
            row 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r6c7=r8c3], r3c9}
            row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, [r3c6=r6c7=r8c3]}
            row 8 {[r3c6=r6c7=r8c3], r8c5, r8c8}
            row 9 {[r5c9=r9c2], r9c6, r9c8}
            Parallel links: row 3Рєћrow 6 (col, col, box); row 3Рєћrow 8 (col, box, box); row 8Рєћrow 9 (box, box, col)
            Рєњ same permutation parity.
            Each pair shares a column Рєњ distinct permutations.
            4 same-parity permutations from 3 available Рєњ pigeonhole contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c6, r8c5:
      Case A: color(r5c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r5c8, [r5c6=r8c5], r8c8, r8c3} (spine [r5c6=r8c5]Рђћr8c8).
            Рєњ color(r5c8) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        3.  Circular ladder {[r5c6=r8c5]Рђћr9c6, [r5c8=r8c3]Рђћr9c2, r8c8Рђћr9c7}:
            Satellites r3c6 (rung 1), r7c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        4.  Circular ladder {r3c6Рђћr7c9, [r5c6=r8c5]Рђћr8c8, r9c6Рђћr9c7}:
            Satellites r3c9 (rung 1), [r5c8=r8c3] (rung 2), r9c2 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub r3c9 forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r3c3, r3c6, r7c9, r6c9, [r5c8=r8c3]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c6) РЅа color(r8c5). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r3c6, r3c9, r7c9, r8c8, r8c5, r5c6}
            Bridges: r3c3Рђћr8c3 (edges r3c6Рђћr3c9 Рєћ r8c8Рђћr8c5), r6c9Рђћr6c5 (edges r3c9Рђћr7c9 Рєћ r8c5Рђћr5c6), r9c7Рђћr9c6 (edges r7c9Рђћr8c8 Рєћ r5c6Рђћr3c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (ladder+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c2Рђћr9c2, r8c5Рђћr9c5, r8c8Рђћr9c8}:
      Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), r6c5 (rung 2), r5c8 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Branch on r3c3, r5c8:

        3.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c3=r5c8] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r6c9, r6c5, r5c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c3) РЅа color(r5c8). Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: r3c6 guards oddagon {r3c6, r3c3, r7c3, r6c5, r5c6} (source r5c8).
            Рєњ color(r3c6) = color(r5c8). Identify.

            Bivalue oddagon {r3c3, r3c9, r6c9, r6c5, r7c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_diamond] (set+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 6, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{box 6, box 7}.

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2, r9c5} (spine r9c1Рђћr9c2).
      Рєњ color(r8c3) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c9, r6c3, r6c5, [r8c3=r9c5]} (spine r6c3Рђћr6c5).
      Рєњ color(r6c9) = color([r8c3=r9c5]). Identify.

      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c8, [r6c9=r8c3=r9c5], r6c5}:
      Diagonals: {r5c6, [r6c9=r8c3=r9c5]} and {r5c8, r6c5} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c6) = color([r6c9=r8c3=r9c5]) = X
        row 6 {[r3c9=r6c3], r6c5, [r6c9=r8c3=r9c5]}: [r6c9=r8c3=r9c5]=X Рєњ [r3c9=r6c3], r6c5РЅаX

        row 9 {[r6c9=r8c3=r9c5], r9c1, r9c2}: [r6c9=r8c3=r9c5]=X Рєњ r9c1, r9c2РЅаX
        col 3 {r3c3, [r3c9=r6c3], [r6c9=r8c3=r9c5]}: [r6c9=r8c3=r9c5]=X Рєњ r3c3РЅаX
        row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, [r3c9=r6c3]}: r3c3, [r3c9=r6c3]РЅаX Рєњ r3c6=X
        r3c6=X and r5c6=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c8) = color(r6c5) = X
        row 6 {[r3c9=r6c3], r6c5, [r6c9=r8c3=r9c5]}: r6c5=X Рєњ [r3c9=r6c3], [r6c9=r8c3=r9c5]РЅаX
        col 3 {r3c3, [r3c9=r6c3], [r6c9=r8c3=r9c5]}: [r3c9=r6c3], [r6c9=r8c3=r9c5]РЅаX Рєњ r3c3=X
        col 5 {r6c5, [r6c9=r8c3=r9c5], r8c5}: r6c5=X Рєњ r8c5РЅаX
        row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, [r3c9=r6c3]}: r3c3=X Рєњ r3c6РЅаX

      Рєњ color(r5c8) = color(r6c2). Identify.

      Рєњ color(r3c6) = color([r5c8=r6c2=r9c5]). Identify.

      row 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r5c8=r6c2=r9c5], r3c9}

      Parallel links: row 3Рєћrow 6 (box, box, col); row 3Рєћrow 8 (col, col, col); row 8Рєћrow 9 (box, box, box)
      Рєњ same permutation parity.
      Each pair shares a column Рєњ distinct permutations.
      4 same-parity permutations from 3 available Рєњ pigeonhole contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

        4.  KРѓё on {r3c9, r5c8, r6c9, r8c9}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c2) РЅа color(r9c5). Add edge.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r9c2] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c8, r6c9, r6c2, r9c5, r9c8} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_set] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9, r6c2} (spine r6c8Рђћr6c9).
      Рєњ color(r5c8) = color(r6c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r5c8=r6c2], r9c2, r9c8, r9c5} (spine r9c2Рђћr9c8).
      Рєњ color([r5c8=r6c2]) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c6, r5c6, r7c6, [r5c8=r6c2=r9c5]} (spine r5c6Рђћr7c6).
      Рєњ color(r3c6) = color([r5c8=r6c2=r9c5]). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c9, [r3c6=r5c8=r6c2=r9c5], r6c9, r6c8} (spine [r3c6=r5c8=r6c2=r9c5]Рђћr6c9).
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r6c8). Identify.
  5.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c3, [r3c9=r6c8], r8c9, r8c3}:
      Diagonals: {r3c3, r8c9} and {[r3c9=r6c8], r8c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c3) = color(r8c9) = X
        row 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r5c8=r6c2=r9c5], [r3c9=r6c8]}: r3c3=X Рєњ [r3c6=r5c8=r6c2=r9c5], [r3c9=r6c8]РЅаX
        row 6 {[r3c6=r5c8=r6c2=r9c5], [r3c9=r6c8], r6c9}: [r3c6=r5c8=r6c2=r9c5], [r3c9=r6c8]РЅаX Рєњ r6c9=X
        col 8 {[r3c6=r5c8=r6c2=r9c5], [r3c9=r6c8], r9c8}: [r3c6=r5c8=r6c2=r9c5], [r3c9=r6c8]РЅаX Рєњ r9c8=X
        col 9 {[r3c9=r6c8], r6c9, r8c9}: r6c9=X and r8c9=X clash
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color([r3c9=r6c8]) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r5c8=r6c2=r9c5], [r3c9=r6c8]}: [r3c9=r6c8]=X Рєњ r3c3, [r3c6=r5c8=r6c2=r9c5]РЅаX
        row 6 {[r3c6=r5c8=r6c2=r9c5], [r3c9=r6c8], r6c9}: [r3c9=r6c8]=X Рєњ r6c9РЅаX
        col 8 {[r3c6=r5c8=r6c2=r9c5], [r3c9=r6c8], r9c8}: [r3c9=r6c8]=X Рєњ r9c8РЅаX
        col 9 {[r3c9=r6c8], r6c9, r8c9}: [r3c9=r6c8]=X Рєњ r8c9РЅаX
        row 9 {[r3c6=r5c8=r6c2=r9c5], r9c2, r9c8}: [r3c6=r5c8=r6c2=r9c5], r9c8РЅаX Рєњ r9c2=X
        r8c3=X and r9c2=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond+set+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c9, r7c9, r9c9, r8c8} (spine r7c9Рђћr9c9).
      Рєњ color(r3c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 3, row 5, row 9}РђЅРѕњРђЅ{col 3, col 6, col 8}.
      Remainder: {[r3c9=r8c8], r5c5, r9c9} = {[r3c9=r8c8], r6c6, r6c8}.
      Рєњ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Diamond {r3c3, r3c6, [r3c9=r8c8], r6c6} (spine r3c6Рђћ[r3c9=r8c8]).
      Рєњ color(r3c3) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  4.  Parity transport:
      row 3 {[r3c3=r6c6], r3c6, [r3c9=r8c8]}
      row 5 {r5c3, r5c5, r5c8}
      row 6 {r6c2, [r3c3=r6c6], r6c8}
      row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c9}
      Parallel links: row 3Рєћrow 6 (box, col, box); row 3Рєћrow 9 (col, col, col); row 5Рєћrow 6 (box, box, col)
      Рєњ same permutation parity.
      Each pair shares a column Рєњ distinct permutations.
      4 same-parity permutations from 3 available Рєњ pigeonhole contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

      Рєњ color(r8c8) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r3c3Рђћr3c6, r6c3Рђћr6c6, r9c3Рђћ[r8c8=r9c6]}:
      Satellites r3c9 (rung 1), r6c7 (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Circular ladder {r3c3Рђћr6c3, r3c6Рђћr6c6, r3c9Рђћr6c7}:
      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), [r8c8=r9c6] (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub r4c9 forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c9, r6c7, r5c8, [r8c8=r9c6], r7c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

              box 7 {[r2c9=r8c3=r9c8], r7c3, r9c2}: [r2c9=r8c3=r9c8], r9c2РЅаX Рєњ r7c3=X
              row 8 {[r2c9=r8c3=r9c8], r8c5, r8c9}: [r2c9=r8c3=r9c8], r8c9РЅаX Рєњ r8c5=X

              r8c5=X and r9c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r8c3) РЅа color(r9c8). Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: r6c5 guards oddagon {r6c5, r6c3, r7c3, r9c2, r9c8, r8c9, r8c5} (source r8c3).
            Рєњ color(r6c5) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c5=r8c3] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r6c3, r6c8, r9c8, r9c2, r7c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

            Bridges: r9c9Рђћr9c4 (edges [r2c9=r5c8]Рђћr6c9 Рєћ r7c6Рђћr8c5), r6c3Рђћr8c3 (edges r6c9Рђћr6c6 Рєћ r8c5Рђћr8c8), r3c6Рђћr3c8 (edges r6c6Рђћr7c6 Рєћ r8c8Рђћ[r2c9=r5c8]).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of CРѓє requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r2c9) РЅа color(r5c8). Add edge.
        3.  Diamond {r3c8, r2c9, r5c8, r6c9} (spine r2c9Рђћr5c8).
            Рєњ color(r3c8) = color(r6c9). Identify.
        4.  Circular ladder {r8c3Рђћr9c2, r8c5Рђћr9c4, r8c8Рђћr9c9}:
            Satellites r7c6 (rung 2), [r3c8=r6c9] (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        5.  KРѓё on {r3c6, [r3c8=r6c9], r6c6, r7c6}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

      Рєњ color(r7c3) = color([r6c9=r8c6]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c9=r7c3=r8c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r2c9=r3c6], r5c6, r6c5, r9c5, r9c2, r8c2, r8c9} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r2c9, r3c7, r3c8, r3c6} (spine r3c7Рђћr3c8).
      Рєњ color(r2c9) = color(r3c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c6, [r2c9=r3c6], r8c6, r8c9} (spine [r2c9=r3c6]Рђћr8c6).
      Рєњ color(r5c6) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r2c9=r3c6], [r5c6=r8c9], r8c6, r8c2} (spine [r5c6=r8c9]Рђћr8c6).
      Рєњ color([r2c9=r3c6]) = color(r8c2). Identify.
  4.  Guardian: r7c3 guards oddagon {r7c3, [r2c9=r3c6=r8c2], [r5c6=r8c9], r6c5, r6c3} (source r6c9).
      Рєњ color(r7c3) = color(r6c9). Identify.
  5.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c5, [r6c9=r7c3], r9c2, r9c5}:
      Diagonals: {r6c5, r9c2} and {[r6c9=r7c3], r9c5} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r6c5) = color(r9c2) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c9=r7c3]}: r6c5=X Рєњ r6c3, [r6c9=r7c3]РЅаX
        box 7 {[r2c9=r3c6=r8c2], [r6c9=r7c3], r9c2}: r9c2=X Рєњ [r2c9=r3c6=r8c2]РЅаX
        col 9 {[r2c9=r3c6=r8c2], [r5c6=r8c9], [r6c9=r7c3]}: [r2c9=r3c6=r8c2], [r6c9=r7c3]РЅаX Рєњ [r5c6=r8c9]=X
        [r5c6=r8c9]=X and r6c5=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color([r6c9=r7c3]) = color(r9c5) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c9=r7c3]}: [r6c9=r7c3]=X Рєњ r6c3, r6c5РЅаX
        col 9 {[r2c9=r3c6=r8c2], [r5c6=r8c9], [r6c9=r7c3]}: [r6c9=r7c3]=X Рєњ [r2c9=r3c6=r8c2], [r5c6=r8c9]РЅаX
        box 7 {[r2c9=r3c6=r8c2], [r6c9=r7c3], r9c2}: [r6c9=r7c3]=X Рєњ r9c2РЅаX
        row 8 {[r2c9=r3c6=r8c2], [r5c6=r8c9], r8c6}: [r2c9=r3c6=r8c2], [r5c6=r8c9]РЅаX Рєњ r8c6=X
        r8c6=X and r9c5=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

  3.  Diamond {[r3c6=r6c5], [r3c3=r9c2], r9c5, r9c9} (spine [r3c3=r9c2]Рђћr9c5).
      Рєњ color([r3c6=r6c5]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c6=r6c5=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r2c9, r3c8, [r3c3=r9c2], r6c2, r6c6, r5c6, r5c9} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c6, r5c6, r6c6, r6c5} (spine r5c6Рђћr6c6).

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c6, r5c6, r6c6, r6c5} (spine r5c6Рђћr6c6).
      Рєњ color(r3c6) = color(r6c5). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3Рђћr8c3).
      Рєњ color(r3c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  3.  Guardian: r2c9 guards oddagon {r2c9, r3c8, [r3c6=r6c5], r9c5, r9c9} (source [r3c3=r9c2]).
      Рєњ color(r2c9) = color([r3c3=r9c2]). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r2c9=r3c3=r9c2], [r3c6=r6c5], r5c6, r5c9}:
      Diagonals: {[r2c9=r3c3=r9c2], r5c6} and {[r3c6=r6c5], r5c9} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r2c9=r3c3=r9c2]) = color(r5c6) = X
        row 3 {[r2c9=r3c3=r9c2], [r3c6=r6c5], r3c8}: [r2c9=r3c3=r9c2]=X Рєњ [r3c6=r6c5], r3c8РЅаX
        row 9 {[r2c9=r3c3=r9c2], r9c5, r9c9}: [r2c9=r3c3=r9c2]=X Рєњ r9c5, r9c9РЅаX
        col 3 {[r2c9=r3c3=r9c2], r7c3, r8c3}: [r2c9=r3c3=r9c2]=X Рєњ r7c3, r8c3РЅаX
        col 6 {[r3c6=r6c5], r5c6, r6c6}: r5c6=X Рєњ r6c6РЅаX
        col 9 {[r2c9=r3c3=r9c2], r5c9, r9c9}: [r2c9=r3c3=r9c2]=X Рєњ r5c9РЅаX
        row 6 {[r3c6=r6c5], r6c2, r6c6}: [r3c6=r6c5], r6c6РЅаX Рєњ r6c2=X
        [r2c9=r3c3=r9c2]=X and r6c2=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color([r3c6=r6c5]) = color(r5c9) = X
        row 3 {[r2c9=r3c3=r9c2], [r3c6=r6c5], r3c8}: [r3c6=r6c5]=X Рєњ [r2c9=r3c3=r9c2], r3c8РЅаX
        row 6 {[r3c6=r6c5], r6c2, r6c6}: [r3c6=r6c5]=X Рєњ r6c2, r6c6РЅаX
        col 6 {[r3c6=r6c5], r5c6, r6c6}: [r3c6=r6c5]=X Рєњ r5c6РЅаX
        col 9 {[r2c9=r3c3=r9c2], r5c9, r9c9}: r5c9=X Рєњ r9c9РЅаX
        row 9 {[r2c9=r3c3=r9c2], r9c5, r9c9}: [r2c9=r3c3=r9c2], r9c9РЅаX Рєњ r9c5=X
        [r3c6=r6c5]=X and r9c5=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_diamond] (oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c3, r9c2:
      Case A: color(r3c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: [r3c3=r9c2] guards oddagon {[r3c3=r9c2], r3c6, r5c6, r6c5, r6c2} (source r6c6).

            Рєњ color(r2c9) = color([r3c3=r6c6=r9c2]). Identify.

            Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, r5c9, r9c9, r9c5, r6c5} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c3) РЅа color(r9c2). Add edge.

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. РќА
